self.assertExpectedInline(\n1364:             actual,\n1365:             \"\"\"\\\n1366: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n1367:     def forward(self):\n1368:         _saved_tensors_hooks_disable = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_disable('This is not supported');  _saved_tensors_hooks_disable = None\n1369: \n1370:         x: \"f32[1]\" = torch.ones(1)\n1371: \n1372:         y: \"f32[1]\" = torch.zeros(1)\n1373: \n1374:         _saved_tensors_hooks_disable_1 = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_disable('This is not supported inner');  _saved_tensors_hooks_disable_1 = None\n1375: \n1376:         add: \"f32[1]\" = x + y;  y = None\n1377: \n1378:         _saved_tensors_hooks_disable_2 = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_disable('This is not supported');  _saved_tensors_hooks_disable_2 = None\n1379: \n1380:         add_1: \"f32[1]\" = add + x;  add = x = None\n1381: \n1382:         _saved_tensors_hooks_disable_3 = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_disable('Previously disabled message');  _saved_tensors_hooks_disable_3 = None\n1383:         return (add_1,)\n1384: \"\"\",  # NOQA: B950\n1385:         )\n1386: \n1387:     def test_disable_saved_tensors_hooks_graph_break(self):\n1388:         def fn(x):\n1389:             with torch.autograd.graph.disable_saved_tensors_hooks(\n1390:                 \"This is not supported\"\n1391:             ):\n1392:                 y = x + 1\n1393:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1394:                 return y * 2\n1395: \n1396:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n1397:         torch.compile(fn, backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(torch.randn(()))\n1398: \n1399:         def check_graph(actual, expected):  # noqa: F841\n1400:             self.assertExpectedInline(actual, expected)\n1401: \n1402:         graph = eager.graphs[0]\n1403:         actual = normalize_gm(graph.print_readable(False))\n1404:         self.assertExpectedInline(\n1405:             actual,\n1406:             \"\"\"\\\n1407: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n1408:     def forward(self, L_x_: \"f32[]\"):\n1409:         l_x_ = L_x_\n1410: \n1411:         _saved_tensors_hooks_disable = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_disable('This is not supported');  _saved_tensors_hooks_disable = None\n1412: \n1413:         y: \"f32[]\" = l_x_ + 1;  l_x_ = None\n1414: \n1415:         _saved_tensors_hooks_enable = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_enable();  _saved_tensors_hooks_enable = None\n1416:         return (y,)\n1417: \"\"\",  # NOQA: B950\n1418:         )\n1419: \n1420:         graph = eager.graphs[1]\n1421:         actual = normalize_gm(graph.print_readable(False))\n1422:         self.assertExpectedInline(\n1423:             actual,\n1424:             \"\"\"\\\n1425: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n1426:     def forward(self, L_y_: \"f32[]\"):\n1427:         l_y_ = L_y_\n1428: \n1429:         _saved_tensors_hooks_disable = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_disable('This is not supported');  _saved_tensors_hooks_disable = None\n1430: \n1431:         mul: \"f32[]\" = l_y_ * 2;  l_y_ = None\n1432: \n1433:         _saved_tensors_hooks_enable = torch._C._autograd._saved_tensors_hooks_enable();  _saved_tensors_hooks_enable = None\n1434:         return (mul,)\n1435: \"\"\",  # NOQA: B950\n1436:         )\n1437: \n1438:     def test_context_wrapping_grad_mode_decorator(self):\n1439:         ctx_wrappers = [(torch.enable_grad, True), (torch.no_grad, False)]\n1440:         for call in [True, False]:\n1441:             for i in range(2):\n1442:                 torch._dynamo.reset()\n1443: \n1444:                 ctx_wrapper, _ = ctx_wrappers[i]\n1445:                 ctx_wrapper_inverse, mode_inverse = ctx_wrappers[(i + 1) % 2]\n1446: \n1447:                 def fn(x):\n1448:                     def inner_func(x):\n1449:                         return x.sin()\n1450: \n1451:                     with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n1452:                         if call:\n1453:                             inner_func = ctx_wrapper()(inner_func)\n1454:                         else:\n1455:                             inner_func = ctx_wrapper(inner_func)\n1456: \n1457:                         # Calling no_grad or enabled_grad should not mutate global state\n1458:                         assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n1459: \n1460:                     with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n1461:                         return inner_func(x)\n1462: \n1463:                 x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n1464:                 opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1465:                 self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1466:                 self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1467: \n1468:     def test_context_wrapping_grad_mode_nested_function_decorator(self):\n1469:         ctx_wrappers = [(torch.enable_grad, True), (torch.no_grad, False)]\n1470: \n1471:         for call in [True, False]:\n1472:             for i in range(2):\n1473:                 torch._dynamo.reset()\n1474: \n1475:                 ctx_wrapper, _ = ctx_wrappers[i]\n1476:                 ctx_wrapper_inverse, mode_inverse = ctx_wrappers[(i + 1) % 2]\n1477: \n1478:                 def fn(x):\n1479:                     with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n1480:                         if call:\n1481: \n1482:                             @ctx_wrapper()\n1483:                             def inner_func(x):\n1484:                                 return x.sin()\n1485: \n1486:                         else:\n1487: \n1488:                             @ctx_wrapper\n1489:                             def inner_func(x):\n1490:                                 return x.sin()\n1491: \n1492:                         # Calling no_grad or enabled_grad should not mutate global state\n1493:                         assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n1494: \n1495:                     with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n1496:                         return inner_func(x)\n1497: \n1498:                 x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n1499:                 opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1500:                 self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1501:                 self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1502: \n1503:     def test_context_wrapping_set_grad_enabled_nested_function(self):\n1504:         modes = [True, False]\n1505:         for decorator in [True, False]:\n1506:             for i in range(2):\n1507:                 torch._dynamo.reset()\n1508: \n1509:                 mode = modes[i]\n1510:                 mode_inverse = modes[(i + 1) % 2]\n1511: \n1512:                 def fn(x):\n1513:                     with torch.set_grad_enabled(mode_inverse):\n1514:                         if decorator:\n1515: \n1516:                             @torch.set_grad_enabled(mode)\n1517:                             def inner_func(x):\n1518:                                 return x.sin()\n1519: \n1520:                         else:\n1521: \n1522:                             def inner_func(x):\n1523:                                 return x.sin()\n1524: \n1525:                             inner_func = torch.set_grad_enabled(mode)(inner_func)\n1526: \n1527:                         # Consuming set_grad_enabled by calling it on a function\n1528:                         # should not mutate global state\n1529:                         assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n1530: \n1531:                     with torch.set_grad_enabled(mode_inverse):\n1532:                         return inner_func(x)\n1533: \n1534:             x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n1535:             opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1536:             self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1537:             self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1538: \n1539:     def test_inactive_context_graph_break_local(self):\n1540:         def fn(x):\n1541:             x = x + 1\n1542:             ctx = torch.set_grad_enabled(True)\n1543:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1544:             with ctx:\n1545:                 x = x + 1\n1546:             return x\n1547: \n1548:         x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=False)\n1549:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1550:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n1551:         self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1552:         self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1553:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1554: \n1555:     def test_inactive_context_graph_break_local_nullctx(self):\n1556:         import contextlib\n1557: \n1558:         # test with context manager that results in None target_values\n1559:         def fn(x):\n1560:             x = x + 1\n1561:             ctx = contextlib.nullcontext()\n1562:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1563:             with ctx:\n1564:                 x = x + 1\n1565:             return x\n1566: \n1567:         x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=False)\n1568:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1569:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n1570:         self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1571:         self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1572:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1573: \n1574:     def test_inactive_context_graph_break_local_nullctx2(self):\n1575:         import contextlib\n1576: \n1577:         # test with nullcontext where graph break happens\n1578:         # in an inlined function that returns something\n1579:         def gn():\n1580:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1581:             return [0, 1, 2]\n1582: \n1583:         def fn(x):\n1584:             x = x + 1\n1585:             ctx = contextlib.nullcontext()\n1586:             lst = gn()\n1587:             with ctx:\n1588:                 x = x + lst[1]\n1589:             return x\n1590: \n1591:         x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=False)\n1592:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1593:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n1594:         self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1595:         self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1596:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1597: \n1598:     def test_inactive_context_graph_break_stack(self):\n1599:         def gn(ctx):\n1600:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1601:             return ctx\n1602: \n1603:         def fn(x):\n1604:             x = x + 1\n1605:             ctx = gn(torch.set_grad_enabled(True))\n1606:             # we expect a graph break on next line as well\n1607:             with ctx:\n1608:                 x = x + 1\n1609:             return x\n1610: \n1611:         x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=False)\n1612:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1613:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n1614:         self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1615:         self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1616: \n1617:     def test_inactive_context_graph_break_stack2(self):\n1618:         def gn(x, ctx, y, z, dummy):\n1619:             with ctx:\n1620:                 return x * y * z\n1621: \n1622:         def fn(x):\n1623:             x = x + 1\n1624:             x = gn(x, torch.set_grad_enabled(True), 2, 3, torch._dynamo.graph_break())\n1625:             return x\n1626: \n1627:         x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=False)\n1628:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1629:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n1630:         self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n1631:         self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n1632:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n1633: \n1634:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager1(self):\n1635:         modified_backend_state = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1636: \n1637:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1638:         def check_backend_state_is_modified():\n1639:             self.assertEqual(\n1640:                 torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends(), modified_backend_state\n1641:             )\n1642: \n1643:         def f(x):\n1644:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(\n1645:                 # pyre-fixme[16]: Module `torch.nn.attention` has no attribute `SDPBackend`.\n1646:                 [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1647:             ):\n1648:                 output = torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x).to(\n1649:                     torch.float32\n1650:                 )\n1651:                 check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1652: \n1653:             return output\n1654: \n1655:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1656:         opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2, 2, 2).to(dtype=torch.float16))\n1657: \n1658:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager2(self):\n1659:         original_backend_state = set(torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends())\n1660:         modified_backend_state = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1661: \n1662:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1663:         def check_backend_state_is_original():\n1664:             self.assertEqual(\n1665:                 set(torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends()),\n1666:                 original_backend_state,\n1667:             )\n1668: \n1669:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1670:         def check_backend_state_is_modified():\n1671:             self.assertEqual(\n1672:                 torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends(), modified_backend_state\n1673:             )\n1674: \n1675:         def g(x):\n1676:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1677:             output = torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x).to(\n1678:                 torch.float32\n1679:             )\n1680:             check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1681:             return output\n1682: \n1683:         def f(x):\n1684:             check_backend_state_is_original()\n1685:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(\n1686:                 # pyre-fixme[16]: Module `torch.nn.attention` has no attribute `SDPBackend`.\n1687:                 [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1688:             ):\n1689:                 output1 = torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x).to(\n1690:                     torch.float32\n1691:                 )\n1692:                 check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1693: \n1694:                 # graph break\n1695:                 output2 = g(x)\n1696: \n1697:                 output3 = torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x).to(\n1698:                     torch.float32\n1699:                 )\n1700:                 check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1701: \n1702:             check_backend_state_is_original()\n1703: \n1704:             return output1 + output2 + output3\n1705: \n1706:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n1707:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=cnts)\n1708:         opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2, 2, 2).to(dtype=torch.float16))\n1709:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 3)\n1710: \n1711:     # test sdpa_kernel graph break with 2 arguments\n1712:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager3(self):\n1713:         modified_backend_state = {\n1714:             torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH,\n1715:             torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.FLASH_ATTENTION,\n1716:         }\n1717: \n1718:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1719:         def check_backend_state_is_modified():\n1720:             self.assertEqual(\n1721:                 set(torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends()),\n1722:                 modified_backend_state,\n1723:             )\n1724: \n1725:         def f(x):\n1726:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(\n1727:                 # pyre-fixme[16]: Module `torch.nn.attention` has no attribute `SDPBackend`.\n1728:                 [\n1729:                     torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH,\n1730:                     torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.FLASH_ATTENTION,\n1731:                 ]\n1732:             ):\n1733:                 # FLASH_ATTENTION may not be supported, but we're not actually\n1734:                 # doing any sdpa\n1735:                 x = x + 1\n1736:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n1737:                 check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1738:                 x = x + 1\n1739: \n1740:             return x\n1741: \n1742:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\")\n1743:         opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2))\n1744: \n1745:     # Regression test to make sure dynamo won't crash on these kwargs.\n1746:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager_kwargs(self):\n1747:         backends = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1748: \n1749:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1750:         def check_backend_state_is_modified():\n1751:             self.assertEqual(\n1752:                 set(torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends()),\n1753:                 set(backends),\n1754:             )\n1755: \n1756:         def f(x):\n1757:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(backends=backends, set_priority=True):\n1758:                 x = x + 1\n1759:                 check_backend_state_is_modified()\n1760:                 x = x + 1\n1761: \n1762:             return x\n1763: \n1764:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\")\n1765:         opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2))\n1766: \n1767:     # Regression test to make sure dynamo won't graph break on calling functions\n1768:     # decorated with special context manager.\n1769:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager_as_decorator(self):\n1770:         SDPA_BACKEND_PRIORITY = [\n1771:             torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH,\n1772:             torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.EFFICIENT_ATTENTION,\n1773:             torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.FLASH_ATTENTION,\n1774:         ]\n1775: \n1776:         @torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(\n1777:             backends=SDPA_BACKEND_PRIORITY, set_priority=True\n1778:         )\n1779:         def scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, *args, **kwargs):\n1780:             return torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(\n1781:                 q, k, v, *args, **kwargs\n1782:             )\n1783: \n1784:         def f(x):\n1785:             return scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x)\n1786: \n1787:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1788:         x = torch.rand(16, 16, 64, 256, dtype=torch.float16)\n1789:         ref = f(x)\n1790:         res = opt_f(x)\n1791: \n1792:         self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n1793: \n1794:     # Regression test to make sure the value of set_priority is used correctly.\n1795:     def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager_set_priority(self):\n1796:         backends = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n1797:         default_priority = torch._C._get_sdp_priority_order()\n1798: \n1799:         @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n1800:         def check_backend_priority(changed: bool):\n1801:             self.assertEqual(\n1802:                 changed,\n1803:                 torch._C._get_sdp_priority_order() != default_priority,\n1804:             )\n1805: \n1806:         def f(x):\n1807:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(backends=backends, set_priority=True):\n1808:                 x = x + 1\n1809:                 check_backend_priority(changed=True)\n1810:                 x = x + 1\n1811: \n1812:             with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(backends=backends, set_priority=False):\n1813:                 x = x + 1\n1814:                 check_backend_priority(changed=False)\n1815:                 x = x + 1\n1816: \n1817:             return x\n1818: \n1819:         opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\")\n1820:         opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2))\n1821: \n1822:     def test_torch_profiler_use_after_with_block(self):\n1823:         counters.clear()\n1824: \n1825:         def fn(x):\n1826:             with torch.profiler.profile() as p:\n1827:                 pass\n1828:             p.profiler.kineto_results.experimental_event_tree()\n1829:             return x + 1\n1830: \n1831:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n1832:         x = torch.ones(1)\n1833:         ref = fn(x)\n1834:         res = opt_fn(x)\n1835:         self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n1836:         self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 1)\n1837: \n1838: \n1839: class ContextlibContextManagerTests(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\n1840:     def setUp(self):\n1841:         self._prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib\n1842:         self._u_prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest\n1843:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = True\n1844:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = True\n1845: \n1846:     def tearDown(self):\n1847:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = self._prev\n1848:         torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = self._u_prev\n1849: \n1850:     def test_ctx_basic0(self):\n1851:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n1852:         def set_default_dtype(dtype):\n1853:             old_dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()\n1854:             try:\n1855:                 torch.set_default_dtype(dtype)\n1856:                 yield\n1857:             finally:\n1858:                 torch.set_default_dtype(old_dtype)\n1859: \n1860:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n1861: \n1862:         @torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=True)\n1863:         def fn():\n1864:             with set_default_dtype(torch.float64):\n1865:                 x = torch.tensor([3.0, 3.0 + 5.0j])\n1866:             return x\n1867: \n1868:         y = fn()\n1869:         self.assertEqual(y.dtype, torch.complex128)\n1870:         graph = eager.graphs[0]\n1871:         actual = normalize_gm(graph.print_readable(False))\n1872: \n1873:         self.assertExpectedInline(\n1874:             actual,\n1875:             \"\"\"\\\n1876: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n1877:     def forward(self):\n1878:         set_default_dtype = torch.set_default_dtype(torch.float64);  set_default_dtype = None\n1879: \n1880:         x: \"c128[2]\" = torch.tensor([3.0, (3+5j)])\n1881: \n1882:         set_default_dtype_1 = torch.set_default_dtype(torch.float32);  set_default_dtype_1 = None\n1883:         return (x,)\n1884: \"\"\",\n1885:         )\n1886: \n1887:     def test_ctx_basic1(self):\n1888:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n1889:         def compute_sin(x):\n1890:             try:\n1891:                 yield x.sin()\n1892:             finally:\n1893:                 pass\n1894: \n1895:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1896:         def fn(x):\n1897:             with compute_sin(x) as y:\n1898:                 return y.cos()\n1899: \n1900:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n1901:         y = fn(x)\n1902:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n1903: \n1904:     def test_change_parent_nonlocal_0(self):\n1905:         # test if a nonlocal actually gets propagated\n1906:         z = 0\n1907:         k = 0\n1908: \n1909:         def create_ctx():\n1910:             @contextmanager\n1911:             def ctx(x):\n1912:                 nonlocal z\n1913:                 nonlocal k\n1914:                 try:\n1915:                     k = 100\n1916:                     yield x.sin()\n1917:                 finally:\n1918:                     pass\n1919: \n1920:             return ctx\n1921: \n1922:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n1923:             nonlocal z\n1924:             with ctx(x) as y:\n1925:                 z = k\n1926:                 return y.cos()\n1927: \n1928:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1929:         def fn(x):\n1930:             ctx = create_ctx()\n1931:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n1932: \n1933:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n1934:         y = fn(x)\n1935:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n1936:         self.assertEqual(z, 100)\n1937:         self.assertEqual(k, 100)\n1938: \n1939:     def test_change_parent_nonlocal_1(self):\n1940:         # test if finally is executed and it is reading the correct variable\n1941:         z = 1\n1942:         k = 2\n1943: \n1944:         def create_ctx():\n1945:             @contextmanager\n1946:             def ctx(x):\n1947:                 nonlocal z\n1948:                 nonlocal k\n1949:                 try:\n1950:                     yield x.sin()\n1951:                 finally:\n1952:                     k = z\n1953: \n1954:             return ctx\n1955: \n1956:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n1957:             nonlocal z\n1958:             z = 100\n1959:             with ctx(x) as y:\n1960:                 return y.cos()\n1961: \n1962:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1963:         def fn(x):\n1964:             ctx = create_ctx()\n1965:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n1966: \n1967:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n1968:         y = fn(x)\n1969:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n1970:         self.assertEqual(z, 100)\n1971:         self.assertEqual(k, 100)\n1972: \n1973:     def test_globals_change_in_other_file(self):\n1974:         @contextmanager\n1975:         def update_global_ctx():\n1976:             global _variable, _variable1\n1977:             try:\n1978:                 _variable += 1\n1979:                 _variable1 += 1\n1980:                 yield\n1981:             finally:\n1982:                 pass\n1983: \n1984:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n1985:         def fn(x):\n1986:             with update_global_ctx():\n1987:                 pass\n1988: \n1989:             with test_functions.update_global_ctx(x) as a:\n1990:                 # Ensure that the updated global values are read\n1991:                 test_functions.constant3(2, 3)\n1992:                 return x * a * (_variable + _variable1 + test_functions._variable)\n1993: \n1994:         res = fn(torch.ones(10))\n1995:         self.assertEqual(_variable, 1)\n1996:         self.assertEqual(_variable1, 1)\n1997:         # Ensure that the reconstructed bytecode updates the global value in the\n1998:         # other file.\n1999:         self.assertEqual(test_functions._variable, 1)\n2000:         self.assertEqual(res, 3 * torch.ones(10))\n2001: \n2002:     def test_change_parent_global_0(self):\n2003:         # test if a global actually gets propagated\n2004:         global z_glb, k_glb\n2005:         z_glb, k_glb = 0, 0\n2006: \n2007:         def create_ctx():\n2008:             @contextmanager\n2009:             def ctx(x):\n2010:                 global k_glb\n2011:                 try:\n2012:                     k_glb = 100\n2013:                     yield x.sin()\n2014:                 finally:\n2015:                     pass\n2016: \n2017:             return ctx\n2018: \n2019:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n2020:             global z_glb\n2021:             with ctx(x) as y:\n2022:                 z_glb = k_glb\n2023:                 return y.cos()\n2024: \n2025:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n2026:         def fn(x):\n2027:             ctx = create_ctx()\n2028:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n2029: \n2030:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2031:         y = fn(x)\n2032:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n2033:         self.assertEqual(z_glb, 100)\n2034:         self.assertEqual(k_glb, 100)\n2035: \n2036:     def test_change_parent_global_1(self):\n2037:         # test if finally is executed and it is reading the correct variable\n2038:         global z_glb, k_glb\n2039:         z_glb, k_glb = 0, 0\n2040: \n2041:         def create_ctx():\n2042:             @contextmanager\n2043:             def ctx(x):\n2044:                 global z_glb, k_glb\n2045:                 try:\n2046:                     yield x.sin()\n2047:                 finally:\n2048:                     k_glb = z_glb\n2049: \n2050:             return ctx\n2051: \n2052:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n2053:             global z_glb\n2054:             z_glb = 100\n2055:             with ctx(x) as y:\n2056:                 return y.cos()\n2057: \n2058:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n2059:         def fn(x):\n2060:             ctx = create_ctx()\n2061:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n2062: \n2063:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2064:         y = fn(x)\n2065:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n2066:         self.assertEqual(z_glb, 100)\n2067:         self.assertEqual(k_glb, 100)\n2068: \n2069:     def test_change_parent_0(self):\n2070:         def create_ctx():\n2071:             @contextlib.contextmanager\n2072:             def ctx(x):\n2073:                 try:\n2074:                     yield x.sin()\n2075:                 finally:\n2076:                     pass\n2077: \n2078:             return ctx\n2079: \n2080:         def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n2081:             with ctx(x) as y:\n2082:                 return y.cos()\n2083: \n2084:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n2085:         def fn(x):\n2086:             ctx = create_ctx()\n2087:             return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n2088: \n2089:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2090:         y = fn(x)\n2091:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n2092: \n2093:     def test_change_parent_1(self):\n2094:         def create_ctx(x):\n2095:             @contextlib.contextmanager\n2096:             def ctx():\n2097:                 try:\n2098:                     yield x.sin()\n2099:                 finally:\n2100:                     pass\n2101: \n2102:             return ctx\n2103: \n2104:         def run_ctx(ctx):\n2105:             with ctx() as y:\n2106:                 return y.cos()\n2107: \n2108:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n2109:         def fn(x):\n2110:             ctx = create_ctx(x)\n2111:             return run_ctx(ctx)\n2112: \n2113:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2114:         y = fn(x)\n2115:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n2116: \n2117:     def test_graph_break_inside_ctx(self):\n2118:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2119:         def whoo(x):\n2120:             y = x.tan()\n2121:             try:\n2122:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2123:                 yield y\n2124:             finally:\n2125:                 pass\n2126: \n2127:         def f(x):\n2128:             y = x.sin()\n2129:             with whoo(x) as z:\n2130:                 y += z.neg()\n2131:             y += x.cos()\n2132:             return y\n2133: \n2134:         x = torch.randn(2)\n2135:         expected = f(x)\n2136:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2137:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n2138:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2139:         # no graph will be generated as we will skip all frames due to the graph break\n2140:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2141: \n2142:     def test_graph_break_inside_ctx_with_side_effects(self):\n2143:         L = []\n2144: \n2145:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2146:         def whoo(x):\n2147:             y = x.tan()\n2148:             try:\n2149:                 L.append(x.sin())\n2150:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2151:                 yield y\n2152:             finally:\n2153:                 L.append(x.cos())\n2154: \n2155:         def f(x):\n2156:             y = x.sin()\n2157:             with whoo(x) as z:\n2158:                 y += z.neg()\n2159:             y += x.cos()\n2160:             return y\n2161: \n2162:         x = torch.randn(2)\n2163:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2164:         y = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n2165:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin() + x.tan().neg() + x.cos())\n2166:         self.assertEqual(L, [x.sin(), x.cos()])\n2167:         # no graph will be generated as we will skip all frames due to the graph break\n2168:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2169: \n2170:     def test_graph_break_inside_ctx_1(self):\n2171:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2172:         def whoo(x):\n2173:             y = x.tan()\n2174:             try:\n2175:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2176:                 yield y\n2177:             finally:\n2178:                 pass\n2179: \n2180:         def bar(x):\n2181:             with whoo(x) as z:\n2182:                 return z.neg()\n2183: \n2184:         def f(x):\n2185:             return x.sin() + bar(x) + x.cos()\n2186: \n2187:         x = torch.randn(2)\n2188:         expected = f(x)\n2189:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2190:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n2191:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2192:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 2)\n2193:         self.assertExpectedInline(\n2194:             normalize_gm(eager.graphs[0].print_readable(False)),\n2195:             \"\"\"\\\n2196: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n2197:     def forward(self, L_x_: \"f32[2]\"):\n2198:         l_x_ = L_x_\n2199: \n2200:         sin: \"f32[2]\" = l_x_.sin();  l_x_ = None\n2201:         return (sin,)\n2202: \"\"\",\n2203:         )\n2204:         self.assertExpectedInline(\n2205:             normalize_gm(eager.graphs[1].print_readable(False)),\n2206:             \"\"\"\\\n2207: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n2208:     def forward(self, L_stack0_: \"f32[2]\", L_stack1_: \"f32[2]\", L_x_: \"f32[2]\"):\n2209:         l_stack0_ = L_stack0_\n2210:         l_stack1_ = L_stack1_\n2211:         l_x_ = L_x_\n2212: \n2213:         add: \"f32[2]\" = l_stack0_ + l_stack1_;  l_stack0_ = l_stack1_ = None\n2214:         cos: \"f32[2]\" = l_x_.cos();  l_x_ = None\n2215:         add_1: \"f32[2]\" = add + cos;  add = cos = None\n2216:         return (add_1,)\n2217: \"\"\",\n2218:         )\n2219: \n2220:     def test_graph_break_inside_ctx_2(self):\n2221:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2222:         def whoo(x):\n2223:             try:\n2224:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2225:                 yield x.cos()\n2226:             finally:\n2227:                 pass\n2228: \n2229:         def g(x):\n2230:             return x.neg() + x.acos()\n2231: \n2232:         def f(x):\n2233:             y = x.sin()\n2234:             with whoo(x) as z:\n2235:                 y += g(z)\n2236:             y += y.tan()\n2237:             return y\n2238: \n2239:         x = torch.randn(2)\n2240:         expected = f(x)\n2241:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2242:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n2243:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2244:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 1)\n2245: \n2246:     def test_graph_break_before___enter__(self):\n2247:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2248:         def whoo(x):\n2249:             try:\n2250:                 yield x + 1\n2251:             finally:\n2252:                 pass\n2253: \n2254:         def fn(x):\n2255:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2256:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2257:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2258:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2259:             return y\n2260: \n2261:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2262:         with self.assertRaises(InternalTorchDynamoError):\n2263:             torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(x)\n2264: \n2265:     def test_graph_break_in_finally(self):\n2266:         z = []\n2267: \n2268:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2269:         def whoo(x):\n2270:             nonlocal z\n2271:             try:\n2272:                 z.append(x)\n2273:                 yield x.sin()\n2274:             finally:\n2275:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2276:                 z.append(x.cos())\n2277: \n2278:         def fn(x):\n2279:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2280:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2281:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2282:             return y\n2283: \n2284:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2285:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2286:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2287:         self.assertEqual(out, x.sin())\n2288:         self.assertEqual(z, [x, x.cos()])\n2289:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2290: \n2291:     def test_graph_break_inside___enter__(self):\n2292:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2293:         def whoo(x):\n2294:             try:\n2295:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2296:                 yield x + 1\n2297:             finally:\n2298:                 pass\n2299: \n2300:         def fn(x):\n2301:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2302:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2303:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2304:             return y\n2305: \n2306:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2307:         expected = fn(x)\n2308: \n2309:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2310:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2311:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2312:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2313: \n2314:     def test_graph_break_after___enter__(self):\n2315:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2316:         def whoo(x):\n2317:             try:\n2318:                 yield x + 1\n2319:             finally:\n2320:                 pass\n2321: \n2322:         def fn(x):\n2323:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2324:             try:\n2325:                 y = ctx.__enter__()\n2326:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2327:             finally:\n2328:                 ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2329:             return y\n2330: \n2331:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2332:         expected = fn(x)\n2333: \n2334:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2335:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2336:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2337:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2338: \n2339:     def test_graph_break_before_and_after___enter__(self):\n2340:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2341:         def whoo(x):\n2342:             try:\n2343:                 yield x + 1\n2344:             finally:\n2345:                 pass\n2346: \n2347:         def fn(x):\n2348:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2349:             try:\n2350:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2351:                 y = ctx.__enter__()\n2352:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2353:             finally:\n2354:                 ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2355:             return y\n2356: \n2357:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2358:         expected = fn(x)\n2359: \n2360:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2361:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2362:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2363:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2364: \n2365:     def test_graph_break_before___enter___and_disable___exit__(self):\n2366:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2367:         def whoo(x):\n2368:             try:\n2369:                 yield x + 1\n2370:             finally:\n2371:                 pass\n2372: \n2373:         def fn(x):\n2374:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2375:             try:\n2376:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2377:                 y = ctx.__enter__()\n2378:             finally:\n2379: \n2380:                 @torch._dynamo.disable\n2381:                 def g():\n2382:                     ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2383: \n2384:                 g()\n2385:             return y\n2386: \n2387:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2388:         expected = fn(x)\n2389: \n2390:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2391:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2392:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2393:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2394: \n2395:     def test_disable___enter__(self):\n2396:         def h(x):\n2397:             return x.cos()\n2398: \n2399:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2400:         def whoo(x):\n2401:             try:\n2402:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2403:             finally:\n2404:                 pass\n2405: \n2406:         def fn(x):\n2407:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2408: \n2409:             @torch._dynamo.disable\n2410:             def g():\n2411:                 return ctx.__enter__()\n2412: \n2413:             y = g()\n2414:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2415:             return y\n2416: \n2417:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2418:         with self.assertRaises(InternalTorchDynamoError):\n2419:             torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(x)\n2420: \n2421:     def test_disable___exit__(self):\n2422:         def h(x):\n2423:             return x.cos()\n2424: \n2425:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2426:         def whoo(x):\n2427:             try:\n2428:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2429:             finally:\n2430:                 pass\n2431: \n2432:         def fn(x):\n2433:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2434:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2435: \n2436:             @torch._dynamo.disable\n2437:             def g():\n2438:                 ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2439: \n2440:             g()\n2441: \n2442:             return y\n2443: \n2444:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2445:         with self.assertRaises(InternalTorchDynamoError):\n2446:             torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(x)\n2447: \n2448:     def test_contextmanager_as_argument(self):\n2449:         def h(x):\n2450:             return x.cos()\n2451: \n2452:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2453:         def whoo(x):\n2454:             try:\n2455:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2456:             finally:\n2457:                 pass\n2458: \n2459:         def fn(x, ctx):\n2460:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2461:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2462:             return x + y\n2463: \n2464:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2465:         expected = fn(x, whoo(x))\n2466: \n2467:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2468:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x, whoo(x))\n2469:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2470:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 2)\n2471: \n2472:     def test_return_new_contextmanager(self):\n2473:         L = []\n2474: \n2475:         def h(x):\n2476:             return x.cos()\n2477: \n2478:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2479:         def whoo(x):\n2480:             try:\n2481:                 L.append(x.sin())\n2482:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2483:             finally:\n2484:                 L.append(x.cos())\n2485: \n2486:         def fn(x):\n2487:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2488:             return x + 1, ctx\n2489: \n2490:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2491:         with self.assertRaises(InternalTorchDynamoError):\n2492:             torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(x)\n2493: \n2494:     def test_return_advanced_contextmanager(self):\n2495:         L = []\n2496: \n2497:         def h(x):\n2498:             return x.cos()\n2499: \n2500:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2501:         def whoo(x):\n2502:             try:\n2503:                 L.append(x.sin())\n2504:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2505:             finally:\n2506:                 L.append(x.cos())\n2507: \n2508:         def fn(x):\n2509:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2510:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2511:             return x + y, ctx\n2512: \n2513:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2514:         with self.assertRaises(InternalTorchDynamoError):\n2515:             torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(x)\n2516: \n2517:     def test_contextmanager_as_argument_only___enter__(self):\n2518:         L = []\n2519: \n2520:         def h(x):\n2521:             return x.cos()\n2522: \n2523:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2524:         def whoo(x):\n2525:             try:\n2526:                 L.append(x.sin())\n2527:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2528:             finally:\n2529:                 L.append(x.cos())\n2530: \n2531:         def fn(x, ctx):\n2532:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2533:             return x + y\n2534: \n2535:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2536:         ctx = whoo(x)\n2537:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2538:         y = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x, ctx)\n2539:         self.assertEqual(y, x + x.cos() + 1)\n2540:         self.assertEqual(L, [x.sin()])  # we should only have one item in L\n2541: \n2542:         ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2543:         self.assertEqual(L, [x.sin(), x.cos()])  # Two items now\n2544: \n2545:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 2)\n2546: \n2547:     def test_contextmanager_as_argument_only___exit__(self):\n2548:         L = []\n2549: \n2550:         def h(x):\n2551:             return x.cos()\n2552: \n2553:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2554:         def whoo(x):\n2555:             try:\n2556:                 L.append(x.sin())\n2557:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2558:             finally:\n2559:                 L.append(x.cos())\n2560: \n2561:         def fn(x, ctx):\n2562:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2563:             return x.sin()\n2564: \n2565:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2566:         ctx = whoo(x)\n2567:         ctx.__enter__()\n2568:         self.assertEqual(L, [x.sin()])\n2569: \n2570:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2571:         y = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x, ctx)\n2572:         self.assertEqual(y, x.sin())\n2573:         self.assertEqual(L, [x.sin(), x.cos()])\n2574:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 1)\n2575: \n2576:     def test_advanced_contextmanager_as_argument(self):\n2577:         def h(x):\n2578:             return x.cos()\n2579: \n2580:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2581:         def whoo(x):\n2582:             try:\n2583:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2584:             finally:\n2585:                 pass\n2586: \n2587:         def fn(x, ctx):\n2588:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2589:             return x + 1\n2590: \n2591:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2592:         ctx = whoo(x)\n2593:         y = ctx.__enter__()\n2594:         self.assertEqual(y, x.cos() + 1)\n2595:         z = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(fn)(x, ctx)\n2596:         self.assertEqual(z, x + 1)\n2597: \n2598:     def test_advanced_contextmanager_as_argument_error(self):\n2599:         def h(x):\n2600:             return x.cos()\n2601: \n2602:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2603:         def whoo(x):\n2604:             try:\n2605:                 yield h(x) + 1\n2606:             finally:\n2607:                 pass\n2608: \n2609:         def fn(x, ctx):\n2610:             y = ctx.__enter__()\n2611:             ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2612:             return y\n2613: \n2614:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2615:         ctx = whoo(x)\n2616:         y = ctx.__enter__()\n2617:         self.assertEqual(y, x.cos() + 1)\n2618: \n2619:         with self.assertRaisesRegex(AttributeError, \"args\"):\n2620:             torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(fn)(x, ctx)\n2621: \n2622:     def test_disable_ctx_manager(self):\n2623:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2624:         def whoo(x):\n2625:             try:\n2626:                 yield x + 1\n2627:             finally:\n2628:                 pass\n2629: \n2630:         @torch._dynamo.disable\n2631:         def g(x):\n2632:             with whoo(x) as y:\n2633:                 return y\n2634: \n2635:         def fn(x):\n2636:             return g(x)\n2637: \n2638:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2639:         expected = fn(x)\n2640: \n2641:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2642:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2643:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2644:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2645: \n2646:     def test_graph_break_and_disable___enter__(self):\n2647:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2648:         def whoo(x):\n2649:             try:\n2650:                 yield x + 1\n2651:             finally:\n2652:                 pass\n2653: \n2654:         def fn(x):\n2655:             ctx = whoo(x)\n2656:             try:\n2657:                 torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n2658: \n2659:                 @torch._dynamo.disable\n2660:                 def g():\n2661:                     return ctx.__enter__()\n2662: \n2663:                 y = g()\n2664:             finally:\n2665:                 ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n2666:             return y\n2667: \n2668:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2669:         expected = fn(x)\n2670: \n2671:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2672:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2673:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2674:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2675: \n2676:     def test_dynamo_disable_ctx(self):\n2677:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2678:         def whoo(x):\n2679:             try:\n2680:                 yield x + 1\n2681:             finally:\n2682:                 pass\n2683: \n2684:         @torch._dynamo.disable\n2685:         def g(x):\n2686:             with whoo(x) as y:\n2687:                 return y\n2688: \n2689:         def fn(x):\n2690:             return g(x)\n2691: \n2692:         x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n2693:         expected = fn(x)\n2694: \n2695:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2696:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n2697:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2698:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n2699: \n2700:     @torch._dynamo.config.patch(enable_trace_contextlib=False)\n2701:     def test_disable_trace_contextmanager(self):\n2702:         @contextlib.contextmanager\n2703:         def whoo(x):\n2704:             try:\n2705:                 yield x.cos()\n2706:             finally:\n2707:                 pass\n2708: \n2709:         def g(x):\n2710:             return x.neg() + x.acos()\n2711: \n2712:         def f(x):\n2713:             y = x.sin()\n2714:             with whoo(x) as z:\n2715:                 y += g(z)\n2716:             y += y.tan()\n2717:             return y\n2718: \n2719:         x = torch.randn(2)\n2720:         expected = f(x)\n2721:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2722:         out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False, dynamic=False)(f)(x)\n2723:         self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n2724:         self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 2)\n2725: \n2726:     @parametrize(\"name\", (\"suppress\", \"stdout\", \"stderr\"))\n2727:     def test_contextlib_suppress(self, name):\n2728:         counters.clear()\n2729:         eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n2730: \n2731:         def fn(t):\n2732:             y = t.sin()\n2733:             # ensure we graph break on the suppress call below\n2734:             if name == \"suppress\":\n2735:                 ctx = contextlib.suppress(ValueError)\n2736:             elif name == \"stdout\":\n2737:                 ctx = contextlib.redirect_stdout(sys.stderr)\n2738:             else:\n2739:                 ctx = contextlib.redirect_stderr(sys.stdout)\n2740: \n2741:             with ctx:\n2742:                 y += t.cos()\n2743:             return y.tan()\n2744: \n2745:         t = torch.randn(2)\n2746:         expected = fn(t)\n2747:         got = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(t)\n2748:         self.assertEqual(expected, got)\n2749:         self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 1)\n2750:         name = f\"redirect_{name}\" if name in (\"stdout\", \"stderr\") else name\n2751:         self.assertRegex(\n2752:             next(iter(counters[\"graph_break\"])),\n2753:             f\"<class 'contextlib.{name}'> not supported\",\n2754:         )\n2755: \n2756:     def test_contextlib_nullcontext(self):\n2757:         counters.clear()\n2758: \n2759:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n2760:         def fn(t):\n2761:             with contextlib.nullcontext():\n2762:                 return t.sin()\n2763: \n2764:         t = torch.randn(2)\n2765:         y = fn(t)\n2766:         # nullcontext is correctly handled in dynamo\n2767:         self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 0)\n2768:         self.assertEqual(y, t.sin())\n2769: \n2770:     @unittest.skipIf(sys.version_info < (3, 11), \"Python 3.11+\")\n2771:     def test_WITH_EXCEPT_START(self):\n2772:         @contextmanager\n2773:         def ctx():\n2774:             try:\n2775:                 yield\n2776:             finally:\n2777:                 pass\n2778: \n2779:         @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n2780:         def fn(t):\n2781:             try:\n2782:                 with ctx():\n2783:                     raise ValueError\n2784:             except ValueError:\n2785:                 return t.sin()\n2786: \n2787:         t = torch.randn(2)\n2788:         y = fn(t)\n2789:         self.assertEqual(y, t.sin())\n2790: \n2791: \n2792: instantiate_parametrized_tests(CtxManagerTests)\n2793: instantiate_parametrized_tests(ContextlibContextManagerTests)\n2794: \n2795: \n2796: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n2797:     from torch._dynamo.test_case import run_tests\n2798: \n2799:     run_tests()\n2800: ",
