Output
"{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: This module provides functionality for resuming Python execution at specific points in code,\\n3: primarily used by PyTorch Dynamo for control flow handling and optimization. It implements\\n4: bytecode transformation and execution state management to enable:\\n5: \\n6: - Resuming execution at arbitrary points in Python bytecode\\n7: - Managing context managers and their state across execution boundaries\\n8: - Transforming and generating new code objects with preserved execution state\\n9: - Supporting Python 3.11+ exception handling and block management\\n10: - Restoring torch function mode stacks and other execution context\\n11: \\n12: The module is critical for PyTorch Dynamo's ability to optimize code while preserving\\n13: Python semantics and execution state.\\n14: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n15: \\n16: import copy\\n17: import dataclasses\\n18: import sys\\n19: import types\\n20: from collections.abc import Iterable\\n21: from contextlib import AbstractContextManager\\n22: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional\\n23: \\n24: from .bytecode_transformation import (\\n25:     add_push_null,\\n26:     bytecode_from_template,\\n27:     create_call_function,\\n28:     create_instruction,\\n29:     create_jump_absolute,\\n30:     create_load_const,\\n31:     Instruction,\\n32:     overwrite_instruction,\\n33:     transform_code_object,\\n34:     unique_id,\\n35: )\\n36: from .utils import ExactWeakKeyDictionary\\n37: \\n38: \\n39: # taken from code.h in cpython\\n40: CO_OPTIMIZED = 0x0001\\n41: CO_NEWLOCALS = 0x0002\\n42: CO_VARARGS = 0x0004\\n43: CO_VARKEYWORDS = 0x0008\\n44: CO_NESTED = 0x0010\\n45: CO_GENERATOR = 0x0020\\n46: CO_NOFREE = 0x0040\\n47: CO_COROUTINE = 0x0080\\n48: CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE = 0x0100\\n49: CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR = 0x0200\\n50: \\n51: # trace_rules.py import this constant for consistency\\n52: TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX = \\\"torch_dynamo_resume_in\\\"\\n53: IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME = \\\"__is_tracing_resume_prologue\\\"\\n54: \\n55: \\n56: def _initial_push_null(insts: list[Instruction]) -> None:\\n57:     if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n58:         insts.append(create_instruction(\\\"PUSH_NULL\\\"))\\n59:         if sys.version_info < (3, 13):\\n60:             insts.append(create_instruction(\\\"SWAP\\\", arg=2))\\n61: \\n62: \\n63: # Generates bytecode from template and splits the code where LOAD_FAST dummy is present.\\n64: def _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\\n65:     template: Callable[..., Any],\\n66:     stack_index: int,\\n67:     varname_map: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\\n68: ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], list[Instruction]]:\\n69:     template_code = bytecode_from_template(template, varname_map=varname_map)\\n70:     template_code.append(create_instruction(\\\"POP_TOP\\\"))\\n71: \\n72:     # adjust exception table entry depth\\n73:     for inst in template_code:\\n74:         if inst.exn_tab_entry:\\n75:             inst.exn_tab_entry.depth += stack_index\\n76: \\n77:     # search for LOAD_FAST dummy and replace it with 2 NOPs (we can break up the bytecode between them)\\n78:     dummy_idx, dummy_inst = next(\\n79:         (\\n80:             (i, inst)\\n81:             for i, inst in enumerate(template_code)\\n82:             if inst.opname == \\\"LOAD_FAST\\\" and inst.argval == \\\"dummy\\\"\\n83:         ),\\n84:         (None, None),\\n85:     )\\n86:     assert dummy_idx is not None and dummy_inst is not None\\n87: \\n88:     # replace LOAD_FAST dummy with first NOP marking exception area\\n89:     overwrite_instruction(dummy_inst, [create_instruction(\\\"NOP\\\")])\\n90: \\n91:     # POP_TOP follows LOAD_FAST dummy - replace with NOP marking end of exception area\\n92:     assert template_code[dummy_idx + 1].opname == \\\"POP_TOP\\\"\\n93:     overwrite_instruction(template_code[dummy_idx + 1], [create_instruction(\\\"NOP\\\")])\\n94: \\n95:     return template_code[: dummy_idx + 1], template_code[dummy_idx + 1 :]\\n96: \\n97: \\n98: def _try_except_tf_mode_template(dummy: Any, stack_var_name: Any) -> None:\\n99:     # NOTE: Make sure this name matches what is generated by symbolic_convert:import_source\\n100:     # on torch._dynamo.utils.\\n101:     global __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils\\n102:     try:\\n103:         dummy\\n104:     except:  # noqa: E722, B001\\n105:         __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils.set_torch_function_mode_stack(  # type: ignore[name-defined]\\n106:             stack_var_name\\n107:         )\\n108:         raise\\n109: \\n110: \\n111: @dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\\n112: class ReenterWith:\\n113:     stack_index: int\\n114:     target_values: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]] = None\\n115: \\n116:     def try_except_torch_function_mode(\\n117:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\\n118:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\\n119:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n120:         Codegen based off of:\\n121:         try:\\n122:             (rest)\\n123:         except:\\n124:             (restore previous tf mode stack)\\n125:             raise\\n126:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n127:         from .variables.torch_function import get_prev_stack_var_name\\n128: \\n129:         setup_try_except, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\\n130:             _try_except_tf_mode_template,\\n131:             self.stack_index,\\n132:             varname_map={\\\"stack_var_name\\\": get_prev_stack_var_name()},\\n133:         )\\n134:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\\n135: \\n136:         return setup_try_except\\n137: \\n138:     # If we do not want to destroy the stack, we can do the same thing as a\\n139:     # `SETUP_WITH` block, only that we store the context manager in a local_symbol\\n140:     def try_finally(\\n141:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\\n142:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\\n143:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n144:         Codegen based off of:\\n145:         load args\\n146:         enter context\\n147:         try:\\n148:             (rest)\\n149:         finally:\\n150:             exit context\\n151:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n152:         # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\\n153:         load_args = []\\n154:         if self.target_values:\\n155:             load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\\n156:         ctx_name = unique_id(f\\\"___context_manager_{self.stack_index}\\\")\\n157:         if ctx_name not in code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"]:\\n158:             code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] += (ctx_name,)\\n159:         for name in [\\\"__enter__\\\", \\\"__exit__\\\"]:\\n160:             if name not in code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"]:\\n161:                 code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"] += (name,)\\n162: \\n163:         create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\\n164:         _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\\n165:         create_ctx.extend(\\n166:             [\\n167:                 *load_args,\\n168:                 *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\\n169:                 create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=ctx_name),\\n170:             ]\\n171:         )\\n172: \\n173:         def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\\n174:             ctx.__enter__()\\n175:             try:\\n176:                 dummy\\n177:             finally:\\n178:                 ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\\n179: \\n180:         setup_try_finally, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\\n181:             _template, self.stack_index, varname_map={\\\"ctx\\\": ctx_name}\\n182:         )\\n183:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\\n184:         return create_ctx + setup_try_finally\\n185: \\n186:     def __call__(\\n187:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\\n188:     ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], Optional[Instruction]]:\\n189:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n190:         Codegen based off of:\\n191:         with ctx(args):\\n192:             (rest)\\n193:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n194:         # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\\n195:         load_args = []\\n196:         if self.target_values:\\n197:             load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\\n198: \\n199:         create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\\n200:         _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\\n201:         create_ctx.extend(\\n202:             [\\n203:                 *load_args,\\n204:                 *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\\n205:             ]\\n206:         )\\n207: \\n208:         def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\\n209:             with ctx:\\n210:                 dummy\\n211: \\n212:         setup_with, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\\n213:             _template, self.stack_index\\n214:         )\\n215:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\\n216: \\n217:         load_fast_ctx_inst = next(\\n218:             (\\n219:                 inst\\n220:                 for inst in setup_with\\n221:                 if inst.opname == \\\"LOAD_FAST\\\" and inst.argval == \\\"ctx\\\"\\n222:             ),\\n223:             None,\\n224:         )\\n225:         assert load_fast_ctx_inst is not None\\n226:         # ctx already loaded on stack before the template - no need to LOAD_FAST\\n227:         overwrite_instruction(load_fast_ctx_inst, [create_instruction(\\\"NOP\\\")])\\n228: \\n229:         # 3.11+ only\\n230:         push_exc_info_gen = (\\n231:             inst for inst in epilogue if inst.opname == \\\"PUSH_EXC_INFO\\\"\\n232:         )\\n233:         push_exc_info_inst = next(push_exc_info_gen, None)\\n234:         # expect only 1 PUSH_EXC_INFO in epilogue\\n235:         assert next(push_exc_info_gen, None) is None\\n236: \\n237:         return create_ctx + setup_with, push_exc_info_inst\\n238: \\n239: \\n240: @dataclasses.dataclass\\n241: class ResumeFunctionMetadata:\\n242:     code: types.CodeType\\n243:     instructions: list[Instruction] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\\n244:     # Python 3.11+ fields\\n245:     # NOTE: Python 3.11 removed blocks, but for our purposes, a \\\"block\\\" consists\\n246:     # of instructions of all exception table entries that have the same target.\\n247: \\n248:     # map from PUSH_EXC_INFO's in the prefix to original block target offset\\n249:     prefix_block_target_offset_remap: list[int] = dataclasses.field(\\n250:         default_factory=list\\n251:     )\\n252:     # per-offset map from new block target offsets to original block target offsets\\n253:     block_target_offset_remap: dict[int, dict[int, int]] = dataclasses.field(\\n254:         default_factory=dict\\n255:     )\\n256: \\n257: \\n258: def _filter_iter(\\n259:     l1: Iterable[Any],\\n260:     l2: Iterable[Any],\\n261:     cond: Callable[[Any, Any], bool],\\n262: ) -> list[Any]:\\n263:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n264:     Two-pointer conditional filter.\\n265:     e.g. _filter_iter(insts, sorted_offsets, lambda i, o: i.offset == o)\\n266:     returns the instructions with offsets in sorted_offsets\\n267:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n268:     it = iter(l2)\\n269:     res: list[Instruction] = []\\n270:     try:\\n271:         cur = next(it)\\n272:         for val in l1:\\n273:             if cond(val, cur):\\n274:                 res.append(val)\\n275:                 cur = next(it)\\n276:     except StopIteration:\\n277:         pass\\n278:     return res\\n279: \\n280: \\n281: def _load_tuple_and_call(tup: tuple[Any, ...]) -> list[Instruction]:\\n282:     insts: list[Instruction] = []\\n283:     _initial_push_null(insts)\\n284:     insts.extend(create_load_const(val) for val in tup)\\n285:     insts.extend(create_call_function(len(tup), False))\\n286:     return insts\\n287: \\n288: \\n289: class ContinueExecutionCache:\\n290:     cache = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\\n291:     generated_code_metadata = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\\n292: \\n293:     @classmethod\\n294:     def lookup(cls, code: types.CodeType, lineno: int, *key: Any) -> types.CodeType:\\n295:         if code not in cls.cache:\\n296:             cls.cache[code] = {}\\n297:         key = tuple(key)\\n298:         if key not in cls.cache[code]:\\n299:             cls.cache[code][key] = cls.generate(code, lineno, *key)\\n300:         return cls.cache[code][key]\\n301: \\n302:     @classmethod\\n303:     def generate(\\n304:         cls,\\n305:         code: types.CodeType,\\n306:         lineno: int,\\n307:         offset: int,\\n308:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],  # only used in Python 3.11+\\n309:         nstack: int,\\n310:         argnames: tuple[str, ...],\\n311:         argnames_null: tuple[str, ...],\\n312:         setup_fns: tuple[ReenterWith, ...],\\n313:         stack_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\\n314:         argnames_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\\n315:         null_idxes: tuple[int, ...],\\n316:         # mainly used to ensure distinct code objects per stack trace,\\n317:         # which prevents excessive recompilation of inner frames\\n318:         nested_code_objs: tuple[types.CodeType],\\n319:     ) -> types.CodeType:\\n320:         assert offset is not None\\n321:         assert not (\\n322:             code.co_flags\\n323:             & (CO_GENERATOR | CO_COROUTINE | CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE | CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR)\\n324:         )\\n325:         assert code.co_flags & CO_OPTIMIZED\\n326:         if code in ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata:\\n327:             return cls.generate_based_on_original_code_object(\\n328:                 code,\\n329:                 lineno,\\n330:                 offset,\\n331:                 setup_fn_target_offsets,\\n332:                 nstack,\\n333:                 argnames,\\n334:                 argnames_null,\\n335:                 setup_fns,\\n336:                 stack_ctx_vars,\\n337:                 argnames_ctx_vars,\\n338:                 null_idxes,\\n339:                 nested_code_objs,\\n340:             )\\n341: \\n342:         is_py311_plus = sys.version_info >= (3, 11)\\n343:         meta = ResumeFunctionMetadata(code)\\n344: \\n345:         def update(\\n346:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n347:         ) -> None:\\n348:             meta.instructions = copy.deepcopy(instructions)\\n349: \\n350:             args = [\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\", \\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"]\\n351:             args += [f\\\"___stack{i}\\\" for i in range(nstack)]\\n352:             args.extend(v for v in argnames if v not in args)\\n353:             freevars = tuple(code_options[\\\"co_cellvars\\\"] or []) + tuple(\\n354:                 code_options[\\\"co_freevars\\\"] or []\\n355:             )\\n356:             freevars = tuple(sorted(freevars))\\n357:             code_options[\\\"co_name\\\"] = (\\n358:                 f\\\"{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{code_options['co_name']}_at_{lineno}\\\"\\n359:             )\\n360:             if is_py311_plus:\\n361:                 qualified_path = code_options[\\\"co_qualname\\\"].rsplit(\\\".\\\", maxsplit=1)\\n362:                 if len(qualified_path) == 1:\\n363:                     code_options[\\\"co_qualname\\\"] = code_options[\\\"co_name\\\"]\\n364:                 else:\\n365:                     assert len(qualified_path) == 2\\n366:                     module_name, co_name = qualified_path\\n367:                     code_options[\\\"co_qualname\\\"] = (\\n368:                         f\\\"{module_name}.{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{co_name}_at_{lineno}\\\"\\n369:                     )\\n370:             code_options[\\\"co_firstlineno\\\"] = lineno\\n371:             code_options[\\\"co_cellvars\\\"] = ()\\n372:             code_options[\\\"co_freevars\\\"] = freevars\\n373:             code_options[\\\"co_argcount\\\"] = len(args)\\n374:             code_options[\\\"co_posonlyargcount\\\"] = 0\\n375:             code_options[\\\"co_kwonlyargcount\\\"] = 0\\n376:             code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] = tuple(\\n377:                 args\\n378:                 + [v for v in argnames_null if v not in args]\\n379:                 + [v for v in code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] if v not in args]\\n380:                 + [IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME]\\n381:             )\\n382:             code_options[\\\"co_flags\\\"] = code_options[\\\"co_flags\\\"] & ~(\\n383:                 CO_VARARGS | CO_VARKEYWORDS\\n384:             )\\n385:             target = next(i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\\n386: \\n387:             prefix = []\\n388:             if is_py311_plus:\\n389:                 if freevars:\\n390:                     prefix.append(\\n391:                         create_instruction(\\\"COPY_FREE_VARS\\\", arg=len(freevars))\\n392:                     )\\n393:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"RESUME\\\", arg=0))\\n394: \\n395:             # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue to prevent graph breaks.\\n396:             # This doesn't really do anything at runtime, but dynamo will trace this\\n397:             # and will know that we're in a resume function prologue.\\n398:             prefix.extend(\\n399:                 [\\n400:                     create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=True),\\n401:                     create_instruction(\\n402:                         \\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\\n403:                     ),\\n404:                 ]\\n405:             )\\n406: \\n407:             cleanup: list[Instruction] = []\\n408:             hooks = {fn.stack_index: fn for fn in setup_fns}\\n409:             hook_target_offsets = {\\n410:                 fn.stack_index: setup_fn_target_offsets[i]\\n411:                 for i, fn in enumerate(setup_fns)\\n412:             }\\n413:             offset_to_inst = {inst.offset: inst for inst in instructions}\\n414:             # map old hook targets to new targets generated by the hook\\n415:             old_hook_target_remap = {}\\n416:             null_idxes_i = 0\\n417:             stack_ctx_vars_d = dict(stack_ctx_vars)  # type: ignore[var-annotated,arg-type]\\n418:             for i in range(nstack):\\n419:                 while (\\n420:                     null_idxes_i < len(null_idxes)\\n421:                     and null_idxes[null_idxes_i] == i + null_idxes_i\\n422:                 ):\\n423:                     prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"PUSH_NULL\\\"))\\n424:                     null_idxes_i += 1\\n425:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=f\\\"___stack{i}\\\"))\\n426:                 if i in hooks:\\n427:                     hook = hooks.pop(i)\\n428:                     hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\\n429:                     prefix.extend(hook_insts)\\n430:                     if is_py311_plus:\\n431:                         hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\\n432:                         old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\\n433:                         meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\\n434:                         old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\\n435:                 if i in stack_ctx_vars_d:\\n436:                     # NOTE: we assume that current stack var is a context manager CLASS!\\n437:                     # Load args for context variable and construct it\\n438:                     prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(stack_ctx_vars_d[i]))\\n439: \\n440:             if is_py311_plus:\\n441:                 # reverse the mapping since targets of later/nested contexts are inserted\\n442:                 # into the mapping later, but show up earlier in the prefix.\\n443:                 meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = list(\\n444:                     reversed(meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap)\\n445:                 )\\n446: \\n447:             assert not hooks\\n448: \\n449:             # NOTE: we assume that local var is a context manager CLASS!\\n450:             # initialize inactive context vars in argnames\\n451:             for name, vals in argnames_ctx_vars:\\n452:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=name))\\n453:                 prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(vals))\\n454:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=name))\\n455: \\n456:             # 3.12+: store NULL into variables that were NULL\\n457:             if argnames_null:\\n458:                 assert sys.version_info >= (3, 12)\\n459:                 for v in argnames_null:\\n460:                     assert v not in args\\n461:                     prefix.extend(\\n462:                         [\\n463:                             create_instruction(\\\"PUSH_NULL\\\"),\\n464:                             create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=v),\\n465:                         ]\\n466:                     )\\n467: \\n468:             # Call nested resume function\\n469:             if nested_code_objs:\\n470:                 prefix.extend(\\n471:                     [\\n472:                         # set up __nested_resume_fns[-1] call\\n473:                         *add_push_null(\\n474:                             [\\n475:                                 create_instruction(\\n476:                                     \\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"\\n477:                                 ),\\n478:                                 create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n479:                                 create_instruction(\\\"BINARY_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n480:                             ]\\n481:                         ),\\n482:                         # del __nested_resume_fns[-1]\\n483:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"),\\n484:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n485:                         create_instruction(\\\"DELETE_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n486:                         # load [__nested_resume_fns, __nested_frame_values]\\n487:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"),\\n488:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"),\\n489:                         create_instruction(\\\"BUILD_LIST\\\", arg=2),\\n490:                         # load __nested_frame_values[-1]\\n491:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"),\\n492:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n493:                         create_instruction(\\\"BINARY_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n494:                         # create [\\n495:                         #     __nested_resume_fns,\\n496:                         #     __nested_frame_values,\\n497:                         #     *__nested_frame_values[-1],\\n498:                         # ]\\n499:                         create_instruction(\\\"LIST_EXTEND\\\", arg=1),\\n500:                         # del __nested_frame_values[-1]\\n501:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"),\\n502:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n503:                         create_instruction(\\\"DELETE_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n504:                         # delete __nested values\\n505:                         create_instruction(\\\"DELETE_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"),\\n506:                         create_instruction(\\n507:                             \\\"DELETE_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"\\n508:                         ),\\n509:                         # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks\\n510:                         # in the nested resume\\n511:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=False),\\n512:                         create_instruction(\\n513:                             \\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\\n514:                         ),\\n515:                         # finish the call\\n516:                         create_instruction(\\\"CALL_FUNCTION_EX\\\", arg=0),\\n517:                     ]\\n518:                 )\\n519:             else:\\n520:                 # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks after the jump\\n521:                 prefix.extend(\\n522:                     [\\n523:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=False),\\n524:                         create_instruction(\\n525:                             \\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\\n526:                         ),\\n527:                     ]\\n528:                 )\\n529: \\n530:             prefix.append(create_jump_absolute(target))\\n531: \\n532:             # because the line number table monotonically increases from co_firstlineno\\n533:             # remove starts_line for any instructions before the graph break instruction\\n534:             # this will ensure the instructions after the break have the correct line numbers\\n535:             for inst in instructions:\\n536:                 if inst.offset == target.offset:\\n537:                     break\\n538:                 inst.starts_line = None\\n539:                 if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n540:                     inst.positions = None\\n541: \\n542:             if cleanup:\\n543:                 prefix.extend(cleanup)\\n544:                 prefix.extend(cls.unreachable_codes(code_options))\\n545: \\n546:             # remap original instructions' exception table entries\\n547:             if old_hook_target_remap:\\n548:                 assert is_py311_plus\\n549:                 for inst in instructions:\\n550:                     if (\\n551:                         inst.exn_tab_entry\\n552:                         and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\\n553:                     ):\\n554:                         inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n555:                             inst.exn_tab_entry.target\\n556:                         ]\\n557: \\n558:             # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\\n559:             instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\\n560: \\n561:         new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\\n562:         ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\\n563:         return new_code\\n564: \\n565:     @staticmethod\\n566:     def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\\n567:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n568:         return [\\n569:             create_load_const(None),\\n570:             create_instruction(\\\"RAISE_VARARGS\\\", arg=1),\\n571:         ]\\n572: \\n573:     @classmethod\\n574:     def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\\n575:         cls,\\n576:         code: types.CodeType,\\n577:         lineno: int,\\n578:         offset: int,\\n579:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\\n580:         *args: Any,\\n581:     ) -> types.CodeType:\\n582:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n583:         This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\\n584:         to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\\n585:         from the original code object so that if control flow paths\\n586:         converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\\n587:         resume functions).\\n588:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n589: \\n590:         meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\\n591:             code\\n592:         ]\\n593:         new_offset = -1\\n594: \\n595:         def find_new_offset(\\n596:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n597:         ) -> None:\\n598:             nonlocal new_offset\\n599:             (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\\n600:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\\n601:             (new_target,) = (\\n602:                 i2\\n603:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\\n604:                 if i1 is target\\n605:             )\\n606:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\\n607:             assert new_target.offset is not None\\n608:             new_offset = new_target.offset\\n609: \\n610:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\\n611:         assert new_offset >= 0\\n612: \\n613:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n614:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\\n615:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\\n616:             # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\\n617:             # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\\n618:             if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\\n619:                 block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\\n620:                     new_offset\\n621:                 ] = {}\\n622: \\n623:                 def remap_block_offsets(\\n624:                     instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n625:                 ) -> None:\\n626:                     # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\\n627:                     # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\\n628:                     # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\\n629:                     # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\\n630:                     # replaced.\\n631:                     prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\\n632:                     for inst in instructions:\\n633:                         if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\\n634:                             meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n635:                         ):\\n636:                             break\\n637:                         if inst.opname == \\\"PUSH_EXC_INFO\\\":\\n638:                             prefix_blocks.append(inst)\\n639: \\n640:                     # offsets into prefix\\n641:                     for inst, o in zip(\\n642:                         prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n643:                     ):\\n644:                         block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\\n645: \\n646:                     # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\\n647:                     old_start_offset = (\\n648:                         cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\\n649:                     )\\n650:                     # offsets into old bytecode\\n651:                     old_inst_offsets = sorted(\\n652:                         n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\\n653:                     )\\n654:                     targets = _filter_iter(\\n655:                         instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\\n656:                     )\\n657:                     new_targets = _filter_iter(\\n658:                         zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\\n659:                         targets,\\n660:                         lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\\n661:                     )\\n662:                     for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\\n663:                         block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\\n664: \\n665:                 transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\\n666: \\n667:             # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\\n668:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\\n669:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\\n670:                 for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\\n671:             )\\n672:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\\n673:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\\n674:         )\\n675: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py\"}"

torch._dynamo.debug_utils import same_two_models\\n47: from torch._dynamo.testing import (\\n48:     CompileCounter,\\n49:     rand_strided,\\n50:     same,\\n51:     skipIfNotPy312,\\n52: "{\"content\":\"2430:         self.push(result)\\n2431: \\n2432:     def LOAD_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2433:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):\\n2434:             if inst.arg % 2:\\n2435:                 self.LOAD_METHOD(inst)\\n2436:                 return\\n2437:         self._load_attr(inst)\\n2438: \\n2439:     def STORE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2440:         speculation = self.speculate()\\n2441:         if speculation.failed(self):\\n2442:             return self.store_attr_graph_break(inst)\\n2443:         val, obj = self.popn(2)\\n2444: \\n2445:         if isinstance(obj, NNModuleVariable) and not isinstance(val, ConstantVariable):\\n2446:             # We don't allow side effects during export on non-constant values\\n2447:             # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1475\\n2448:             assert not self.export, (\\n2449:                 f\\\"Mutating module attribute {inst.argval} during export.\\\"\\n2450:             )\\n2451: \\n2452:         try:\\n2453:             BuiltinVariable(setattr).call_function(\\n2454:                 self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n2455:                 [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval), val],\\n2456:                 {},\\n2457:             )\\n2458:             return\\n2459:         except Unsupported as e:\\n2460:             if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\\n2461:                 raise\\n2462:             log.debug(\\\"STORE_ATTR triggered compile\\\", exc_info=True)\\n2463:             e.remove_from_stats()\\n2464:             e.add_to_stats(\\\"graph_break\\\")\\n2465:         speculation.fail_and_restart_analysis(self.error_on_graph_break)\\n2466: \\n2467:     def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2468:         log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\\\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\\\")\\n2469:         if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\\n2470:             unimplemented_v2(\\n2471:                 gb_type=\\\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\\\",\\n2472:                 context=\\\"\\\",\\n2473:                 explanation=\\\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \\\"\\n2474:                 \\\"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\\\",\\n2475:                 hints=[],\\n2476:             )\\n2477:         all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\\n2478:             self,\\n2479:             reason=GraphCompileReason(\\\"store_attr\\\", [self.frame_summary()]),\\n2480:             stack_pops=2,\\n2481: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2430-2480\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1820: \\n1821:         def fn3():\\n1822:             fn1()\\n1823:             return fn2()\\n1824: \\n1825:         ref = fn3()\\n1826:         opt_fn3 = torch.compile(fn3, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1827:         res = opt_fn3()\\n1828:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1829: \\n1830:     def test_with_on_graph_break_inst(self):\\n1831:         def reversible(x):\\n1832:             print(\\\"Hello world\\\")  # Cause graph break so inline fails\\n1833:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\\n1834: \\n1835:         def fn(x):\\n1836:             with torch.enable_grad():\\n1837:                 a = torch.sin(x)\\n1838:                 b = reversible(a)\\n1839:                 c = torch.sigmoid(b)\\n1840:                 c.sum().backward()\\n1841:                 return x.grad\\n1842: \\n1843:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1844:         x.grad = None\\n1845:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1846:             ref = fn(x)\\n1847: \\n1848:         x.grad = None\\n1849:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1850:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1851:             res = opt_fn(x)\\n1852:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1853: \\n1854:     def test_with_on_graph_break_nested(self):\\n1855:         def reversible(x):\\n1856:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()  # Cause graph break so inline fails\\n1857:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\\n1858: \\n1859:         def fn(x):\\n1860:             # nested context manager failed previously\\n1861:             with torch.no_grad():\\n1862:                 with torch.enable_grad():\\n1863:                     a = torch.sin(x)\\n1864:                     b = reversible(a)\\n1865:                     c = torch.sigmoid(b)\\n1866:                     c.sum().backward()\\n1867:                     return x.grad\\n1868: \\n1869:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1870:         x.grad = None\\n1871:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1872:             ref = fn(x)\\n1873: \\n1874:         x.grad = None\\n1875:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1876:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1877:             res = opt_fn(x)\\n1878:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1879: \\n1880:     # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1446\\n1881:     def test_grad_mode_carrying_correct_state_after_graph_break(self):\\n1882:         def fn(x):\\n1883:             with torch.no_grad():\\n1884:                 y = x * 3\\n1885:                 print(\\\"Break\\\")\\n1886:                 z = x + 2\\n1887:             return y, z\\n1888: \\n1889:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1890:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1891:         y, z = opt_fn(x)\\n1892:         self.assertFalse(y.requires_grad)\\n1893:         self.assertFalse(z.requires_grad)\\n1894: \\n1895:     def test_abc_setattr(self):\\n1896:         # tests that we correctly bail out of __setattr__ calls\\n1897: \\n1898:         # TODO: does not ensure ABC classes are correctly inferred as ClassVariables\\n1899:         # (doesn't test the fix for 'super()')\\n1900: \\n1901: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL1820-1900\"}"

"{\"content\":\"600:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\\n601:             (new_target,) = (\\n602:                 i2\\n603:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\\n604:                 if i1 is target\\n605:             )\\n606:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\\n607:             assert new_target.offset is not None\\n608:             new_offset = new_target.offset\\n609: \\n610:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\\n611:         assert new_offset >= 0\\n612: \\n613:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n614:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\\n615:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\\n616:             # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\\n617:             # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\\n618:             if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\\n619:                 block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\\n620:                     new_offset\\n621:                 ] = {}\\n622: \\n623:                 def remap_block_offsets(\\n624:                     instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n625:                 ) -> None:\\n626:                     # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\\n627:                     # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\\n628:                     # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\\n629:                     # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\\n630:                     # replaced.\\n631:                     prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\\n632:                     for inst in instructions:\\n633:                         if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\\n634:                             meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n635:                         ):\\n636:                             break\\n637:                         if inst.opname == \\\"PUSH_EXC_INFO\\\":\\n638:                             prefix_blocks.append(inst)\\n639: \\n640:                     # offsets into prefix\\n641:                     for inst, o in zip(\\n642:                         prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n643:                     ):\\n644:                         block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\\n645: \\n646:                     # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\\n647:                     old_start_offset = (\\n648:                         cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\\n649:                     )\\n650:                     # offsets into old bytecode\\n651:                     old_inst_offsets = sorted(\\n652:                         n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\\n653:                     )\\n654:                     targets = _filter_iter(\\n655:                         instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\\n656:                     )\\n657:                     new_targets = _filter_iter(\\n658:                         zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\\n659:                         targets,\\n660:                         lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\\n661:                     )\\n662:                     for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\\n663:                         block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\\n664: \\n665:                 transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\\n666: \\n667:             # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\\n668:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\\n669:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\\n670:                 for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\\n671:             )\\n672:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\\n673:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\\n674:         )\\n675: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py%3FL600-675\"}"

"{\"content\":\"2467:     def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2468:         log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\\\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\\\")\\n2469:         if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\\n2470:             unimplemented_v2(\\n2471:                 gb_type=\\\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\\\",\\n2472:                 context=\\\"\\\",\\n2473:                 explanation=\\\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \\\"\\n2474:                 \\\"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\\\",\\n2475:                 hints=[],\\n2476:             )\\n2477:         all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\\n2478:             self,\\n2479:             reason=GraphCompileReason(\\\"store_attr\\\", [self.frame_summary()]),\\n2480:             stack_pops=2,\\n2481:         )\\n2482:         self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\\n2483:         self.popn(2)\\n2484:         self.output.add_output_instructions(\\n2485:             self.create_call_resume_at(\\n2486:                 self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\\n2487:             )\\n2488:         )\\n2489: \\n2490:     def DELETE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2491:         obj = self.pop()\\n2492:         BuiltinVariable(delattr).call_function(\\n2493:             self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n2494:             [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval)],\\n2495:             {},\\n2496:         )\\n2497: \\n2498:     def create_call_resume_at(\\n2499:         self,\\n2500:         inst: Instruction,\\n2501:         all_stack_locals_metadata: Any,\\n2502:         disable_current_frame_resume: bool,\\n2503:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\\n2504:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2505:         Codegen resume function(s) and call it.\\n2506:         Assumes that the unsupported instruction has already been run.\\n2507: \\n2508:         Expects the stack to be in the state:\\n2509:             [\\n2510:                 frame N locals,\\n2511:                 frame N-1 stack + locals,\\n2512:                 ...,\\n2513:                 frame 1 stack + locals\\n2514:             ], frame N stack (post-instruction)\\n2515: \\n2516:         Args:\\n2517:             - inst: the instruction of the current (deepest) frame to resume at\\n2518:             - all_stack_locals_metadata: metadata returned from OutputGraph.compile_subgraph - contains\\n2519:                 metadata such as local names, NULL positions, stack length, etc.\\n2520:             - disable_current_frame_resume: If True, disable tracing on the current frame's resume function.\\n2521:                 Used for implementing nested step_graph_break.\\n2522:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2523: \\n2524:         self.instruction_pointer = None\\n2525: \\n2526:         if inst.opname == \\\"RETURN_VALUE\\\":\\n2527:             return [create_instruction(\\\"RETURN_VALUE\\\")]\\n2528:         elif inst.opname == \\\"RETURN_CONST\\\":\\n2529:             return [create_instruction(\\\"RETURN_CONST\\\", argval=inst.argval)]\\n2530: \\n2531: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2467-2530\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1860:         # only exists in python<=3.7\\n1861:         assert inst.target is not None\\n1862:         self.block_stack.append(BlockStackEntry(inst, inst.target, len(self.stack)))\\n1863: \\n1864:     def POP_BLOCK(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1865:         self.block_stack.pop()\\n1866: \\n1867:     def SETUP_WITH(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1868:         self.setup_or_before_with(inst)\\n1869: \\n1870:     def SETUP_FINALLY(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1871:         assert inst.target is not None\\n1872:         self.block_stack.append(BlockStackEntry(inst, inst.target, len(self.stack)))\\n1873: \\n1874:     def BEGIN_FINALLY(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1875:         self.push(None)\\n1876: \\n1877:     def WITH_CLEANUP_START(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1878:         exit, exc = self.popn(2)\\n1879:         assert exc is None\\n1880:         self.push(exc)\\n1881:         self.push(exit.call_function(self, [ConstantVariable.create(None)] * 3, {}))\\n1882: \\n1883:     def WITH_CLEANUP_FINISH(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1884:         self.popn(2)\\n1885:         self.push(None)\\n1886: \\n1887:     def FOR_ITER(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1888:         it = self.pop().realize()\\n1889:         try:\\n1890:             val = it.next_variable(self)\\n1891:             self.push(it)\\n1892:             self.push(val)\\n1893:         except (StopIteration, exc.ObservedUserStopIteration) as e:\\n1894:             if isinstance(e, exc.ObservedUserStopIteration):\\n1895:                 exc.handle_observed_exception(self)\\n1896: \\n1897:             # leave iterator upon exhaustion in 3.12\\n1898:             if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):\\n1899:                 # CPython 3.12 actually jumps to the instruction after the END_FOR\\n1900:                 # and performs the action of END_FOR as part of FOR_ITER. We jump\\n1901:                 # to the END_FOR and run it, so we need to make sure 2 values are\\n1902:                 # on the stack for it to pop.\\n1903:                 self.push(it)\\n1904:                 self.push(ConstantVariable.create(None))\\n1905:             self.jump(inst)\\n1906: \\n1907:     def _create_exception_type(self, val: VariableTracker) -> VariableTracker:\\n1908:         if isinstance(\\n1909:             val, (variables.BuiltinVariable, UserDefinedExceptionClassVariable)\\n1910:         ):\\n1911:             # Create the instance of the exception type\\n1912:             # https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Python/ceval.c#L6547-L6549\\n1913:             val = val.call_function(self, [], {})  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n1914:         return val\\n1915: \\n1916:     def _raise_exception_variable(self, val: VariableTracker) -> NoReturn:\\n1917:         # User can raise exception in 2 ways\\n1918:         #   1) raise exception type - raise NotImplementedError\\n1919:         #   2) raise exception instance - raise NotImplemetedError(\\\"foo\\\")\\n1920: \\n1921:         # 1) when user raises exception type\\n1922:         val = self._create_exception_type(val)\\n1923: \\n1924:         # Handle https://peps.python.org/pep-0479/\\n1925:         # CPython 3.12+ has a specific bytecode instruction (CALL_INTRINSIC_1 3) for this\\n1926:         if (\\n1927:             is_generator(self.f_code)\\n1928:             and isinstance(val, variables.ExceptionVariable)\\n1929:             and val.exc_type is StopIteration\\n1930:         ):\\n1931: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL1860-1930\"}"

self.start_point\\n1842:         )\\n1843:         self.instruction_pointer = self.indexof[inst.target]\\n1844:         self.start_point = self.instruction_pointer\\n1845: \\n1846:     JUMP_FORWARD = jump\\n1847:     JUMP_ABSOLUTE = jump\\n1848: \\n1849:     POP_JUMP_IF_FALSE = generic_jump(operator.not_, False)\\n1850:     POP_JUMP_IF_TRUE = generic_jump(operator.truth, False)\\n1851:     JUMP_IF_FALSE_OR_POP = generic_jump(operator.not_, True)\\n1852:     JUMP_IF_TRUE_OR_POP = generic_jump(operator.truth, True)\\n1853: \\n1854:     def SETUP_LOOP(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1855:         # only exists in python<=3.7\\n1856:         assert inst.target is not None\\n1857:         self.block_stack.append(BlockStackEntry(inst, inst.target, len(self.stack)))\\n1858: \\n1859:     def SETUP_EXCEPT(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1860:         # only exists in python<=3.7\\n1861:         assert inst.target is not None\\n1862:         self.block_stack.append(BlockStackEntry(inst, inst.target, len(self.stack)))\\n1863: \\n1864:     def POP_BLOCK(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1865:         self.block_stack.pop()\\n1866: \\n1867:     def SETUP_WITH(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1868:         self.setup_or_before_with(inst)\\n1869: \\n1870:     def SETUP_FINALLY(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1871:         assert inst.target is not None\\n1872:         self.block_stack.append(BlockStackEntry(inst, inst.target, len(self.stack)))\\n1873: \\n1874:     def BEGIN_FINALLY(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1875:         self.push(None)\\n1876: \\n1877:     def WITH_CLEANUP_START(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n1878:         exit, exc = self.popn(2)\\n1879:         assert exc is None\\n1880:         self.push(exc)\\n1881: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL1830-1880\"}"

\\n90: ## Graph Break in a Context Manager\\n91: \\n92: Another common example of an unresumable graph break is a graph break in most context managers:\\n93: \\n94: \",\"endLine\":94,\"startLine\":89},{\"content\":\"121: \\n122: There are some context managers where Dynamo can resume after a graph break.\\n123: Some of these can be found in `supported_ctx_manager_classes` in `torch/_dynamo/variables/torch.py`.\\n124: In general, any context manager represented by a `ContextWrappingVariable` subclass in\\n125: `torch/_dynamo/variables/ctx_manager.py` support resuming after a graph break. For example:\\n126: \\n127: \",\"endLine\":127,\"startLine\":121},{\"content\":\"108: \\n109: We can avoid skipping by moving the graph break outside of the context manager:\\n110: \\n111: \",\"endLine\":111,\"startLine\":108}],\"file\":\"docs/source/compile/programming_model.skipped_functions.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/blob/docs/source/compile/programming_model.skipped_functions.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"96: \\n97:         e.g. if the caller is not in a join context manager.\\n98:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n99: \",\"endLine\":99,\"startLine\":96},{\"content\":\"316:         Returns:\\n317:             An async work handle for the all-reduce meant to notify the context\\n318:             manager that the process has not yet joined if ``joinable`` is the\\n319:             first one passed into the context manager; ``None`` otherwise.\\n320:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n321: \",\"endLine\":321,\"startLine\":316},{\"content\":\"199: \\n200:         If there are multiple joinables, then the context manager uses the\\n201:         first specified device.\\n202: \",\"endLine\":202,\"startLine\":199}],\"file\":\"torch/distributed/algorithms/join.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/blob/torch/distributed/algorithms/join.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"210:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n211:         Similar to MetricsContext, but used to gather the runtime metrics that are\\n212:         decoupled from compilation, where there's not a natural place to insert a\\n213:         context manager.\\n214:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n215: \",\"endLine\":215,\"startLine\":210},{\"content\":\"6: It includes two main components:\\n7: - MetricsContext: A context manager for collecting metrics during compilation, supporting\\n8:   nested contexts and various metric types (counters, sets, key-value pairs)\\n9: - RuntimeMetricsContext: A specialized context for runtime metrics collection that doesn't\\n10:   require explicit context management\\n11: \\n12: \",\"endLine\":12,\"startLine\":6},{\"content\":\"63:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n64:         Use this class as a contextmanager to create a context under which to accumulate\\n65:         a set of metrics, e.g., metrics gathered during a compilation. On exit of the\\n66:         contextmanager, call the provided 'on_exit' function and pass a dictionary of\\n67:         all metrics set during the lifetime of the contextmanager.\\n68:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n69: "{\"content\":\"3430:         pass\\n3431: \\n3432:     def BEFORE_WITH(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n3433:         self.setup_or_before_with(inst)\\n3434: \\n3435:     def setup_or_before_with(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n3436:         ctx = self.pop()\\n3437:         if not isinstance(\\n3438:             ctx, (ContextWrappingVariable, GenericContextWrappingVariable)\\n3439:         ):\\n3440:             unimplemented_v2(\\n3441:                 gb_type=\\\"Unsupported context manager\\\",\\n3442:                 context=f\\\"Attempted SETUP_WITH/BEFORE_WITH on {ctx}\\\",\\n3443:                 explanation=f\\\"Dynamo does not know how to enter a `{ctx.python_type_name()}` context manager.\\\",\\n3444:                 hints=[\\n3445:                     \\\"Avoid using the unsupported context manager.\\\",\\n3446:                     \\\"If the context manager seems like it should be supported (e.g. torch.set_grad_enabled), then \\\"\\n3447:                     \\\"it may be the case that it was created outside the compiled region, which Dynamo does not support. \\\"\\n3448:                     \\\"Supported context managers can cross graph break boundaries only if they are local non-closure \\\"\\n3449:                     \\\"variables, or are intermediate values.\\\",\\n3450:                     \\\"File an issue to PyTorch. Simple context managers can potentially be supported, \\\"\\n3451:                     \\\"but note that context managers can't be supported in general\\\",\\n3452:                 ],\\n3453:             )\\n3454: \\n3455:         if (\\n3456:             isinstance(ctx, GenericContextWrappingVariable)\\n3457:             and not ctx.supports_graph_breaks()\\n3458:         ):\\n3459:             self.active_generic_context_managers.append(ctx)\\n3460: \\n3461:         # Need this redundant check for mypy\\n3462:         assert isinstance(\\n3463:             ctx, (ContextWrappingVariable, GenericContextWrappingVariable)\\n3464:         )\\n3465: \\n3466:         exit = WithExitFunctionVariable(\\n3467:             ctx,\\n3468:             inst.target,\\n3469:         )\\n3470: \\n3471:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n3472:             # See create_call_resume_at for block stack details.\\n3473:             # Only push a block if the current instruction's block is a\\n3474:             # with block that is not nested in a try block - that is, the current\\n3475:             # instruction's block target is the same as the top block's target.\\n3476:             if inst.exn_tab_entry and (\\n3477:                 not self.block_stack\\n3478:                 or inst.exn_tab_entry.target is not self.block_stack[-1].target\\n3479:             ):\\n3480:                 target = None\\n3481:             else:\\n3482:                 assert self.next_instruction.exn_tab_entry is not None\\n3483:                 target = self.next_instruction.exn_tab_entry.target\\n3484:         else:\\n3485:             target = inst.target\\n3486: \\n3487:         self.push(exit)\\n3488: \\n3489:         if target:\\n3490:             if isinstance(self, InstructionTranslator) or config.nested_graph_breaks:\\n3491:                 self.block_stack.append(\\n3492:                     BlockStackEntry(inst, target, len(self.stack), ctx)\\n3493:                 )\\n3494:             else:\\n3495:                 self.block_stack.append(BlockStackEntry(inst, target, len(self.stack)))\\n3496: \\n3497:         self.push(ctx.enter(self))\\n3498: \\n3499:     def append_prefix_inst(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n3500:         assert self.accept_prefix_inst\\n3501:         self.prefix_insts.append(inst)\\n3502: \\n3503:     def MAKE_CELL(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n3504:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 12) and not self.accept_prefix_inst:\\n3505:             # In 3.12+, MAKE_CELL is not longer necessarily a prefix instruction.\\n3506:             # It can be generated by inlined comprehensions.\\n3507:             assert isinstance(self.symbolic_locals[inst.argval], NullVariable)\\n3508:             self.symbolic_locals[inst.argval] = (\\n3509:                 self.output.side_effects.track_cell_new()\\n3510:             )\\n3511:         else:\\n3512:             self.append_prefix_inst(inst)\\n3513: \\n3514:     def COPY_FREE_VARS(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n3515:         self.append_prefix_inst(inst)\\n3516: \\n3517:     def RETURN_GENERATOR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n3518:         self.append_prefix_inst(inst)\\n3519: \\n3520:     # 3.12 opcodes\\n3521:     # BINARY/STORE_SLICE opcodes are broken down into\\n3522:     # BUILD_SLICE 2 and BINARY/STORE_SUBSCR\\n3523: \\n3524:     def END_FOR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n3525:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 13):\\n3526:             self.pop()\\n3527:         else:\\n3528:             self.popn(2)\\n3529: \\n3530:     def LOAD_FAST_CHECK(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n3531:         if istype(self.symbolic_locals.get(inst.argval, None), NullVariable):\\n3532:             unimplemented_v2(\\n3533:                 gb_type=\\\"LOAD_FAST_CHECK on uninitialized variable\\\",\\n3534:                 context=inst.argval,\\n3535:                 explanation=f\\\"Attempted to load uninitialized local variable {inst.argval}\\\",\\n3536:                 hints=[*graph_break_hints.USER_ERROR],\\n3537:             )\\n3538:         self.LOAD_FAST(inst)\\n3539: \\n3540:     def LOAD_FAST_AND_CLEAR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n3541:         if inst.argval not in self.symbolic_locals:\\n3542:             self.push(NullVariable())\\n3543:         else:\\n3544:             self.LOAD_FAST(inst)\\n3545:         self.symbolic_locals[inst.argval] = NullVariable()\\n3546: \\n3547:     def LOAD_SUPER_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n3548:         self.CALL_FUNCTION(dataclasses.replace(inst, argval=2))\\n3549:         assert inst.arg is not None\\n3550:         if inst.arg & 1:\\n3551: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL3430-3550\"}"

[\\n2553:         #   [frame N stack (fixed) + locals]\\n2554:         #   ...,\\n2555:         #   [frame 1 stack + locals]\\n2556:         # ],\\n2557: \\n2558:         #\\n2559:         txes = []\\n2560:         cur_tx: Optional[InstructionTranslatorBase] = self\\n2561:         while cur_tx is not None:\\n2562:             txes.append(cur_tx)\\n2563:             cur_tx = cur_tx.parent\\n2564:         assert len(txes) == len(all_stack_locals_metadata)\\n2565: \\n2566:         # Handle inactive context variables.\\n2567:         # The resume function assumes that context variables are the class, NOT the object.\\n2568:         # e.g. torch.set_grad_enabled(True) will be reconstructed as torch.set_grad_enabled\\n2569:         # NOTE: if the unsupported instruction modifies the inactive context variable, it may\\n2570:         # result in silent incorrectness!\\n2571:         for i, meta in enumerate(all_stack_locals_metadata):\\n2572:             if i == 0 and disable_current_frame_resume:\\n2573:                 continue\\n2574: \\n2575:             for (j, _), j_orig in zip(meta.stack_ctx_args, meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig):\\n2576:                 # Replace the stack var with the context class\\n2577:                 ctx = cast(ContextWrappingVariable, txes[i].stack[j_orig])\\n2578:                 # frames[i][j] = reconstructed_ctx\\n2579:                 cg.append_output(create_dup_top())\\n2580:                 ctx.reconstruct_type(cg)\\n2581:                 cg.extend_output(\\n2582:                     [\\n2583:                         *create_swap(2),\\n2584:                         cg.create_load_const(i),\\n2585:                         cg.create_binary_subscr(),\\n2586:                         cg.create_load_const(j),\\n2587:                         create_instruction(\\\"STORE_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n2588:                     ]\\n2589:                 )\\n2590: \\n2591:             for name, _ in meta.locals_ctx_args:\\n2592:                 # Replace the local with the context class\\n2593:                 ctx = cast(ContextWrappingVariable, txes[i].symbolic_locals[name])\\n2594:                 # frames[i][meta.num_stack +meta.locals_names[name]] = reconstructed_ctx\\n2595:                 cg.append_output(create_dup_top())\\n2596:                 ctx.reconstruct_type(cg)\\n2597:                 cg.extend_output(\\n2598:                     [\\n2599:                         *create_swap(2),\\n2600:                         cg.create_load_const(i),\\n2601:                         cg.create_binary_subscr(),\\n2602:                         cg.create_load_const(meta.num_stack + meta.locals_names[name]),\\n2603:                         create_instruction(\\\"STORE_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n2604:                     ]\\n2605:                 )\\n2606: \\n2607:         # build the resume function for each frame\\n2608:         resume_names = []\\n2609:         resume_codes: list[types.CodeType] = []\\n2610:         for i, meta in enumerate(all_stack_locals_metadata):\\n2611:             cur_tx = txes[i]\\n2612:             if cur_tx is self:\\n2613:                 resume_inst = inst\\n2614:             else:\\n2615:                 resume_inst = cur_tx.next_instruction\\n2616:             # If the resume instruction is a jump absolute, then resume\\n2617:             # at the target instead. This handles the case where we\\n2618:             # graph break again in a nested function before jump-resuming\\n2619:             # this frame.\\n2620:             if is_jump_absolute(resume_inst):\\n2621:                 assert resume_inst.target\\n2622:                 resume_inst = resume_inst.target\\n2623:             resume_name = unique_id(f\\\"__resume_at_{resume_inst.offset}\\\")\\n2624:             resume_names.append(resume_name)\\n2625: \\n2626:             # More locals may have been pruned in the current frame\\n2627:             # after the unsupported instruction (e.g. branch).\\n2628:             # There should not be any pruning in the other frames since\\n2629:             # the current instruction is a CALL.\\n2630:             if cur_tx is self:\\n2631: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2530-2630\"}"

current stack state: frames, frames[i], *(frame i live locals), frames[i]\\n2664:                 cg.extend_output(\\n2665:                     [\\n2666:                         create_instruction(\\\"POP_TOP\\\"),\\n2667:                         create_instruction(\\\"BUILD_LIST\\\", arg=len(argnames)),\\n2668:                         *create_swap(2),\\n2669:                         # frames, frames i live locals, frames[i]\\n2670:                         *create_binary_slice(meta.num_stack, None, True),\\n2671:                         # frames[i][num_stack:] = frame i live locals\\n2672:                     ]\\n2673:                 )\\n2674:                 # current stack state: frames\\n2675:             else:\\n2676:                 argnames = tuple(meta.locals_names.keys())\\n2677:                 argnames_null = tuple(meta.locals_null_keys)\\n2678: \\n2679:             if sys.version_info < (3, 12):\\n2680:                 assert len(argnames_null) == 0, \\\"variables should not be NULL in < 3.12\\\"\\n2681: \\n2682:             # compile_subgraph did not codegen any NULLs,\\n2683:             # so we should not count NullVariables\\n2684:             stack_len = len(cur_tx.stack) - len(meta.stack_null_idxes)\\n2685: \\n2686:             new_code: types.CodeType = ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\\n2687:                 cur_tx.f_code,\\n2688:                 cur_tx.lineno,\\n2689:                 resume_inst.offset,\\n2690:                 tuple(b.target.offset for b in cur_tx.block_stack),\\n2691:                 stack_len,\\n2692:                 argnames,\\n2693:                 argnames_null,\\n2694:                 tuple(b.resume_fn() for b in cur_tx.block_stack),\\n2695:                 tuple(meta.stack_ctx_args),\\n2696:                 tuple(meta.locals_ctx_args),\\n2697:                 tuple(meta.stack_null_idxes),\\n2698:                 tuple(resume_codes),\\n2699:             )\\n2700:             resume_codes.append(new_code)\\n2701: \\n2702:             # Add original GraphModule context to the resume function to handle\\n2703:             # the case of a graph break while tracing a GraphModule\\n2704:             orig_graphmodule_maybe = code_context.get_context(cur_tx.f_code).get(\\n2705:                 \\\"orig_graphmodule\\\", lambda: None\\n2706:             )()\\n2707:             if orig_graphmodule_maybe is not None:\\n2708:                 code_context.get_context(new_code)[\\\"orig_graphmodule\\\"] = weakref.ref(\\n2709:                     orig_graphmodule_maybe\\n2710:                 )\\n2711: \\n2712:             # add resume function to the global scope\\n2713:             if new_code.co_freevars:\\n2714:                 # expose code object for debugging purposes\\n2715:                 cur_tx.output.install_global_unsafe(resume_name, new_code)\\n2716:                 package_name = None\\n2717:             else:\\n2718:                 # This is safe: we pre-generate a unique name\\n2719:                 cur_tx.output.install_global_unsafe(\\n2720:                     resume_name,\\n2721:                     types.FunctionType(new_code, cur_tx.f_globals, resume_name),\\n2722:                 )\\n2723:                 package_name = resume_name\\n2724: \\n2725:             if cur_tx.package is not None:\\n2726:                 cur_tx.package.add_resume_function(\\n2727:                     new_code, cur_tx.f_globals[\\\"__name__\\\"], package_name\\n2728:                 )\\n2729: \\n2730:         if disable_current_frame_resume:\\n2731:             from .eval_frame import skip_code\\n2732: \\n2733:             skip_code(resume_codes[0])\\n2734: \\n2735:         # load first resume function (to be called this frame)\\n2736:         if resume_codes[-1].co_freevars:\\n2737:             cg.make_function_with_closure(\\n2738:                 txes[-1], resume_names[-1], resume_codes[-1], True, 1\\n2739:             )\\n2740:         else:\\n2741:             cg.extend_output(cg.load_function_name(resume_names[-1], True, 1))\\n2742: \\n2743:         # load all other resume functions (to be called later)\\n2744:         resume_names.pop()\\n2745:         resume_codes.pop()\\n2746:         for tx, name, code in zip(txes, resume_names, resume_codes):\\n2747:             if code.co_freevars:\\n2748:                 cg.make_function_with_closure(tx, name, code, False, 0)\\n2749:             else:\\n2750:                 cg.extend_output(cg.load_function_name(name, False, 0))\\n2751: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2630-2750\"}"

  "content": "{\"content\":\"2438: \\n2439:     def STORE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2440:         speculation = self.speculate()\\n2441:         if speculation.failed(self):\\n2442:             return self.store_attr_graph_break(inst)\\n2443:         val, obj = self.popn(2)\\n2444: \\n2445:         if isinstance(obj, NNModuleVariable) and not isinstance(val, ConstantVariable):\\n2446:             # We don't allow side effects during export on non-constant values\\n2447:             # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1475\\n2448:             assert not self.export, (\\n2449:                 f\\\"Mutating module attribute {inst.argval} during export.\\\"\\n2450:             )\\n2451: \\n2452:         try:\\n2453:             BuiltinVariable(setattr).call_function(\\n2454:                 self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n2455:                 [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval), val],\\n2456:                 {},\\n2457:             )\\n2458:             return\\n2459:         except Unsupported as e:\\n2460:             if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\\n2461:                 raise\\n2462: speculation.fail_and_restart_analysis(self.error_on_graph_break)\\n2466: \\n2467:     def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2468:         log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\\\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\\\")\\n2469:         if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\\n2470:             unimplemented_v2(\\n2471:                 gb_type=\\\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\\\",\\n2472:                 context=\\\"\\\",\\n2473:                 explanation=\\\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \\\"\\n2474:                 \\\"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\\\",\\n2475:                 hints=[],\\n2476:             )\\n2477:         all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\\n2478:             self,\\n2479:             reason=GraphCompileReason(\\\"store_attr\\\", [self.frame_summary()]),\\n2480:             stack_pops=2,\\n2481:         )\\n2482:         self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\\n2483:         self.popn(2)\\n2484:         self.output.add_output_instructions(\\n2485:             self.create_call_resume_at(\\n2486:                 self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\\n2487:             )\\n2488:         )\\n2489: \\n2490:     def DELETE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2491: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2438-2490\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2438: \n2439:     def STORE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2440:         speculation = self.speculate()\n2441:         if speculation.failed(self):\n2442:             return self.store_attr_graph_break(inst)\n2443:         val, obj = self.popn(2)\n2444: \n2445:         if isinstance(obj, NNModuleVariable) and not isinstance(val, ConstantVariable):\n2446:             # We don't allow side effects during export on non-constant values\n2447:             # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1475\n2448:             assert not self.export, (\n2449:                 f\"Mutating module attribute {inst.argval} during export.\"\n2450:             )\n2451: \n2452:         try:\n2453:             BuiltinVariable(setattr).call_function(\n2454:                 self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n2455:                 [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval), val],\n2456:                 {},\n2457:             )\n2458:             return\n2459:         except Unsupported as e:\n2460:             if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n2461:                 raise\n2462:             log.debug(\"STORE_ATTR triggered compile\", exc_info=True)\n2463:             e.remove_from_stats()\n2464:             e.add_to_stats(\"graph_break\")\n2465:         speculation.fail_and_restart_analysis(self.error_on_graph_break)\n2466: \n2467:     def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2468:         log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\")\n2469:         if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n2470:             unimplemented_v2(\n2471:                 gb_type=\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\",\n2472:                 context=\"\",\n2473:                 explanation=\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \"\n2474:                 \"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\",\n2475:                 hints=[],\n2476:             )\n2477:         all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n2478:             self,\n2479:             reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),\n2480:             stack_pops=2,\n2481:         )\n2482:         self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\n2483:         self.popn(2)\n2484:         self.output.add_output_instructions(\n2485:             self.create_call_resume_at(\n2486:                 self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n2487:             )\n2488:         )\n2489: \n2490:     def DELETE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2491: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2438-2490"
  }
}

  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "\"\"\"\nThis module provides functionality for resuming Python execution at specific points in code,\nprimarily used by PyTorch Dynamo for control flow handling and optimization. It implements\nbytecode transformation and execution state management to enable:\n\n- Resuming execution at arbitrary points in Python bytecode\n- Managing context managers and their state across execution boundaries\n- Transforming and generating new code objects with preserved execution state\n- Supporting Python 3.11+ exception handling and block management\n- Restoring torch function mode stacks and other execution context\n\nThe module is critical for PyTorch Dynamo's ability to optimize code while preserving\nPython semantics and execution state.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport copy\nimport dataclasses\nimport sys\nimport types\nfrom collections.abc import Iterable\nfrom contextlib import AbstractContextManager\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional\n\nfrom .bytecode_transformation import (\n    add_push_null,\n    bytecode_from_template,\n    create_call_function,\n    create_instruction,\n    create_jump_absolute,\n    create_load_const,\n    Instruction,\n    overwrite_instruction,\n    transform_code_object,\n    unique_id,\n)\nfrom .utils import ExactWeakKeyDictionary\n\n\n# taken from code.h in cpython\nCO_OPTIMIZED = 0x0001\nCO_NEWLOCALS = 0x0002\nCO_VARARGS = 0x0004\nCO_VARKEYWORDS = 0x0008\nCO_NESTED = 0x0010\nCO_GENERATOR = 0x0020\nCO_NOFREE = 0x0040\nCO_COROUTINE = 0x0080\nCO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE = 0x0100\nCO_ASYNC_GENERATOR = 0x0200"
}
