map old hook targets to new targets generated by the hook\n            old_hook_target_remap = {}\n            null_idxes_i = 0\n            stack_ctx_vars_d = dict(stack_ctx_vars)  # type: ignore[var-annotated,arg-type]\n            for i in range(nstack):\n                while (\n                    null_idxes_i < len(null_idxes)\n                    and null_idxes[null_idxes_i] == i + null_idxes_i\n                ):\n                    prefix.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n                    null_idxes_i += 1\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=f\"___stack{i}\"))\n                if i in hooks:\n                    hook = hooks.pop(i)\n                    hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\n                    prefix.extend(hook_insts)\n                    if is_py311_plus:\n                        hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\n                        old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n                        meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\n                        old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\n                if i in stack_ctx_vars_d:\n                    # NOTE: we assume that current stack var is a context manager CLASS!\n                    # Load args for context variable and construct it\n                    prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(stack_ctx_vars_d[i]))\n\n            if is_py311_plus:\n                # reverse the mapping since targets of later/nested contexts are inserted\n                # into the mapping later, but show up earlier in the prefix.\n                meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = list(\n                    reversed(meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap)\n                )\n\n            assert not hooks\n\n            # NOTE: we assume that local var is a context manager CLASS!\n            # initialize inactive context vars in argnames\n            for name, vals in argnames_ctx_vars:\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=name))\n                prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(vals))\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=name))\n\n            # 3.12+: store NULL into variables that were NULL\n            if argnames_null:\n                assert sys.version_info >= (3, 12)\n                for v in argnames_null:\n                    assert v not in args\n                    prefix.extend(",

  "newString": "                if i in hooks:\n                    hook = hooks.pop(i)\n                    hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\n                    prefix.extend(hook_insts)\n                    if is_py311_plus:\n                        hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\n                        if hook_target_offset in offset_to_inst:\n                            old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n                            meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\n                            old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target",
  "oldString": "                if i in hooks:\n                    hook = hooks.pop(i)\n                    hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\n                    prefix.extend(hook_insts)\n                    if is_py311_plus:\n                        hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\n                        old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n                        meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\n                        old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target",
  "originalFile": "\"\"\"\nThis module provides functionality for resuming Python execution at specific points in code,\nprimarily used by PyTorch Dynamo for control flow handling and optimization. It implements\nbytecode transformation and execution state management to enable:\n\n- Resuming execution at arbitrary points in Python bytecode\n- Managing context managers and their state across execution boundaries\n- Transforming and generating new code objects with preserved execution state\n- Supporting Python 3.11+ exception handling and block management\n- Restoring torch function mode stacks and other execution context\n\nThe module is critical for PyTorch Dynamo's ability to optimize code while preserving\nPython semantics and execution state.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport copy\nimport dataclasses\nimport sys\nimport types\nfrom collections.abc import Iterable\nfrom contextlib import AbstractContextManager\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional\n\nfrom .bytecode_transformation import (\n    add_push_null,\n    bytecode_from_template,\n    create_call_function,\n    create_instruction,\n    create_jump_absolute,\n    create_load_const,\n    Instruction,\n    overwrite_instruction,\n    transform_code_object,\n    unique_id,\n)\nfrom .utils import ExactWeakKeyDictionary\n\n\n# taken from code.h in cpython\nCO_OPTIMIZED = 0x0001\nCO_NEWLOCALS = 0x0002\nCO_VARARGS = 0x0004\nCO_VARKEYWORDS = 0x0008\nCO_NESTED = 0x0010\nCO_GENERATOR = 0x0020\nCO_NOFREE = 0x0040\nCO_COROUTINE = 0x0080\nCO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE = 0x0100\nCO_ASYNC_GENERATOR = 0x0200\n\n# trace_rules.py import this constant for consistency\nTORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX = \"torch_dynamo_resume_in\"\nIS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME = \"__is_tracing_resume_prologue\"\n\n\ndef _initial_push_null(insts: list[Instruction]) -> None:\n    if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n        insts.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n        if sys.version_info < (3, 13):\n            insts.append(create_instruction(\"SWAP\", arg=2))\n\n\n# Generates bytecode from template and splits the code where LOAD_FAST dummy is present.\ndef _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n    template: Callable[..., Any],\n    stack_index: int,\n    varname_map: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\n) -> tuple[list[Instruction], list[Instruction]]:\n    template_code = bytecode_from_template(template, varname_map=varname_map)\n    template_code.append(create_instruction(\"POP_TOP\"))\n\n    # adjust exception table entry depth\n    for inst in template_code:\n        if inst.exn_tab_entry:\n            inst.exn_tab_entry.depth += stack_index\n\n    # search for LOAD_FAST dummy and replace it with 2 NOPs (we can break up the bytecode between them)\n    dummy_idx, dummy_inst = next(\n        (\n            (i, inst)\n            for i, inst in enumerate(template_code)\n            if inst.opname == \"LOAD_FAST\" and inst.argval == \"dummy\"\n        ),\n        (None, None),\n    )\n    assert dummy_idx is not None and dummy_inst is not None\n\n    # replace LOAD_FAST dummy with first NOP marking exception area\n    overwrite_instruction(dummy_inst, [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n\n    # POP_TOP follows LOAD_FAST dummy - replace with NOP marking end of exception area\n    assert template_code[dummy_idx + 1].opname == \"POP_TOP\"\n    overwrite_instruction(template_code[dummy_idx + 1], [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n\n    return template_code[: dummy_idx + 1], template_code[dummy_idx + 1 :]\n\n\ndef _try_except_tf_mode_template(dummy: Any, stack_var_name: Any) -> None:\n    # NOTE: Make sure this name matches what is generated by symbolic_convert:import_source\n    # on torch._dynamo.utils.\n    global __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils\n    try:\n        dummy\n    except:  # noqa: E722, B001\n        __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils.set_torch_function_mode_stack(  # type: ignore[name-defined]\n            stack_var_name\n        )\n        raise\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass ReenterWith:\n    stack_index: int\n    target_values: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]] = None\n\n    def try_except_torch_function_mode(\n        self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n    ) -> list[Instruction]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Codegen based off of:\n        try:\n            (rest)\n        except:\n            (restore previous tf mode stack)\n            raise\n        \"\"\"\n        from .variables.torch_function import get_prev_stack_var_name\n\n        setup_try_except, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n            _try_except_tf_mode_template,\n            self.stack_index,\n            varname_map={\"stack_var_name\": get_prev_stack_var_name()},\n        )\n        cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n\n        return setup_try_except\n\n    # If we do not want to destroy the stack, we can do the same thing as a\n    # `SETUP_WITH` block, only that we store the context manager in a local_symbol\n    def try_finally(\n        self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n    ) -> list[Instruction]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Codegen based off of:\n        load args\n        enter context\n        try:\n            (rest)\n        finally:\n            exit context\n        \"\"\"\n        # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\n        load_args = []\n        if self.target_values:\n            load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\n        ctx_name = unique_id(f\"___context_manager_{self.stack_index}\")\n        if ctx_name not in code_options[\"co_varnames\"]:\n            code_options[\"co_varnames\"] += (ctx_name,)\n        for name in [\"__enter__\", \"__exit__\"]:\n            if name not in code_options[\"co_names\"]:\n                code_options[\"co_names\"] += (name,)\n\n        create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\n        _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\n        create_ctx.extend(\n            [\n                *load_args,\n                *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\n                create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=ctx_name),\n            ]\n        )\n\n        def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\n            ctx.__enter__()\n            try:\n                dummy\n            finally:\n                ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n\n        setup_try_finally, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n            _template, self.stack_index, varname_map={\"ctx\": ctx_name}\n        )\n        cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n        return create_ctx + setup_try_finally\n\n    def __call__(\n        self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n    ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], Optional[Instruction]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Codegen based off of:\n        with ctx(args):\n            (rest)\n        \"\"\"\n        # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\n        load_args = []\n        if self.target_values:\n            load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\n\n        create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\n        _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\n        create_ctx.extend(\n            [\n                *load_args,\n                *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\n            ]\n        )\n\n        def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\n            with ctx:\n                dummy\n\n        setup_with, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n            _template, self.stack_index\n        )\n        cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n\n        load_fast_ctx_inst = next(\n            (\n                inst\n                for inst in setup_with\n                if inst.opname == \"LOAD_FAST\" and inst.argval == \"ctx\"\n            ),\n            None,\n        )\n        assert load_fast_ctx_inst is not None\n        # ctx already loaded on stack before the template - no need to LOAD_FAST\n        overwrite_instruction(load_fast_ctx_inst, [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n\n        # 3.11+ only\n        push_exc_info_gen = (\n            inst for inst in epilogue if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\"\n        )\n        push_exc_info_inst = next(push_exc_info_gen, None)\n        # expect only 1 PUSH_EXC_INFO in epilogue\n        assert next(push_exc_info_gen, None) is None\n\n        return create_ctx + setup_with, push_exc_info_inst\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass ResumeFunctionMetadata:\n    code: types.CodeType\n    instructions: list[Instruction] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\n    # Python 3.11+ fields\n    # NOTE: Python 3.11 removed blocks, but for our purposes, a \"block\" consists\n    # of instructions of all exception table entries that have the same target.\n\n    # map from PUSH_EXC_INFO's in the prefix to original block target offset\n    prefix_block_target_offset_remap: list[int] = dataclasses.field(\n        default_factory=list\n    )\n    # per-offset map from new block target offsets to original block target offsets\n    block_target_offset_remap: dict[int, dict[int, int]] = dataclasses.field(\n        default_factory=dict\n    )\n\n\ndef _filter_iter(\n    l1: Iterable[Any],\n    l2: Iterable[Any],\n    cond: Callable[[Any, Any], bool],\n) -> list[Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Two-pointer conditional filter.\n    e.g. _filter_iter(insts, sorted_offsets, lambda i, o: i.offset == o)\n    returns the instructions with offsets in sorted_offsets\n    \"\"\"\n    it = iter(l2)\n    res: list[Instruction] = []\n    try:\n        cur = next(it)\n        for val in l1:\n            if cond(val, cur):\n                res.append(val)\n                cur = next(it)\n    except StopIteration:\n        pass\n    return res\n\n\ndef _load_tuple_and_call(tup: tuple[Any, ...]) -> list[Instruction]:\n    insts: list[Instruction] = []\n    _initial_push_null(insts)\n    insts.extend(create_load_const(val) for val in tup)\n    insts.extend(create_call_function(len(tup), False))\n    return insts\n\n\nclass ContinueExecutionCache:\n    cache = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\n    generated_code_metadata = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\n\n    @classmethod\n    def lookup(cls, code: types.CodeType, lineno: int, *key: Any) -> types.CodeType:\n        if code not in cls.cache:\n            cls.cache[code] = {}\n        key = tuple(key)\n        if key not in cls.cache[code]:\n            cls.cache[code][key] = cls.generate(code, lineno, *key)\n        return cls.cache[code][key]\n\n    @classmethod\n    def generate(\n        cls,\n        code: types.CodeType,\n        lineno: int,\n        offset: int,\n        setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],  # only used in Python 3.11+\n        nstack: int,\n        argnames: tuple[str, ...],\n        argnames_null: tuple[str, ...],\n        setup_fns: tuple[ReenterWith, ...],\n        stack_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\n        argnames_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\n        null_idxes: tuple[int, ...],\n        # mainly used to ensure distinct code objects per stack trace,\n        # which prevents excessive recompilation of inner frames\n        nested_code_objs: tuple[types.CodeType],\n    ) -> types.CodeType:\n        assert offset is not None\n        assert not (\n            code.co_flags\n            & (CO_GENERATOR | CO_COROUTINE | CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE | CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR)\n        )\n        assert code.co_flags & CO_OPTIMIZED\n        if code in ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata:\n            return cls.generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n                code,\n                lineno,\n                offset,\n                setup_fn_target_offsets,\n                nstack,\n                argnames,\n                argnames_null,\n                setup_fns,\n                stack_ctx_vars,\n                argnames_ctx_vars,\n                null_idxes,\n                nested_code_objs,\n            )\n\n        is_py311_plus = sys.version_info >= (3, 11)\n        meta = ResumeFunctionMetadata(code)\n\n        def update(\n            instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n        ) -> None:\n            meta.instructions = copy.deepcopy(instructions)\n\n            args = [\"__nested_resume_fns\", \"__nested_frame_values\"]\n            args += [f\"___stack{i}\" for i in range(nstack)]\n            args.extend(v for v in argnames if v not in args)\n            freevars = tuple(code_options[\"co_cellvars\"] or []) + tuple(\n                code_options[\"co_freevars\"] or []\n            )\n            freevars = tuple(sorted(freevars))\n            code_options[\"co_name\"] = (\n                f\"{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{code_options['co_name']}_at_{lineno}\"\n            )\n            if is_py311_plus:\n                qualified_path = code_options[\"co_qualname\"].rsplit(\".\", maxsplit=1)\n                if len(qualified_path) == 1:\n                    code_options[\"co_qualname\"] = code_options[\"co_name\"]\n                else:\n                    assert len(qualified_path) == 2\n                    module_name, co_name = qualified_path\n                    code_options[\"co_qualname\"] = (\n                        f\"{module_name}.{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{co_name}_at_{lineno}\"\n                    )\n            code_options[\"co_firstlineno\"] = lineno\n            code_options[\"co_cellvars\"] = ()\n            code_options[\"co_freevars\"] = freevars\n            code_options[\"co_argcount\"] = len(args)\n            code_options[\"co_posonlyargcount\"] = 0\n            code_options[\"co_kwonlyargcount\"] = 0\n            code_options[\"co_varnames\"] = tuple(\n                args\n                + [v for v in argnames_null if v not in args]\n                + [v for v in code_options[\"co_varnames\"] if v not in args]\n                + [IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME]\n            )\n            code_options[\"co_flags\"] = code_options[\"co_flags\"] & ~(\n                CO_VARARGS | CO_VARKEYWORDS\n            )\n            target = next(i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n\n            prefix = []\n            if is_py311_plus:\n                if freevars:\n                    prefix.append(\n                        create_instruction(\"COPY_FREE_VARS\", arg=len(freevars))\n                    )\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"RESUME\", arg=0))\n\n            # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue to prevent graph breaks.\n            # This doesn't really do anything at runtime, but dynamo will trace this\n            # and will know that we're in a resume function prologue.\n            prefix.extend(\n                [\n                    create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=True),\n                    create_instruction(\n                        \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n                    ),\n                ]\n            )\n\n            cleanup: list[Instruction] = []\n            hooks = {fn.stack_index: fn for fn in setup_fns}\n            hook_target_offsets = {\n                fn.stack_index: setup_fn_target_offsets[i]\n                for i, fn in enumerate(setup_fns)\n            }\n            offset_to_inst = {inst.offset: inst for inst in instructions}\n            # map old hook targets to new targets generated by the hook\n            old_hook_target_remap = {}\n            null_idxes_i = 0\n            stack_ctx_vars_d = dict(stack_ctx_vars)  # type: ignore[var-annotated,arg-type]\n            for i in range(nstack):\n                while (\n                    null_idxes_i < len(null_idxes)\n                    and null_idxes[null_idxes_i] == i + null_idxes_i\n                ):\n                    prefix.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n                    null_idxes_i += 1\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=f\"___stack{i}\"))\n                if i in hooks:\n                    hook = hooks.pop(i)\n                    hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\n                    prefix.extend(hook_insts)\n                    if is_py311_plus:\n                        hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\n                        old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n                        meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\n                        old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\n                if i in stack_ctx_vars_d:\n                    # NOTE: we assume that current stack var is a context manager CLASS!\n                    # Load args for context variable and construct it\n                    prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(stack_ctx_vars_d[i]))\n\n            if is_py311_plus:\n                # reverse the mapping since targets of later/nested contexts are inserted\n                # into the mapping later, but show up earlier in the prefix.\n                meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = list(\n                    reversed(meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap)\n                )\n\n            assert not hooks\n\n            # NOTE: we assume that local var is a context manager CLASS!\n            # initialize inactive context vars in argnames\n            for name, vals in argnames_ctx_vars:\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=name))\n                prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(vals))\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=name))\n\n            # 3.12+: store NULL into variables that were NULL\n            if argnames_null:\n                assert sys.version_info >= (3, 12)\n                for v in argnames_null:\n                    assert v not in args\n                    prefix.extend(\n                        [\n                            create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"),\n                            create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=v),\n                        ]\n                    )\n\n            # Call nested resume function\n            if nested_code_objs:\n                prefix.extend(\n                    [\n                        # set up __nested_resume_fns[-1] call\n                        *add_push_null(\n                            [\n                                create_instruction(\n                                    \"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"\n                                ),\n                                create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                                create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n                            ]\n                        ),\n                        # del __nested_resume_fns[-1]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                        create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n                        # load [__nested_resume_fns, __nested_frame_values]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"BUILD_LIST\", arg=2),\n                        # load __nested_frame_values[-1]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                        create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n                        # create [\n                        #     __nested_resume_fns,\n                        #     __nested_frame_values,\n                        #     *__nested_frame_values[-1],\n                        # ]\n                        create_instruction(\"LIST_EXTEND\", arg=1),\n                        # del __nested_frame_values[-1]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                        create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n                        # delete __nested values\n                        create_instruction(\"DELETE_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n                        create_instruction(\n                            \"DELETE_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"\n                        ),\n                        # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks\n                        # in the nested resume\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=False),\n                        create_instruction(\n                            \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n                        ),\n                        # finish the call\n                        create_instruction(\"CALL_FUNCTION_EX\", arg=0),\n                    ]\n                )\n            else:\n                # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks after the jump\n                prefix.extend(\n                    [\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=False),\n                        create_instruction(\n                            \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n                        ),\n                    ]\n                )\n\n            prefix.append(create_jump_absolute(target))\n\n            # because the line number table monotonically increases from co_firstlineno\n            # remove starts_line for any instructions before the graph break instruction\n            # this will ensure the instructions after the break have the correct line numbers\n            for inst in instructions:\n                if inst.offset == target.offset:\n                    break\n                inst.starts_line = None\n                if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n                    inst.positions = None\n\n            if cleanup:\n                prefix.extend(cleanup)\n                prefix.extend(cls.unreachable_codes(code_options))\n\n            # remap original instructions' exception table entries\n            if old_hook_target_remap:\n                assert is_py311_plus\n                for inst in instructions:\n                    if (\n                        inst.exn_tab_entry\n                        and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\n                    ):\n                        inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                            inst.exn_tab_entry.target\n                        ]\n\n            # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\n            instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\n\n        new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\n        ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\n        return new_code\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\n        \"\"\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\"\"\"\n        return [\n            create_load_const(None),\n            create_instruction(\"RAISE_VARARGS\", arg=1),\n        ]\n\n    @classmethod\n    def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n        cls,\n        code: types.CodeType,\n        lineno: int,\n        offset: int,\n        setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\n        *args: Any,\n    ) -> types.CodeType:\n        \"\"\"\n        This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\n        to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\n        from the original code object so that if control flow paths\n        converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\n        resume functions).\n        \"\"\"\n\n        meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\n            code\n        ]\n        new_offset = -1\n\n        def find_new_offset(\n            instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n        ) -> None:\n            nonlocal new_offset\n            (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n            # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\n            (new_target,) = (\n                i2\n                for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\n                if i1 is target\n            )\n            assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\n            assert new_target.offset is not None\n            new_offset = new_target.offset\n\n        transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\n        assert new_offset >= 0\n\n        if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n            # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\n            # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\n            # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\n            # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\n            if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\n                block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\n                    new_offset\n                ] = {}\n\n                def remap_block_offsets(\n                    instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n                ) -> None:\n                    # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\n                    # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\n                    # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\n                    # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\n                    # replaced.\n                    prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\n                    for inst in instructions:\n                        if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\n                            meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n                        ):\n                            break\n                        if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\":\n                            prefix_blocks.append(inst)\n\n                    # offsets into prefix\n                    for inst, o in zip(\n                        prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n                    ):\n                        block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\n\n                    # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\n                    old_start_offset = (\n                        cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\n                    )\n                    # offsets into old bytecode\n                    old_inst_offsets = sorted(\n                        n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\n                    )\n                    targets = _filter_iter(\n                        instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\n                    )\n                    new_targets = _filter_iter(\n                        zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\n                        targets,\n                        lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\n                    )\n                    for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\n                        block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\n\n                transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\n\n            # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\n            setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\n                meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\n                for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\n            )\n        return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\n            meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\n        )\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

        "+                            old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target",

75. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py",
  "limit": 60,
  "offset": 2438

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\n    def STORE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        speculation = self.speculate()\n        if speculation.failed(self):\n            return self.store_attr_graph_break(inst)\n        val, obj = self.popn(2)\n\n        if isinstance(obj, NNModuleVariable) and not isinstance(val, ConstantVariable):\n            # We don't allow side effects during export on non-constant values\n            # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1475\n            assert not self.export, (\n                f\"Mutating module attribute {inst.argval} during export.\"\n            )\n\n        try:\n            BuiltinVariable(setattr).call_function(\n                self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n                [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval), val],\n                {},\n            )\n            return\n        except Unsupported as e:\n            if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n                raise\n            log.debug(\"STORE_ATTR triggered compile\", exc_info=True)\n            e.remove_from_stats()\n            e.add_to_stats(\"graph_break\")\n        speculation.fail_and_restart_analysis(self.error_on_graph_break)\n\n    def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\")\n        if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\",\n                context=\"\",\n                explanation=\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \"\n                \"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n        all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n            self,\n            reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),\n            stack_pops=2,\n        )\n        self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\n        self.popn(2)\n        self.output.add_output_instructions(\n            self.create_call_resume_at(\n                self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n            )\n        )\n\n    def DELETE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        obj = self.pop()\n        BuiltinVariable(delattr).call_function(\n            self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval)],\n            {},\n        )\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 2438,
    "totalLines": 4673
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "originalFile": "\"\"\"\nCore module responsible for converting Python bytecode into TorchDynamo's symbolic execution format.\n\nThis module implements the bytecode-level tracing system that allows TorchDynamo to analyze\nand transform Python code. It converts Python bytecode instructions into a symbolic format\nthat tracks the flow of tensors and other values through the program.\n\nKey components:\n- InstructionTranslatorBase: Base class for converting bytecode to symbolic execution\n- InstructionTranslator: Main translator for function bytecode\n- InliningInstructionTranslator: Handles inlining of called functions\n- SpeculationLog: Manages state for speculative execution and rollback\n\nThe symbolic conversion process handles:\n- Control flow (loops, conditionals, etc.)\n- Function inlining and call stack management\n- Tracking of program values and side effects\n- Graph breaks and resumption points\n- Exception handling and stack frame management\n\nThis is a core part of TorchDynamo's tracing system that enables ahead-of-time\noptimization of PyTorch programs.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport collections\nimport collections.abc\nimport contextlib\nimport copy\nimport dataclasses\nimport dis\nimport functools\nimport importlib\nimport inspect\nimport itertools\nimport linecache\nimport logging\nimport operator\nimport re\nimport sys\nimport threading\nimport traceback\nimport types\nimport weakref\nfrom traceback import StackSummary\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, cast, NoReturn, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\nfrom typing_extensions import TypeAlias, TypeIs\nfrom unittest.mock import patch\n\nimport torch\nimport torch._logging\nfrom torch._dynamo.exc import ObservedException, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\nfrom torch._guards import tracing, TracingContext\nfrom torch._logging.structured import dump_file\nfrom torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import guard_bool\nfrom torch.utils._functools import cache_method\n\nfrom . import (\n    config,\n    exc,\n    graph_break_hints,\n    logging as torchdynamo_logging,\n    trace_rules,\n    variables,\n)\nfrom .bytecode_analysis import (\n    get_indexof,\n    JUMP_OPNAMES,\n    livevars_analysis,\n    propagate_line_nums,\n)\nfrom .bytecode_transformation import (\n    cleaned_instructions,\n    create_binary_slice,\n    create_call_function,\n    create_copy,\n    create_dup_top,\n    create_instruction,\n    create_jump_absolute,\n    create_rot_n,\n    create_swap,\n    get_code_keys,\n    Instruction,\n    is_generator,\n    is_jump_absolute,\n    unique_id,\n)\nfrom .code_context import code_context\nfrom .codegen import PyCodegen\nfrom .exc import (\n    ArgsMismatchError,\n    BackendCompilerFailed,\n    collapse_resume_frames,\n    format_graph_break_message,\n    get_stack_above_dynamo,\n    ResumePrologueTracingError,\n    unimplemented_v2,\n    Unsupported,\n)\nfrom .funcname_cache import get_funcname\nfrom .guards import GuardBuilder, install_guard\nfrom .output_graph import GraphCompileReason, OutputGraph\nfrom .polyfills import impl_CONTAINS_OP_fallback\nfrom .replay_record import DummyModule, ExecutionRecorder\nfrom .resume_execution import (\n    ContinueExecutionCache,\n    IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME,\n    ReenterWith,\n)\nfrom .source import (\n    AttrSource,\n    DictGetItemSource,\n    GlobalSource,\n    GlobalWeakRefSource,\n    LocalCellSource,\n    LocalSource,\n    SkipGuardSource,\n    Source,\n)\nfrom .trace_rules import is_builtin_constant, is_forbidden\nfrom .utils import (\n    _get_error_on_graph_break,\n    counters,\n    get_fake_value,\n    get_instruction_source_311,\n    get_metrics_context,\n    graph_break_dup_warning_checker,\n    istype,\n    LazyString,\n    proxy_args_kwargs,\n)\nfrom .variables.base import typestr, ValueMutationNew, VariableTracker\nfrom .variables.builder import FrameStateSizeEntry, VariableBuilder, wrap_fx_proxy\nfrom .variables.builtin import BuiltinVariable\nfrom .variables.constant import ConstantVariable\nfrom .variables.ctx_manager import (\n    ContextWrappingVariable,\n    GenericContextWrappingVariable,\n    WithExitFunctionVariable,\n)\nfrom .variables.dicts import ConstDictVariable, SetVariable\nfrom .variables.functions import (\n    BaseUserFunctionVariable,\n    LocalGeneratorFunctionVariable,\n    LocalGeneratorObjectVariable,\n    NestedUserFunctionVariable,\n    SkipFunctionVariable,\n    UserFunctionVariable,\n    UserMethodVariable,\n)\nfrom .variables.iter import MAX_ITERATOR_LIMIT\nfrom .variables.lazy import LazyVariableTracker\nfrom .variables.lists import (\n    BaseListVariable,\n    IteratorVariable,\n    ListIteratorVariable,\n    ListVariable,\n    SliceVariable,\n    TupleVariable,\n)\nfrom .variables.misc import (\n    CellVariable,\n    ExceptionVariable,\n    GetAttrVariable,\n    NullVariable,\n    PythonModuleVariable,\n    UnknownVariable,\n)\nfrom .variables.nn_module import NNModuleVariable\nfrom .variables.tensor import supported_comparison_ops, SymNodeVariable, TensorVariable\nfrom .variables.torch_function import (\n    SymbolicTorchFunctionState,\n    TorchFunctionModeVariable,\n)\nfrom .variables.user_defined import (\n    RemovableHandleVariable,\n    UserDefinedClassVariable,\n    UserDefinedExceptionClassVariable,\n    UserDefinedExceptionObjectVariable,\n    UserDefinedObjectVariable,\n)\n\n\nif TYPE_CHECKING:\n    from collections.abc import Generator, Sequence\n\n    from torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensorMode\n\n    from .package import CompilePackage\n\nlog = logging.getLogger(__name__)\ngraph_break_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"graph_breaks\")\ntrace_call_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"trace_call\")\ntrace_source_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"trace_source\")\ntrace_bytecode_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"trace_bytecode\")\ntls = threading.local()\ncompare_op_handlers: dict[str, Any] = {\n    k: BuiltinVariable(v).call_function for k, v in supported_comparison_ops.items()\n}\nhandle_contains = BuiltinVariable(operator.contains).call_function\nhandle_not = BuiltinVariable(operator.not_).call_function\ncompare_op_handlers[\"in\"] = lambda tx, args, _: handle_contains(\n    tx, [*reversed(args)], {}\n)\ncompare_op_handlers[\"not in\"] = lambda tx, args, _: handle_not(\n    tx, [handle_contains(tx, [*reversed(args)], {})], {}\n)\n\nPT2_ISSUE_TRACKER_URL = \"https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/new?&labels=oncall%3A+pt2&projects=&template=pt2-bug-report.yml\"\n\nExceptionVals: TypeAlias = Union[\n    variables.ExceptionVariable,\n    UserDefinedExceptionClassVariable,\n    UserDefinedExceptionObjectVariable,\n]\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef _import_module(name: str) -> types.ModuleType:\n    \"\"\"\n    Import the named module and cache the result. importlib.import_module()\n    seems to do some filesystem checking to validate the name so not caching\n    this can be slow.\n    \"\"\"\n    return importlib.import_module(name)\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass SpeculationEntry:\n    filename: str\n    lineno: int\n    instruction_pointer: int\n    inst: Instruction  # for debugging only\n    _failed: bool = False\n    error_on_graph_break: Optional[bool] = None\n    reason: Optional[GraphCompileReason] = None\n\n    def fail_and_restart_analysis(self, error_on_graph_break: bool) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Start tracing of the current frame over again, and don't take this branch.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._failed = True\n        self.error_on_graph_break = error_on_graph_break\n        if self.reason is not None:\n            restart_reason = self.reason.reason\n        else:\n            restart_reason = \"Unknown fail_and_restart_analysis\"\n        raise exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis(restart_reason=restart_reason)\n\n    def failed(self, tx: InstructionTranslatorBase) -> bool:\n        if self._failed:\n            assert self.error_on_graph_break is not None\n            tx.error_on_graph_break = self.error_on_graph_break\n            return True\n        return False\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass SpeculationLog:\n    \"\"\"\n    SpeculationLog replaces the prior copy_graphstate/restore_graphstate\n    checkpointing.  Rather than saving/restoring state, we restart the\n    dynamo conversion process over from the beginning -- but when we\n    hit the start of the speculation that failed, we instead generate\n    a graph break.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    entries: list[SpeculationEntry] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\n    index: int = 0\n\n    def restart(self) -> None:\n        self.index = 0\n\n    def clear(self) -> None:\n        self.entries.clear()\n        self.index = 0\n\n    def next(\n        self, filename: str, lineno: int, instruction_pointer: int, inst: Instruction\n    ) -> SpeculationEntry:\n        \"\"\"\n        Lookup or create a SpeculationEntry() that is shared across\n        RestartAnalysis calls.  Args are used only for debug checks.\n        \"\"\"\n        if len(self.entries) == self.index:\n            self.entries.append(\n                SpeculationEntry(filename, lineno, instruction_pointer, inst)\n            )\n        entry = self.entries[self.index]\n        prev_entry_msg = \"\"\n        if self.index != 0:\n            prev_entry = self.entries[self.index - 1]\n            prev_entry_msg = (\n                f\"Previous instruction: {prev_entry.filename}:{prev_entry.lineno}\"\n                f\"({prev_entry.inst.opname} @ {prev_entry.instruction_pointer})\\n\"\n            )\n        if not (\n            entry.instruction_pointer == instruction_pointer\n            and entry.filename == filename\n            and entry.lineno == lineno\n        ):\n            raise SpeculationLogDivergence(\n                f\"\"\"\nSpeculationLog diverged at index {self.index} (log had {len(self.entries)} entries):\n- Expected: {entry.filename}:{entry.lineno} ({entry.inst.opname} at ip={entry.instruction_pointer})\n- Actual: {filename}:{lineno} ({inst.opname} at ip={instruction_pointer})\n{prev_entry_msg}\nThere are two usual reasons why this may have occurred:\n- When Dynamo analysis restarted, the second run took a different path than\n  the first.  If this occurred, the previous instruction is the critical instruction that\n  behaved differently.\n- Speculation entries are only added under certain conditions (as seen in\n  step()), e.g., there must exist operators in the graph; those conditions may\n  have changed on restart.\n\nIf this divergence was intentional, clear the speculation log before restarting (do NOT\ndo this for graph breaks, you will infinite loop).\n\nOtherwise, please submit a bug report, ideally including the contents of TORCH_LOGS=+dynamo\n\"\"\"\n            )\n        self.index += 1\n        return entry\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass LocalState:\n    automatic_dynamic: dict[str, FrameStateSizeEntry] = dataclasses.field(\n        default_factory=dict\n    )\n\n    def render(self) -> str:\n        return \"\\n\".join(\n            f\"{k}: {v.render()}\" for k, v in self.automatic_dynamic.items()\n        )\n\n\n# Mutable box that is shared across restarts\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass DistributedState:\n    compile_pg: Any\n    local_state: LocalState\n    all_states: Optional[list[LocalState]] = None\n\n\nclass TensorifyState:\n    # These are the set of string symfloats names (eg. \"zf0\") that we collect\n    # from the tensorify_python_scalars.py joint fx pass to inform us about\n    # which float inputs we should specialize when we restart analysis.\n    force_specializations: set[str] = set()\n\n    @classmethod\n    def specialize(cls, index: str) -> None:\n        cls.force_specializations.add(index)\n\n    @classmethod\n    def should_specialize(cls, index: str) -> bool:\n        return index in cls.force_specializations\n\n    @classmethod\n    def clear(cls) -> None:\n        cls.force_specializations.clear()\n\n    @classmethod\n    def empty(cls) -> bool:\n        return len(cls.force_specializations) == 0\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef _step_logger() -> Callable[..., None]:\n    return torchdynamo_logging.get_step_logger(log)\n\n\n@contextlib.contextmanager\ndef save_and_restart_speculation_log(\n    tx: InstructionTranslatorBase,\n) -> Generator[None, None, None]:\n    # When reconstructing a generator after a graph break, we advance it until\n    # it is fully exhausted. This process adds new entries to the speculation\n    # log that were not previously observed. Without temporarily clearing the\n    # speculation log, this could lead to a divergence error.\n\n    entries = tx.speculation_log.entries\n    index = tx.speculation_log.index\n    try:\n        tx.speculation_log.entries = []\n        tx.speculation_log.index = 0\n        yield\n    finally:\n        tx.speculation_log.entries = entries\n        tx.speculation_log.index = index\n\n\n@contextlib.contextmanager\ndef temporarely_allow_writes_to_output_graph(\n    tx: InstructionTranslatorBase,\n) -> Generator[None, None, None]:\n    try:\n        tmp = tx.output.should_exit\n        tx.output.should_exit = False\n        yield\n    finally:\n        tx.output.should_exit = tmp\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass BlockStackEntry:\n    # Current instruction that pushes something to block_stack\n    inst: Instruction\n    target: Instruction\n    stack_index: int\n    with_context: Optional[\n        Union[ContextWrappingVariable, GenericContextWrappingVariable]\n    ] = None\n\n    def can_restore(self) -> bool:\n        return self.with_context is not None\n\n    def resume_fn(self) -> ReenterWith:\n        assert self.stack_index is not None\n        if (\n            self.with_context\n            and hasattr(self.with_context, \"target_values\")\n            and self.with_context.target_values\n        ):\n            return ReenterWith(\n                self.stack_index - 1, tuple(self.with_context.target_values)\n            )\n        else:\n            return ReenterWith(self.stack_index - 1)\n\n    def exit(self, tx: InstructionTranslatorBase, is_graph_break: bool) -> None:\n        assert self.with_context is not None\n        if (\n            is_graph_break and self.with_context.exit_on_graph_break()\n        ) or not is_graph_break:\n            return self.with_context.exit(tx)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n\nclass SpeculationLogDivergence(AssertionError):\n    pass\n\n\nclass ReturnValueOp(Exception):\n    pass\n\n\nclass YieldValueOp(Exception):\n    \"\"\"\n    Signal to the symbolic tracer to stop and return control flow to the\n    caller\n    \"\"\"\n\n\ndef stack_op(fn: Callable[..., object]) -> Callable[..., Any]:\n    nargs = len(inspect.signature(fn).parameters)\n    fn_var = BuiltinVariable(fn)\n\n    @functools.wraps(fn)\n    def impl(self: InstructionTranslator, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.push(fn_var.call_function(self, self.popn(nargs), {}))\n\n    return impl\n\n\ndef is_stdlib(mod: object) -> bool:\n    if sys.version_info < (3, 10):\n        # For < 3.10, no easy way to identify a stdlib module name.\n        return False\n    if not isinstance(mod, types.ModuleType):\n        return False\n    return mod.__name__.split(\".\")[0] in sys.stdlib_module_names\n\n\ndef _detect_and_normalize_assert_statement(\n    self: InstructionTranslatorBase,\n    truth_fn: Callable[[object], bool],\n    push: bool,\n) -> bool:\n    # Detect if this jump instruction is assert and normalize the assert\n    # by pushing dummy error message when nothing is given.\n    #\n    # Python 3.9 assertion is in following format:\n    # 18 POP_JUMP_IF_TRUE       28\n    # 20 LOAD_ASSERTION_ERROR\n    # 22 LOAD_CONST               3 ('Assert message') -> optional instruction\n    # 24 CALL_FUNCTION            1                    -> optional instruction\n    # 26 RAISE_VARARGS\n    #\n    # Python 3.8 assertion is in following format:\n    # 18 POP_JUMP_IF_TRUE       28\n    # 20 LOAD_GLOBAL              0 (Assertion type)\n    # 22 LOAD_CONST               3 ('Assert message') -> optional instruction\n    # 24 CALL_FUNCTION            1                    -> optional instruction\n    # 26 RAISE_VARARGS            1\n\n    if (truth_fn is not operator.truth) or push:\n        return False\n\n    assert isinstance(self.instruction_pointer, int)\n    current_instruction_pointer = self.instruction_pointer\n    inst = self.instructions[current_instruction_pointer]\n    # Detect LOAD_ASSERTION_ERROR or LOAD_GLOBAL 0\n    if inst.opname != \"LOAD_ASSERTION_ERROR\":\n        return False\n\n    current_instruction_pointer += 1\n\n    # Use dummy error message if its hard to extract\n    error_msg = \"assertion error\"\n\n    inst = self.instructions[current_instruction_pointer]\n    # DETECT RAISE_VARARGS or LOAD CONST\n    if inst.opname == \"LOAD_CONST\":\n        if not isinstance(inst.argval, str):\n            return False\n        error_msg = inst.argval\n\n        # if it is LOAD_CONSTANT, it must be followed by CALL_FUNCTION\n        # (PRECALL for Python 3.11, CALL for Python 3.12+)\n        current_instruction_pointer += 1\n        inst = self.instructions[current_instruction_pointer]\n        if inst.opname not in (\"CALL_FUNCTION\", \"PRECALL\", \"CALL\"):\n            return False\n\n        # for Python 3.11, PRECALL should be followed by CALL, then RAISE_VARARGS\n        # for Python != 3.11, CALL_FUNCTION/CALL should be followed by RAISE_VARARGS\n        current_instruction_pointer += 1\n        if inst.opname == \"PRECALL\":\n            current_instruction_pointer += 1\n        inst = self.instructions[current_instruction_pointer]\n\n    if inst.opname != \"RAISE_VARARGS\":\n        return False\n\n    self.push(ConstantVariable.create(error_msg))\n\n    return True\n\n\nexplain = False\n\n\ndef log_graph_break(\n    code_options: dict[str, Any],\n    reason: str = \"\",\n    exc_info: bool = False,\n    user_stack: Optional[StackSummary] = None,\n) -> None:\n    if user_stack is None:\n        user_stack = torch._guards.TracingContext.extract_stack()\n\n    try:\n        frame_loc = (user_stack[-1].filename, user_stack[-1].lineno)\n    except IndexError:\n        # first instruction\n        frame_loc = (\n            code_options[\"co_filename\"],\n            code_options[\"co_firstlineno\"],\n        )\n\n    stack_above_dynamo_formatted = \"\"\n    if config.verbose:\n        stack_above_dynamo = get_stack_above_dynamo()\n        stack_above_dynamo_formatted = \"\".join(\n            traceback.format_list(stack_above_dynamo)\n        )\n    else:\n        user_stack = get_stack_above_dynamo() + user_stack  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        user_stack = collapse_resume_frames(user_stack)\n    user_stack_formatted = \"\".join(traceback.format_list(user_stack))\n    user_stack_trace = (\n        f\"Graph break in user code at {frame_loc[0]}:{frame_loc[1]}\\n\"\n        f\"Graph Break Reason: {reason}\\n\"\n        \"User code traceback:\\n\"\n    )\n\n    if config.verbose:\n        user_stack_trace += (\n            f\"{stack_above_dynamo_formatted}\\n\"\n            \"========== most recent `torch.compile` tracing attempt started here ==========\\n\\n\"\n            f\"{user_stack_formatted}\\n\"\n            \"NOTE: the most recent `torch.compile` tracing attempt might not be where you applied `torch.compile`! \"\n            \"This is due to how graph breaks are implemented - the optimized code object returned by Dynamo will call another \"\n            \"Dynamo-generated resume function and tracing is re-enabled by calling the resume function as a normal Python \"\n            \"function, which Dynamo intercepts as a top-level frame.\\n\"\n        )\n    else:\n        user_stack_trace += str(user_stack_formatted)\n\n    torch._logging.trace_structured(\n        \"artifact\",\n        metadata_fn=lambda: {\n            \"name\": \"dynamo_graph_break_reason\",\n            \"encoding\": \"string\",\n        },\n        payload_fn=lambda: f\"{user_stack_trace}\\n{traceback.format_exc() if exc_info else ''}\",\n    )\n\n    # torch._dynamo.explain() formats this a little nicer, and presents a slightly\n    # more actionable user code pointer\n    if (\n        graph_break_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG)\n        and not explain\n        and graph_break_dup_warning_checker.add(frame_loc)\n    ):\n        # This log line MUST contain the string \"Graph break in user code\",\n        # This log line is exercised from\n        #   python test/dynamo/test_exc.py -k test_graph_break_log\n        graph_break_log.debug(\n            user_stack_trace,\n        )\n    else:\n        # This log line MUST not contain the string \"Graph break in user code\",\n        # exercised by\n        #   python test/dynamo/test_misc.py -k test_duplicate_graph_break_log\n        graph_break_log.debug(\n            \"Graph break (user stack suppressed due to duplicate graph break) in user code at %s:%s\\nGraph Break Reason: %s\",\n            frame_loc[0],\n            frame_loc[1],\n            reason,\n        )\n\n\ndef generic_jump(\n    truth_fn: Callable[[object], bool], push: bool\n) -> Callable[[InstructionTranslatorBase, Instruction], None]:\n    # graph break message fields for data dependent branching\n    _gb_type = \"Data-dependent branching\"\n    _explanation = (\n        \"Detected data-dependent branching (e.g. `if my_tensor.sum() > 0:`). \"\n        \"Dynamo does not support tracing dynamic control flow.\"\n    )\n    _hints = [\n        *graph_break_hints.FUNDAMENTAL,\n        \"Use `torch.cond` to express dynamic control flow.\",\n    ]\n\n    def jump_graph_break(\n        self: InstructionTranslatorBase,\n        inst: Instruction,\n        value: VariableTracker,\n        extra_msg: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        log_graph_break(\n            self.code_options,\n            reason=format_graph_break_message(\n                gb_type=_gb_type,\n                context=f\"attempted to jump with {value}\",\n                explanation=_explanation,\n                hints=_hints,\n            ),\n        )\n        assert self.should_compile_partial_graph()\n        # compile a partial subgraph prefix then jump into user code\n        if self.maybe_has_backedge():\n            msg = (\n                \"Skipping frame because there is a graph break in a for/while loop\\n\"\n                f\"{self.frame_summary()}\"\n            )\n            log.info(msg)\n            raise exc.SkipFrame(msg)\n\n        self.push(value)\n        log.debug(\"generic_jump triggered compile\")\n        all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n            self,\n            reason=GraphCompileReason(\n                f\"generic_jump {typestr(value)}{extra_msg}\", [self.frame_summary()]\n            ),\n            stack_pops=1,\n        )\n        self.pop()\n\n        if_next = self.create_call_resume_at(\n            self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n        )\n        if push:\n            self.push(value)\n        assert inst.target is not None\n        if_jump = self.create_call_resume_at(\n            inst.target, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n        )\n\n        if sys.version_info >= (3, 13):\n            # 3.13 requires stack[-1] to be bool type\n            self.output.add_output_instructions([create_instruction(\"TO_BOOL\")])\n\n        jump_inst = create_instruction(inst.opname, target=if_jump[0])\n        jump_inst.copy_positions(inst)\n        self.output.add_output_instructions([jump_inst] + if_next + if_jump)\n\n    def inner(self: InstructionTranslatorBase, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        value: VariableTracker = self.pop()\n        if (\n            config.rewrite_assert_with_torch_assert\n            and _detect_and_normalize_assert_statement(self, truth_fn, push)\n        ):\n            error_msg: VariableTracker = self.pop()\n            # Skip over things like `assert True`\n            if value.is_python_constant():\n                if bool(value.as_python_constant()):\n                    return self.jump(inst)\n                elif self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n                    jump_graph_break(self, inst, value)\n                else:\n                    unimplemented_v2(\n                        gb_type=\"Data-dependent assertion failed (cannot compile partial graph)\",\n                        context=f\"value: {value}\",\n                        explanation=\"Dynamo has determined when encountering a data-dependent assert failure \"\n                        \"that it should not compile the partial graph.\",\n                        hints=[\n                            *graph_break_hints.FUNDAMENTAL,\n                            \"Use `torch._assert()` to raise a hard AssertionError when the check fails. \"\n                            \"This error will propagate back the user code \"\n                            \"that called the compiled function (i.e. Dynamo will not trace any exception handling).\",\n                            \"Remove the assert statement.\",\n                            \"Move the assert statement outside of any context managers in order to graph break with \"\n                            \"partial graph compilation (if fullgraph=False).\",\n                        ],\n                    )\n\n            # TODO maybe should respect DtoH sync intention of users later??\n            # Manually insert torch._assert_async instead of python assert and jump over\n            # assert related instructions as we don't need them anymore.\n\n            # if we see Tensor as assert statement, no need to call scalar_tensor\n            if isinstance(value, TensorVariable):\n                self.output.create_proxy(\n                    \"call_function\",\n                    torch._assert_async,\n                    *proxy_args_kwargs((value, error_msg), {}),\n                )\n                self.jump(inst)\n                return\n\n            if isinstance(value, SymNodeVariable):\n                # if the assertion is normal shape expression.\n                # just install guard and bail out.\n                sym_expr = value.sym_num\n                if not isinstance(sym_expr, torch.SymBool):\n                    sym_expr = sym_expr != 0\n\n                result = torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes.expect_true(sym_expr)\n                if not result:\n                    unimplemented_v2(\n                        gb_type=\"Assertion failed on symbolic shapes\",\n                        context=str(sym_expr),\n                        explanation=\"\",\n                        hints=[*graph_break_hints.USER_ERROR],\n                    )\n                self.jump(inst)\n                return\n\n            scalar_to_tensor_proxy = self.output.create_proxy(\n                \"call_function\", torch.scalar_tensor, *proxy_args_kwargs((value,), {})\n            )\n\n            scalar_to_tensor = wrap_fx_proxy(\n                self,\n                scalar_to_tensor_proxy,\n                example_value=get_fake_value(scalar_to_tensor_proxy.node, self),\n            )\n\n            self.output.create_proxy(\n                \"call_function\",\n                torch._assert_async,\n                *proxy_args_kwargs((scalar_to_tensor, error_msg), {}),\n            )\n            self.jump(inst)\n            return\n\n        if value.is_python_constant():\n            # ConstDictVariable is optimized to be very lazy about insertion of\n            # guards, so we have to manually insert a SEQUENCE_LENGTH guard\n            # here.\n            if isinstance(value, ConstDictVariable) and value.source:\n                install_guard(value.source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.SEQUENCE_LENGTH))\n            if truth_fn(value.as_python_constant()):\n                if push:\n                    self.push(value)\n                self.jump(inst)\n        elif (\n            isinstance(value, (TensorVariable)) and self.should_compile_partial_graph()\n        ):\n            jump_graph_break(self, inst, value)\n        elif isinstance(value, NNModuleVariable):\n            # Equivalent of \"self.nn_module is not None\"\n            mod = self.output.get_submodule(value.module_key)\n            if truth_fn(mod):\n                if push:\n                    self.push(value)\n                self.jump(inst)\n        elif isinstance(value, UserDefinedObjectVariable):\n            try:\n                x = value.var_getattr(self, \"__bool__\")  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            except exc.ObservedAttributeError:\n                exc.handle_observed_exception(self)\n                # if __bool__ is missing, trying __len__ to infer a truth value.\n                try:\n                    x = value.var_getattr(self, \"__len__\")  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n                except exc.ObservedAttributeError:\n                    exc.handle_observed_exception(self)\n                    x = None\n\n            # __bool__ or __len__ is function\n            if isinstance(x, UserMethodVariable):\n                result = x.call_function(self, [], {})  # type: ignore[arg-type, assignment]\n                if isinstance(result, ConstantVariable) and isinstance(\n                    result.value, (bool, int)\n                ):\n                    if truth_fn(result.value):\n                        if push:\n                            self.push(value)\n                        self.jump(inst)\n                elif isinstance(result, SymNodeVariable):\n                    if result.evaluate_expr():\n                        if push:\n                            self.push(value)\n                        self.jump(inst)\n                else:\n                    unimplemented_v2(\n                        gb_type=\"Data-dependent branching with non-constant __bool__\",\n                        context=f\"method: {x}, result: {result}\",\n                        explanation=\"Attempted to perform data-dependent branching on a user-defined \"\n                        \"object with a __bool__ method that did not return a constant.\",\n                        hints=[],\n                    )\n            # __bool__ or __len__ is non-function or not existed in the user defined object\n            else:\n                if truth_fn(True):\n                    if push:\n                        self.push(value)\n                    self.jump(inst)\n        elif not isinstance(value, TensorVariable) and value.has_unpack_var_sequence(\n            self\n        ):\n            if truth_fn(len(value.unpack_var_sequence(self))):\n                if push:\n                    self.push(value)\n                self.jump(inst)\n        elif isinstance(value, SymNodeVariable):\n            try:\n                # if the user is branching on a SymBool, guard on it\n                # if the user has code like:\n                #    if size:\n                #        ...\n                # then they are just testing truthiness: guard that the expr != 0\n                if isinstance(value.sym_num, torch.SymBool):\n                    eval_result = value.evaluate_expr(self.output)\n                else:\n                    eval_result = guard_bool(value.sym_num != 0)\n            except exc.UserError as e:\n                if self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n                    return jump_graph_break(self, inst, value, extra_msg=f\"\\n{e}\")\n                raise\n            if truth_fn(eval_result):\n                if push:\n                    self.push(value)\n                self.jump(inst)\n        elif isinstance(value, variables.BackwardHookVariable):\n            if truth_fn(True):\n                if push:\n                    self.push(value)\n                self.jump(inst)\n        else:\n            from .source import is_constant_source\n\n            if value.source is not None and is_constant_source(value.source):\n                if truth_fn(value.get_real_value()):  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                    if push:\n                        self.push(value)\n                    self.jump(inst)\n            else:\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"Data-dependent branching\",\n                    context=f\"attempted to jump with {value}\",\n                    explanation=_explanation,\n                    hints=[\n                        *graph_break_hints.FUNDAMENTAL,\n                        \"Use `torch.cond` to express dynamic control flow.\",\n                    ],\n                )\n\n    return inner\n\n\ndef break_graph_if_unsupported(\n    *, push: int\n) -> Callable[\n    [Callable[..., None]], Callable[[InstructionTranslatorBase, Instruction], None]\n]:\n    def decorator(\n        inner_fn: Callable[..., None],\n    ) -> Callable[[InstructionTranslatorBase, Instruction], None]:\n        @functools.wraps(inner_fn)\n        def wrapper(self: InstructionTranslatorBase, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n            speculation = self.speculate()\n            if speculation.failed(self):\n                assert speculation.reason is not None\n                return handle_graph_break(self, inst, speculation.reason)\n            try:\n                return inner_fn(self, inst)\n            except Unsupported as excp:\n                if self.active_generic_context_managers:\n                    # We don't support graph break under GenericContextWrappingVariable,\n                    # If there is, we roll back to the checkpoint and fall back.\n                    excp.remove_from_stats()\n                    unimplemented_v2(\n                        gb_type=\"Graph break under GenericContextWrappingVariable\",\n                        context=f\"Active generic context managers: {self.active_generic_context_managers}\",\n                        explanation=\"Attempted to graph break in an active context manager(s) that doesn't support graph breaking.\",\n                        hints=[\n                            \"Move the offending context manager(s) to outside the compiled region.\",\n                            *graph_break_hints.CAUSED_BY_EARLIER_GRAPH_BREAK,\n                        ],\n                        from_exc=excp,\n                    )\n\n                if isinstance(excp, exc.UncapturedHigherOrderOpError):\n                    raise\n\n                if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n                    raise\n\n                log_graph_break(\n                    self.code_options,\n                    exc_info=True,\n                    reason=str(excp),\n                    user_stack=excp.real_stack,\n                )\n\n                if self.maybe_has_backedge():\n                    msg = (\n                        \"Skipping frame because there is a graph break in a for/while loop\\n\"\n                        f\"{self.frame_summary()}\"\n                    )\n                    log.info(msg)\n                    raise exc.SkipFrame(msg) from excp\n\n                excp.remove_from_stats()\n                excp.add_to_stats(\"graph_break\")\n                speculation.reason = GraphCompileReason(excp.msg, excp.real_stack)\n            speculation.fail_and_restart_analysis(self.error_on_graph_break)\n\n        def handle_graph_break(\n            self: InstructionTranslatorBase,\n            inst: Instruction,\n            reason: GraphCompileReason,\n        ) -> None:\n            if (\n                sys.version_info >= (3, 11)\n                and sys.version_info < (3, 12)\n                and inst.opname == \"CALL\"\n            ):\n                # stack effect for PRECALL + CALL is split between the two instructions\n                stack_effect = dis.stack_effect(\n                    dis.opmap[\"PRECALL\"], inst.arg\n                ) + dis.stack_effect(dis.opmap[\"CALL\"], inst.arg)\n            else:\n                stack_effect = dis.stack_effect(inst.opcode, inst.arg)\n\n            all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n                self, reason=reason, stack_pops=push - stack_effect\n            )\n            cg = PyCodegen(self)\n            cleanup: list[Instruction] = []\n            # Reconstruct the context variable CLASS in the block stack\n            for b in self.block_stack:\n                # Don't exit any modes we have entered,\n                # output bytecode will mutate the tf mode stack accordingly\n                if isinstance(b.with_context, TorchFunctionModeVariable):\n                    cg.extend_output(\n                        b.resume_fn().try_except_torch_function_mode(\n                            cg.code_options, cleanup\n                        )\n                    )\n                    continue\n                assert b.with_context is not None\n                assert isinstance(b.with_context, (ContextWrappingVariable))\n                b.with_context.reconstruct_type(cg)\n                cg.extend_output(b.resume_fn().try_finally(cg.code_options, cleanup))\n            self.output.add_output_instructions(cg.get_instructions())\n            del cg\n\n            if sys.version_info >= (3, 11) and inst.opname == \"CALL\":\n                kw_names = (\n                    self.kw_names.as_python_constant()\n                    if self.kw_names is not None\n                    else ()\n                )\n                if len(kw_names) > 0:\n                    # KW_NAMES no longer used in 3.13\n                    assert sys.version_info < (3, 13)\n                    self.output.add_output_instructions(\n                        [create_instruction(\"KW_NAMES\", argval=kw_names)]\n                    )\n                assert inst.arg is not None\n                call_insts = create_call_function(inst.arg, False)\n                call_insts[-1].copy_positions(inst)\n                self.output.add_output_instructions(call_insts)\n            else:\n                # copy instruction, but without exception table data\n                assert inst.target is None\n                inst_copy = copy.copy(inst)\n                inst_copy.exn_tab_entry = None\n                self.output.add_output_instructions([inst_copy])\n\n            self.output.add_output_instructions(cleanup)\n\n            self.popn(push - stack_effect)\n            for _ in range(push):\n                self.push(UnknownVariable())\n            self.output.add_output_instructions(\n                self.create_call_resume_at(\n                    self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n                )\n            )\n\n        return wrapper\n\n    return decorator\n\n\nclass BytecodeDistpatchTableMeta(type):\n    \"\"\"Installs a `cls.dispatch_table` on every subclass to speed up calls to self.OPCODE()\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(cls: type, name: str, bases: Any, dct: Any) -> None:\n        super().__init__(name, bases, dct)  # type: ignore[misc]\n\n        def _missing(opname: str, *args: Any) -> None:\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Missing bytecode handler\",\n                context=f\"{opname} with args {args}\",\n                explanation=f\"Dynamo does not know how to handle the bytecode instruction `{opname}`.\",\n                hints=[\n                    f\"Do not trace code that produces the `{opname}` bytecode instruction \"\n                    \"(see https://docs.python.org/3/library/dis.html for bytecode semantics).\",\n                    *graph_break_hints.SUPPORTABLE,\n                ],\n            )\n\n        dispatch_table = {\n            op: getattr(cls, opname, functools.partial(_missing, opname))\n            for opname, op in dis.opmap.items()\n        }\n        cls.dispatch_table = [dispatch_table.get(i) for i in range(2**8)]\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass ExceptionStack:\n    \"\"\"\n    Exception stack that it is shared among all InstructionTranslator instances\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # Exception handling in CPython is a bit confusing and some of the bytecode\n    # have a slightly different behavior than what is is documented. While reading\n    # the documentation, is important to notice that the terms \"current exception\"\n    # and \"stack\" sometimes refers to a C variable with the same name and the\n    # exception stack, respectively.\n    #\n    # The lifetime of an exception is (Python 3.11+):\n    #  + tx._raise_exception_variable(...) := sets the current_exception variable\n    #  + PUSH_EXC_INFO := pushes the current_exception to the *exception stack*\n    #  + POP_EXCEPT := pops TOS from the *exception stack*\n\n    _exc_stack: list[ExceptionVals] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\n    _current_exception: Optional[ExceptionVals] = dataclasses.field(default=None)\n\n    def clear_current_exception(self) -> None:\n        self._current_exception = None\n\n    def set_current_exception(self, val: ExceptionVals) -> None:\n        self._set_context_and_break_context_reference_cycle(val)\n        self._current_exception = val\n\n    def move_current_exception_to_stack(self) -> None:\n        assert self._current_exception is not None\n        self.append(self._current_exception)\n        self.clear_current_exception()\n\n    def get_current_exception(self) -> ExceptionVals:\n        assert self._current_exception is not None\n        return self._current_exception\n\n    def _set_context_recursive(\n        self, val: ExceptionVals, prev_idx: int\n    ) -> ExceptionVals:\n        if (ctx := val.__context__) and type(ctx) is not ConstantVariable:  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n            return val\n        if len(self._exc_stack) + prev_idx > 0:\n            prev = self._exc_stack[prev_idx]\n            self._set_context_recursive(prev, prev_idx - 1)\n            val.set_context(prev)  # type: ignore[union-attr, arg-type]\n        return val\n\n    def _break_context_reference_cycle(self, val: ExceptionVals) -> None:\n        # See test_exceptions::test_raise_does_not_create_context_chain_cycle\n        # Based on https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/e635bf2e49797ecb976ce45a67fce2201a25ca68/Python/errors.c#L207-L228\n        # As noted on CPython, this is O(chain length) but the context chains\n        # are usually very small\n        o = slow_o = val\n        slow_update_toggle = False  # floyd's algorithm for detecting cycle\n        while True:\n            context = o.__context__  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n            if type(context) is ConstantVariable:  # context not set\n                break\n\n            if context is val:\n                o.set_context(ConstantVariable(None))  # type: ignore[union-attr, arg-type]\n                break\n\n            o = context  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            if o is slow_o:\n                # pre-existing cycle - all exceptions on the path were\n                # visited and checked\n                break\n\n            if slow_update_toggle:\n                # visited all exceptions\n                slow_o = slow_o.__context__  # type: ignore[union-attr, assignment]\n            slow_update_toggle = not slow_update_toggle\n\n    def _set_context_and_break_context_reference_cycle(\n        self, val: ExceptionVals\n    ) -> None:\n        # set Exception.__context__\n        self._set_context_recursive(val, len(self._exc_stack) - 1)\n        self._break_context_reference_cycle(val)\n\n    def pop(self) -> ExceptionVals:\n        return self._exc_stack.pop()\n\n    def append(self, val: ExceptionVals) -> None:\n        self._exc_stack.append(val)\n\n    def __len__(self) -> int:\n        return len(self._exc_stack)\n\n    def __getitem__(self, index: int) -> ExceptionVals:\n        return self._exc_stack[index]\n\n    def __str__(self) -> str:\n        return f\"{self._exc_stack=} -
