k(x):\n            return x\n\n        def g(x):\n            return k(x)\n\n        def f(x):\n            return g(x)\n\n        # TODO clear this on all tests\n        torch._dynamo.eval_frame.clear_dynamo_tls()\n\n        opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=fullgraph, dynamic=False)\n        opt_f(torch.randn(3))\n        # we should not be compiling g or h as top-level functions\n        self.assertEqual(len(torch._dynamo.eval_frame.dynamo_tls.traced_frame_infos), 1)\n        # no recompilation\n        opt_f(torch.randn(3))\n        self.assertEqual(len(torch._dynamo.eval_frame.dynamo_tls.traced_frame_infos), 1)\n        # recompilation\n        opt_f(torch.randn(4))\n        self.assertEqual(len(torch._dynamo.eval_frame.dynamo_tls.traced_frame_infos), 2)\n\n    def test_torchname(self):\n        def fn(obj):\n            return torch.typename(obj)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        self.assertEqual(fn(typing.Any), opt_fn(typing.Any))\n\n    @unittest.skipIf(not TEST_CUDA, \"test requires CUDA\")\n    @unittest.skipIf(not dist.is_available(), \"test requires distributed\")\n    # TODO: Remoe this skip once nccl issue if fixed\n    @unittest.skip(\n        \"Failing with ncc update 2.25.1 : https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/147141\"\n    )\n    def test_ddp_checkpoint(self):\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144035\n        DIM = 256\n        SEQ_LEN = 32\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def mlp_forward(x, w1, w2, b1, b2):\n            y = F.linear(x, w1, b1)\n            y = F.relu(y)\n            y = F.linear(y, w2, b2)\n            return y\n\n        class MLP(nn.Module):\n            def __init__(\n                self,\n                in_features: int,\n                hidden_features: int,\n                out_features: int,\n            ):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.w_in = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(hidden_features, in_features))\n                self.w_out = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(out_features, hidden_features))\n                self.b_in = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(hidden_features))\n                self.b_out = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(out_features))\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                result = torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint(\n                    mlp_forward,\n                    x,\n                    self.w_in,\n                    self.w_out,\n                    self.b_in,\n                    self.b_out,\n                    use_reentrant=False,\n                )\n                assert isinstance(result, torch.Tensor)\n                return result\n\n        x = torch.randn(100, SEQ_LEN, DIM)\n        y = torch.zeros(100)\n        dataset = torch.utils.data.TensorDataset(x, y)\n        dataloader = torch.utils.data.DataLoader(dataset, batch_size=10)\n        model = MLP(DIM, 4 * DIM, DIM)\n\n        try:\n            # required for DDP wrapper initialization\n            prior_master_addr = os.environ.get(\"MASTER_ADDR\", None)\n            prior_master_port = os.environ.get(\"MASTER_PORT\", None)\n            os.environ[\"MASTER_ADDR\"] = \"localhost\"\n            os.environ[\"MASTER_PORT\"] = \"12355\"\n            dist.init_process_group(backend=\"nccl\", world_size=1, rank=0)\n            model = model.to(\"cuda\")\n            model = nn.parallel.DistributedDataParallel(model)\n\n            for batch in dataloader:\n                x, y = batch\n                x = x.to(\"cuda\")\n                output = model(x)\n                loss = output.sum()\n                loss.backward()\n        finally:\n            dist.destroy_process_group()\n            if prior_master_addr:\n                os.environ[\"MASTER_ADDR\"] = prior_master_addr\n            else:\n                del os.environ[\"MASTER_ADDR\"]\n\n            if prior_master_port:\n                os.environ[\"MASTER_PORT\"] = prior_master_port\n            else:\n                del os.environ[\"MASTER_PORT\"]\n\n    @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\n        recompile_limit=1,\n        fail_on_recompile_limit_hit=True,\n    )\n    def test_compilation_metrics_on_error(self):\n        torch._dynamo.utils.clear_compilation_metrics()\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def fn(x):\n            # force a recompile in a way friendly to test_dynamic_shapes\n            if x.numel() == 100:\n                return x.sum()\n            elif x.numel() == 10000:\n                return x.sum()\n\n        x = torch.randn(10, 10)\n        y = torch.randn(100, 100)\n        metrics = torch._dynamo.utils._compilation_metrics\n        self.assertEqual(len(metrics), 0)\n\n        fn(x)\n        self.assertTrue(metrics is torch._dynamo.utils._compilation_metrics)\n        self.assertEqual(len(metrics), 1)\n        latest_metrics = metrics[-1]\n        self.assertTrue(latest_metrics.dynamo_config is not None)\n        self.assertTrue(latest_metrics.recompile_reason is None)\n\n        with self.assertRaises(torch._dynamo.exc.FailOnRecompileLimitHit):\n            fn(y)\n        self.assertTrue(metrics is torch._dynamo.utils._compilation_metrics)\n        self.assertEqual(len(metrics), 2)\n        latest_metrics = metrics[-1]\n        self.assertTrue(latest_metrics.dynamo_config is not None)\n        self.assertTrue(latest_metrics.recompile_reason is not None)\n\n        torch._dynamo.utils.clear_compilation_metrics()\n\n    # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/156580\n    @serialTest()\n    def test_dont_dce_rand(self):\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/143431\n        def f(image_latent):\n            B = 2\n            num_ref = 3\n            num_tar = 3\n            x = torch.rand(B, 12)\n            indices = torch.argsort(torch.rand(*x.shape), dim=-1)[\n                :, : num_ref + num_tar\n            ]\n            return image_latent[torch.arange(B).unsqueeze(-1), indices][:, :num_ref]\n\n        torch.manual_seed(54321)\n        torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(54321)\n        expected = f(torch.randn((2, 12, 16, 32, 32))).sum()\n\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/147171\n        torch._inductor.config.fallback_random = True\n\n        for backend in [\"eager\", \"aot_eager\"]:\n            torch.manual_seed(54321)\n            torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(54321)\n            actual = torch.compile(backend=backend, fullgraph=True)(f)(\n                torch.randn((2, 12, 16, 32, 32))\n            ).sum()\n            self.assertEqual(actual, expected)\n\n    def test_incompatible_configs(self):\n        with torch._dynamo.config.patch(\n            suppress_errors=False, fail_on_recompile_limit_hit=False\n        ):\n            torch.compile(lambda: None)\n\n        with torch._dynamo.config.patch(\n            suppress_errors=True, fail_on_recompile_limit_hit=False\n        ):\n            torch.compile(lambda: None)\n\n        with torch._dynamo.config.patch(\n            suppress_errors=False, fail_on_recompile_limit_hit=True\n        ):\n            torch.compile(lambda: None)\n\n        with (\n            torch._dynamo.config.patch(\n                suppress_errors=True, fail_on_recompile_limit_hit=True\n            ),\n            self.assertRaises(AssertionError),\n        ):\n            torch.compile(lambda: None)\n\n    def test_str_isalnum(self):\n        def f(x, c):\n            str.isalnum(c)\n            return x.sin()\n\n        opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        x = torch.randn(3)\n        c = \"foobar\"\n        self.assertEqual(f(x, c), opt_f(x, c))\n\n    def test_nn_param_freevar_codegen(self):\n        class Model2(nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super().__init__()\n                self.conv = nn.Conv2d(in_channels=3, out_channels=5, kernel_size=3)\n                self.batchnorm = nn.BatchNorm2d(num_features=5)\n                self.conv_weight = torch.randn(5, 3, 3, 3)\n                self.conv_bias = torch.randn(5)\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                self.conv.weight = nn.Parameter(self.conv_weight)\n                self.conv.bias = nn.Parameter(self.conv_bias, requires_grad=False)\n                self.conv.eval()\n                x = self.conv(x)\n                x = self.batchnorm(x)\n                x = F.relu(x)\n                return x\n\n        input_tensor = torch.randn(1, 3, 10, 10)\n        func = Model2().to(\"cpu\")\n\n        @functools.wraps(func)\n        def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):\n            return func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            func.train(False)\n            v1 = func(input_tensor)\n            jit_func = torch.compile(wrapper, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n            v2 = jit_func(input_tensor)\n            self.assertEqual(v1, v2)\n\n    def test_amp_foreach_fake_impl(self):\n        inv_scale = torch.full((1,), 0.25)\n        found_inf = torch.full((1,), 0.0)\n        grads = [torch.ones(10), torch.ones(10)]\n\n        def f():\n            res = torch._amp_foreach_non_finite_check_and_unscale_(\n                grads, found_inf, inv_scale\n            )\n            return res\n\n        ref = f()\n        res = torch.compile(f, backend=\"aot_eager\")()\n        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n\n    def test_deleted_compile_wrapper_segfault(self):\n        def fn(x):\n            return x + 1\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        # This calls cached_backend.clear() which removes any strong references\n        # to the callback\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n        opt_fn(torch.randn(3))\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        opt_fn(torch.randn(3))  # possible segfault due to first opt_fn deletion\n\n    def test_delete_local_error(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(x):\n            y = x + 1\n            del y\n            z = y + 1  # noqa: F821\n            return z\n\n        with self.assertRaises(torch._dynamo.exc.Unsupported):\n            fn(torch.ones(3))\n\n    def test_nanmean_out(self):\n        def f(x, out):\n            torch.nanmean(x, out=out)\n\n        x = torch.randn(4)\n        out_ref = torch.tensor(0.0)\n        out_res = torch.tensor(0.0)\n\n        f(x, out_ref)\n        torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)(x, out_res)\n        self.assertEqual(out_ref, out_res)\n\n    @skipIfNotPy312\n    def test_sys_monitoring(self):\n        found_dynamo = False\n        found_compiled_graph = False\n        compiled_graph = None\n\n        def backend(gm, _):\n            nonlocal compiled_graph\n            compiled_graph = gm\n            return gm\n\n        def callback(code, offset):\n            nonlocal found_dynamo\n            nonlocal found_compiled_graph\n            torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n            if (\n                code\n                is torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert.InstructionTranslator.run.__code__\n            ):\n                found_dynamo = True\n            elif compiled_graph and code is compiled_graph.__call__.__code__:\n                found_compiled_graph = True\n\n        sys.monitoring.use_tool_id(0, \"test\")\n        old_callback = sys.monitoring.register_callback(\n            0, sys.monitoring.events.PY_START, callback\n        )\n        sys.monitoring.set_events(0, sys.monitoring.events.PY_START)\n        try:\n\n            @torch.compile(backend=backend, fullgraph=True)\n            def fn(x):\n                return x + 1\n\n            fn(torch.ones(3))\n            # sys.monitoring should still run in Python dynamo\n            self.assertTrue(found_dynamo)\n            # sys.monitoring should still run on the compiled graph\n            self.assertTrue(found_compiled_graph)\n        finally:\n            sys.monitoring.register_callback(\n                0, sys.monitoring.events.PY_START, old_callback\n            )\n\n    def test_312_local_cell_overlap(self):\n        keys = range(10)\n        allowed = [0, 1, 2, 3]\n\n        def fn(x):\n            x = x + 1\n            torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n            key = [key for key in keys if key in allowed]\n\n            def inner():\n                nonlocal key\n\n            return x + key[0]\n\n        self.assertEqual(\n            fn(torch.ones(3)), torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(torch.ones(3))\n        )\n\n    def test_311_resume_block_keyerror(self):\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/162313\n        flag = True\n\n        def fn(x):\n            x = x + 1\n            torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n            x = x + 2\n            if flag:\n                with torch.no_grad():\n                    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                x = x + 4\n            else:\n                with torch.no_grad():\n                    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                x = x + 8\n            return x + 16\n\n        inp = torch.ones(3)\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        self.assertEqual(fn(inp), opt_fn(inp))\n        flag = False\n        self.assertEqual(fn(inp), opt_fn(inp))\n\n    def test_unbind_copy_out(self):\n        def f(eye, out):\n            torch.unbind_copy(eye, out=out)\n\n        eye = torch.eye(3)\n        out_ref = (torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3))\n        out_res = (torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3))\n\n        f(eye, out_ref)\n        torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)(eye, out_res)\n        self.assertEqual(out_ref, out_res)\n\n    def test_setitem_tensor_prop(self):\n        # Using the composite implicit of the forward would be incorrect\n        class MyFn(torch.autograd.Function):\n            @staticmethod\n            def forward(ctx, x):\n                return torch.matmul(x, x.t())\n\n            @staticmethod\n            def backward(ctx, grad_out):\n                return grad_out\n\n        def fn(x, y):\n            x[0] = y[0]\n            return MyFn.apply(x)\n\n        def inputs():\n            torch.manual_seed(123)\n            x = torch.randn(10, 10)\n            y = torch.randn(10, 10, requires_grad=True)\n            return x, y\n\n        x1, y1 = inputs()\n        fn(x1, y1).sum().backward()\n        self.assertTrue(x1.requires_grad)\n\n        x2, y2 = inputs()\n        torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(x2, y2).sum().backward()\n        self.assertTrue(x2.requires_grad)\n\n        self.assertEqual(y1.grad, y2.grad)\n\n    def test_nn_parameter_ctor_graph_breaks(self):\n        def fn():\n            param = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.ones(10))\n            return param * 2\n\n        self.maxDiff = None\n        eb = ExplainWithBackend(\"eager\")\n        optimized_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=eb)\n        _ = optimized_fn()\n        explain_output = eb.output()\n        self.assertEqual(explain_output.graph_break_count, 1)\n        expected_msg = (\n            \"Attempted to use `torch.nn.Parameter()` constructor with Dynamo\\n\"\n            \"  Explanation: Dynamo does not support this\\n\"\n            \"  Hint: Try to construct `torch.nn.Parameter()` outside the compiled region.\\n\"\n            \"  Hint: If this is not possible, turn `graph_break_on_nn_param_ctor` off\\n\"\n            \"  Hint: It may be possible to write Dynamo tracing rules for this code. \"\n            \"Please report an issue to PyTorch if you encounter this graph break often and it is causing performance issues.\\n\\n\"\n            \"  Developer debug context: \\n\\n\"\n            \" For more details about this graph break, please visit: \"\n            \"https://meta-pytorch.github.io/compile-graph-break-site/gb/gb0264.html\"\n        )\n        self.assertEqual(explain_output.break_reasons[0].reason, expected_msg)\n\n\nclass ReproTestsDevice(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\n    def test_sub_alpha_scalar_repro(self, device):\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"aot_eager\")\n        def f(x):\n            return x.sub(1, alpha=2)\n\n        f(torch.ones(2, device=device, dtype=torch.float64))\n\n    @requires_cuda\n    def test_norm_dtype(self, device):\n        def foo(_stack0):\n            getitem = _stack0[(slice(None, None, None), -1)]\n            _stack0 = None\n            normalize = torch.nn.functional.normalize(getitem, p=2, dim=1)\n            getitem = None\n            return (normalize,)\n\n        args = [((2, 50, 256), (1, 256, 1), torch.float16, device, False)]\n        args = [\n            rand_strided(sh, st, dt, dev).requires_grad_(rg)\n            for (sh, st, dt, dev, rg) in args\n        ]\n\n        torch.compile(foo, backend=\"aot_eager_decomp_partition\")\n        with torch.cuda.amp.autocast(enabled=True):\n            ref = foo(*args)[0]\n            res = foo(*args)[0]\n            self.assertEqual(ref.dtype, res.dtype)\n\n            self.assertTrue(same(res, ref))\n\n    def test_guard_default_device(self, device):\n        try:\n            torch.set_default_device(device)\n\n            counter = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n\n            @torch._dynamo.optimize(counter)\n            def f():\n                x = torch.randn(3)\n                return x * 2\n\n            self.assertEqual(f().device.type + \":0\", device)\n            self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 1)\n\n            torch.set_default_device(\"cpu\")\n\n            self.assertEqual(f().device.type, \"cpu\")\n            self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 2)\n\n        finally:\n            torch.set_default_device(None)\n\n    @skipIfHpu\n    @unittest.skipIf(\n        TEST_WITH_ROCM or not PLATFORM_SUPPORTS_FLASH_ATTENTION,\n        \"flash attention not supported\",\n    )\n    def test_flash_attn_backward_mixed_strides(self, device):\n        # in this repro, \"grad_out\" and \"value\" are transposed tensors,\n        # but \"key\" and \"value\" are contiguous\n        def gen_inputs(device):\n            return (\n                torch.randn(\n                    2, 513, 16, 64, dtype=torch.float16, device=device\n                ).transpose(1, 2),\n                torch.randn(2, 16, 513, 64, dtype=torch.float16, device=device),\n                torch.randn(2, 16, 513, 64, dtype=torch.float16, device=device),\n                torch.randn(\n                    2, 513, 16, 64, dtype=torch.float16, device=device\n                ).transpose(1, 2),\n                torch.randn(2, 16, 513, 64, dtype=torch.float16, device=device),\n                torch.randn(2, 16, 513, device=device),\n                None,\n                None,\n                513,\n                513,\n                0.0,\n                False,\n                torch.tensor(1, dtype=torch.int64),\n                torch.tensor(1, dtype=torch.int64),\n            )\n\n        inps_device = gen_inputs(device)\n        inps_meta = gen_inputs(\"meta\")\n        (\n            out1_ref,\n            out2_ref,\n            out3_ref,\n        ) = torch.ops.aten._scaled_dot_product_flash_attention_backward(\n            *inps_device, scale=0.125\n        )\n        from torch._meta_registrations import meta__scaled_dot_product_flash_backward\n\n        out1_test, out2_test, out3_test = meta__scaled_dot_product_flash_backward(\n            *inps_meta, scale=0.125\n        )\n\n        self.assertEqual(out1_ref.shape, out1_test.shape)\n        self.assertEqual(out1_ref.stride(), out1_test.stride())\n        self.assertEqual(out2_ref.shape, out2_test.shape)\n        self.assertEqual(out2_ref.stride(), out2_test.stride())\n        self.assertEqual(out3_ref.shape, out3_test.shape)\n        self.assertEqual(out3_ref.stride(), out3_test.stride())\n\n    def test_megablocks_moe(self, device):\n        try:\n            from megablocks.layers import moe\n            from megablocks.layers.arguments import Arguments\n        except ImportError as e:\n            raise unittest.SkipTest(\"requires megablocks\") from e\n        bs, sl, hs, num_experts, top_k = (16, 1024, 512, 1, 1)\n        args = Arguments(\n            hidden_size=hs,\n            ffn_hidden_size=hs * 2,\n            moe_num_experts=num_experts,\n            moe_capacity_factor=1,\n            moe_top_k=top_k,\n        )\n        moe_mlp = moe.MoE(args)\n        # moe_mlp.cuda(torch.cuda.current_device()).half()\n        moe_mlp.device(torch.device.current_device()).half()\n        x = torch.randn(sl, bs, hs).device().half()\n        out1, _ = moe_mlp(x)\n        out2, _ = torch.compile(moe_mlp, backend=\"eager\")(x)\n        self.assertEqual(out1, out2)\n\n    def test_tensor_size_hasattr(self):\n        def fn(x):\n            if hasattr(x, \"size\"):\n                x = x * 2\n            if hasattr(x, \"stride\"):\n                x = x * 3\n            return x * 5\n\n        x = torch.ones(4)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n\n    @requires_cuda\n    def test_memleak_when_graph_input_has_tensor_attr(self, device):\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def f(x):\n            x.add_(1)\n\n        mem_before = torch.cuda.memory_allocated()\n\n        x = torch.ones(2, device=device)\n        x.foo = torch.zeros(2, device=device)\n        f(x)\n        del x.foo\n        del x\n        mem_after = torch.cuda.memory_allocated()\n        self.assertEqual(mem_before, mem_after)\n\n        # check when non-tensor data structure attribute contains a tensor\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def f(x):\n            x.add_(1)\n\n        mem_before = torch.cuda.memory_allocated()\n        x = torch.ones(2, device=device)\n        x.foo = [torch.zeros(2, device=device) for _ in range(5)]\n        f(x)\n        del x.foo\n        del x\n        mem_after = torch.cuda.memory_allocated()\n        self.assertEqual(mem_before, mem_after)\n\n        # check with tensor refcycle\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def g(x, y):\n            return x + y\n\n        mem_before = torch.cuda.memory_allocated()\n        x = torch.ones(2, device=device)\n        y = torch.zeros(2, device=device)\n        x.foo = [y]\n        y.foo = [x]\n        g(x, y)\n        del x.foo\n        del y.foo\n        del x\n        del y\n        mem_after = torch.cuda.memory_allocated()\n        self.assertEqual(mem_before, mem_after)\n\n    def test_udf_class_source(self):\n        class Foo:\n            pass\n\n        def fn(x):\n            foo = Foo()\n            bar = type(foo)()  # noqa: F841\n            return torch.cos(x)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        x = torch.randn(4)\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n\n    def test_truthiness_of_symints_no_recompiles(self, device):\n        def f(x):\n            numel = x.numel()\n            if numel:\n                return x + 1\n            else:\n                return x + 2\n\n        cnt = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        f_compiled = torch.compile(f, backend=cnt, dynamic=True)\n\n        x1 = torch.randn(4)\n        _ = f_compiled(x1)\n        x2 = torch.randn(5)\n        _ = f_compiled(x2)\n\n        self.assertEqual(cnt.frame_count, 1)\n\n    @requires_cuda\n    def test_sdpa_dynamic_shapes(self, device):\n        def f(x, s0, s1, s2):\n            q = x.view(2, s0, s2, s0)\n            return torch._C._nn.scaled_dot_product_attention(\n                q, q, q, attn_mask=None, dropout_p=0.0, is_causal=True\n            )\n\n        x = torch.randn(2, 32, 4096, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=device)\n        x_ref = x.clone().detach().requires_grad_()\n        s0 = 32\n        s1 = 64\n        s2 = 128\n\n        f_compiled = torch.compile(f, dynamic=True)\n\n        with torch._dynamo.config.patch(assume_static_by_default=False):\n            out_ref = f(x_ref, s0, s1, s2)\n            out = f_compiled(x, s0, s1, s2)\n            self.assertEqual(out_ref, out)\n\n    @skipIfRocm\n    @unittest.skipIf(not PLATFORM_SUPPORTS_FP8, \"requires gpu with fp8 support\")\n    @requires_cuda\n    def test_partitioner_saves_weights_for_bw(self):\n        def mul_tiled(a, *bs):\n            for b in bs:\n                a = a.unflatten(0, (b.shape[0], -1)).unflatten(-1, (b.shape[-1], -1))\n                a = a * b[:, None, :, None]\n                a = a.flatten(end_dim=1).flatten(start_dim=-2)\n            return a\n\n        def scale(t, amax_t):\n            max_v = torch.finfo(torch.float8_e4m3fn).max\n            scale_t = torch.clamp(amax_t.float(), min=1e-12) / max_v\n            t_fp8 = mul_tiled(t, scale_t.reciprocal()).to(torch.float8_e4m3fn)\n            return t_fp8, scale_t\n\n        def matmul(first, amax_first, second_t, amax_second_t, bias):\n            first_fp8, scale_first = scale(first, amax_first)\n            second_t_fp8, scale_second_t = scale(second_t, amax_second_t)\n            post_scales = []\n            post_bias = None\n            post_scales = [scale_first, scale_second_t.t()]\n            scale_first = scale_first.new_ones((1, 1))\n            scale_second_t = scale_second_t.t().new_ones((1, 1))\n            post_bias, bias = bias, None\n            res = torch._scaled_mm(\n                first_fp8,\n                second_t_fp8.t(),\n                scale_a=scale_first,\n                scale_b=scale_second_t.t(),\n                bias=bias,\n                out_dtype=torch.bfloat16,\n                use_fast_accum=False,\n            )\n            res = mul_tiled(res, *post_scales).to(torch.bfloat16)\n            if post_bias is not None:\n                res += post_bias\n            return res\n\n        @torch.compiler.allow_in_graph\n        class Fp8LinearFn(torch.autograd.Function):\n            @staticmethod\n            def forward(ctx, a, b_t, bias):\n                amax_a = a.abs().unflatten(-1, (1, -1)).amax(dim=-1)\n                amax_b_t = b_t.abs().unflatten(-1, (1, -1)).amax(dim=-1)\n                out = matmul(a, amax_a, b_t, amax_b_t, bias)\n                ctx.a_requires_grad = a.requires_grad\n                ctx.b_requires_grad = b_t.requires_grad\n                ctx.bias_requires_grad = (\n                    bias.requires_grad if bias is not None else False\n                )\n                ctx.save_for_backward(a, b_t, amax_b_t)\n                return out\n\n            @staticmethod\n            def backward(ctx, grad_out):\n                a, b_t, amax_b_t = ctx.saved_tensors\n                # Workaround for https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141881.\n                # The partitioner would pre-compute the transposed scaling of the weight\n                # in the forward (as it's most efficient, but it actually uses too much\n                # memory). We prevent that by making the scaling depend on the gradient\n                # in a way that has no effect and will be optimized away later.\n                # Care is needed to support tensor parallelism and circumvent bugs.\n                #        b_t = b_t + grad_out[:1, :, None].squeeze(0) * 0\n                if ctx.a_requires_grad:\n                    b = b_t.t().contiguous()\n                    amax_grad_out = grad_out.abs().unflatten(-1, (1, -1)).amax(dim=-1)\n                    amax_b = amax_b_t.t().unflatten(-1, (1, -1)).amax(dim=-1)\n                    amax_b = amax_b.repeat_interleave(\n                        b.shape[0] // amax_b.shape[0], dim=0, output_size=b.shape[0]\n                    )\n                    grad_a = matmul(grad_out, amax_grad_out, b, amax_b, None)\n                else:\n                    grad_a = None\n                if ctx.b_requires_grad:\n                    grad_b = grad_out.t() @ a\n                else:\n                    grad_b = None\n                if ctx.bias_requires_grad:\n                    grad_bias = grad_out.sum(dim=0)\n                else:\n                    grad_bias = None\n                return grad_a, grad_b, grad_bias\n\n        class Mod(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.a = torch.nn.Parameter(\n                    torch.randn(\n                        64, 64, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True\n                    )\n                )\n                self.b = torch.nn.Parameter(\n                    torch.randn(\n                        64, 64, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True\n                    )\n                )\n                self.bias = torch.nn.Parameter(\n                    torch.randn(\n                        64, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True\n                    )\n                )\n\n        class CustomLinear(torch.nn.Linear):\n            def forward(self, input: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:\n                out = Fp8LinearFn.apply(\n                    input.flatten(end_dim=-2), self.weight, self.bias\n                )\n                out = out.unflatten(0, input.shape[:-1])\n                return out\n\n        m = CustomLinear(64, 64, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=\"cuda\")\n        m = torch.compile(m, backend=\"aot_eager\")\n\n        # simple mode to track how many collective ops we saw in the backward\n        class TrackingMode(TorchDispatchMode):\n            def __init__(self):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.ops_counter = defaultdict(int)\n\n            def __torch_dispatch__(self, func, types, args=(), kwargs=None):\n                if kwargs is None:\n                    kwargs = {}\n                rs = func(*args, **kwargs)\n                self.ops_counter[func] += 1\n                return rs\n\n        a = torch.randn(64, 64, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True)\n        out = m(a)\n        with TrackingMode() as mode:\n            out.sum().backward()\n        # If you print out the AOT fw and bw graphs,\n        # the main thing to look for is that both weights (primals_1/primals_2)\n        # *are* saved for backward, and become back inputs.\n        # The easier-to-test thing I'm checking for here is that the recompute\n        # on primals_2 happens in the backward. With the recompute,\n        # there are 5 _to_copy ops in the backward. Without it, there are 4\n        # (aka if you set torch._functorch.config.treat_parameters_as_free_to_save = False)\n        self.assertEqual(mode.ops_counter[torch.ops.aten._to_copy.default], 5)\n\n    def test_getattr_return(self):\n        _WrapperDescriptor = type(type.__call__)\n        _MethodWrapper = type(all.__call__)\n        _ClassMethodWrapper = type(int.__dict__[\"from_bytes\"])\n\n        _NonUserDefinedCallables = (\n            _WrapperDescriptor,\n            _MethodWrapper,\n            _ClassMethodWrapper,\n            types.BuiltinFunctionType,\n        )\n\n        def _signature_get_user_defined_method(cls, method_name):\n            try:\n                meth = getattr(cls, method_name)\n            except AttributeError:\n                return\n            else:\n                if not isinstance(meth, _NonUserDefinedCallables):\n                    # Once '__signature__' will be added to 'C'-level\n                    # callables, this check won't be necessary\n                    return meth\n\n        def fn(x):\n            s = _signature_get_user_defined_method(type(torch.nn.Linear), \"__call__\")\n            if s is None:\n                return torch.cos(x)\n\n            return torch.sin(x)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        x = torch.randn(4)\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n\n    def test_data_dependent_error_log_no_print(self):\n        # This is a regression test case for\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149831\n        from io import StringIO\n\n        capturedOutput = StringIO()\n        sys.stderr = capturedOutput\n\n        @torch.compile(fullgraph=True)\n        def func(a):\n            if a.sum() > 0:\n                return a + 1\n            return a + 2\n\n        a = torch.rand(10, 10)\n        try:\n            func(a)\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n        sys.stderr = sys.__stderr__\n\n        # Make sure we don't _print_ out the graph module.\n        output = capturedOutput.getvalue()\n        self.assertNotIn(\"class GraphModule\", output)\n\n    def test_deepcopy_constant_tensor_in_aot_bwd(self):\n        class Fn(torch.autograd.Function):\n            @staticmethod\n            def forward(ctx, x):\n                return x + 1\n\n            @staticmethod\n            def backward(ctx, grad_out):\n                return grad_out * torch.tensor(2) * grad_out.shape[0]\n\n        def f(x):\n            return Fn.apply(x)\n\n        x = torch.randn(8, requires_grad=True)\n        out = f(x)  # should not raise\n        c_out = torch.compile(f, backend=\"aot_eager\", dynamic=True)(x)\n        expected = torch.autograd.grad(out.sum(), inputs=(x,))\n        actual = torch.autograd.grad(c_out.sum(), inputs=(x,))\n        self.assertEqual(expected, actual)\n\n    def test_module_attribute_error(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def f1(x):\n            return torch._bar(x)\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def f2(x):\n            try:\n                return torch._bar(x)\n            except AttributeError:\n                return x + 1\n\n        with self.assertRaises(AttributeError):\n            f1(torch.ones(3))\n\n        self.assertEqual(f2(torch.ones(3)), torch.ones(3) + 1)\n\n    def test_torch_cuda_is_initialized(self):\n        @torch.compile(fullgraph=True, backend=\"eager\")\n        def f(x):\n            if torch.cuda.is_initialized():\n                return x + 1\n            return x + 2\n\n        inp = torch.randn(3)\n        self.assertEqual(f(inp), inp + 1)\n\n        with mock.patch(\"torch.cuda.is_initialized\", lambda: False):\n            self.assertEqual(f(inp), inp + 2)\n\n    def test_named_tuple_vt_clone(self):\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/157945\n        class SVDCompressor(nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self, k=10):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.k = k\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                U, S = torch.linalg.svd(x)[:2]\n                reduced = U[:, :, : self.k] @ torch.diag_embed(S[:, : self.k])\n                return reduced\n\n        input = torch.randn(4, 8, 6)\n        model = SVDCompressor(k=5)\n\n        out1 = model(input.clone())\n        out2 = torch.compile(model, backend=\"eager\")(input.clone())\n        self.assertEqual(out1, out2)\n\n    @requires_cuda\n    def test_zero_dim_param_mixed_device_grad(self):\n        # cpu 0-dim params with cuda grads\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/160084\n        class RegressionModel(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self, a=0, b=0):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.a = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(a).float())\n                self.b = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(b).float())\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                return x * self.a + self.b\n\n        model = RegressionModel()\n        model.forward = torch.compile(\n            model.forward, backend=\"aot_eager\", fullgraph=True\n        )\n        inputs = torch.randn(4, 10).to(\"cuda\")\n        out = model(inputs)\n        out.sum().backward()\n        self.assertIsNotNone(model.a.grad)\n        self.assertIsNotNone(model.b.grad)\n        self.assertEqual(model.a.grad.device, torch.device(\"cpu\"))\n        self.assertEqual(model.b.grad.device, torch.device(\"cpu\"))\n\n    @unittest.skipIf(not TEST_CUDA, \"test requires CUDA\")\n    def test_cuda_sync(self):\n        def fn(x):\n            y = x + 1\n            torch.cuda.synchronize()\n            return y * 2\n\n        x = torch.ones(2, device=\"cuda\")\n        cnt = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnt)\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n        self.assertEqual(cnt.frame_count, 2)\n\n    def test_filter_warnings(self):\n        x = torch.ones(2, 2, requires_grad=True)\n\n        def call_foobar(x):\n            warnings.warn(\"foobar\")\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def f(x):\n            call_foobar(x)\n            call_foobar(x)\n            call_foobar(x)\n            call_foobar(x)\n            return call_foobar(x)\n\n        with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) as w:\n            f(x)\n            self.assertEqual(len(w), 1)\n            self.assertEqual(str(w[0].message), \"foobar\")\n\n    def test_filter_safe_grad_warning(self):\n        x = torch.ones(2, 2, requires_grad=True)\n        y = x * 5  # non-leaf, .grad should warn\n        torch._subclasses.meta_utils.safe_grad(y)  # filters out warning\n\n        def unsafe_grad(y):\n            return y.grad\n\n        with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) as w:\n            unsafe_grad(y)  # should still warn, different callsite\n            self.assertEqual(len(w), 1)\n            self.assertTrue(\"The .grad attribute of a Tensor\" in str(w[0].message))\n\n            unsafe_grad(y)  # should not warn\n            self.assertEqual(len(w), 1)\n\n    def test_filter_user_warnings(self):\n        x = torch.ones(2, 2, requires_grad=True)\n        y = x * 5  # non-leaf, .grad should warn\n\n        @torch._dynamo.eval_frame.TorchPatcher.suppress_torch_distributed_warnings\n        def mute_warn(y):\n            return y.grad\n\n        mute_warn(y)  # filters out warning\n\n        def unsafe_grad(y):\n            return y.grad\n\n        with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) as w:\n            unsafe_grad(y)  # should still warn, different callsite\n            self.assertEqual(len(w), 1)\n            self.assertTrue(\"The .grad attribute of a Tensor\" in str(w[0].message))\n\n            unsafe_grad(y)  # should not warn\n            self.assertEqual(len(w), 1)\n\n\ninstantiate_parametrized_tests(ReproTests)\n\ndevices = [\"cuda\", \"hpu\"]\ninstantiate_device_type_tests(ReproTestsDevice, globals(), only_for=devices)\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    from torch._dynamo.test_case import run_tests\n\n    run_tests()\n",

    "content": "    def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\")\n        if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\",\n                context=\"\",\n                explanation=\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \"\n                \"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n        all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n            self,\n            reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),\n            stack_pops=2,\n        )\n        store_attr_inst = copy.copy(inst)\n        # Clear exception table entry since this instruction is being moved outside\n        # its original with block context\n        store_attr_inst.exn_tab_entry = None\n        self.output.add_output_instructions([store_attr_inst])\n        self.popn(2)\n        self.output.add_output_instructions(\n            self.create_call_resume_at(\n                self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n            )",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py",
    "numLines": 25,
    "startLine": 2467,
    "totalLines": 4677
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{
  "file": {
    "content": "        self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 1)\n\n    def test_with_on_graph_break_inst(self):\n        def reversible(x):\n            print(\"Hello world\")  # Cause graph break so inline fails\n            return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\n\n        def fn(x):\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                a = torch.sin(x)\n                b = reversible(a)\n                c = torch.sigmoid(b)\n                c.sum().backward()\n                return x.grad\n\n        x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n        x.grad = None\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            ref = fn(x)\n\n        x.grad = None\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            res = opt_fn(x)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n    def test_with_on_graph_break_nested(self):\n        def reversible(x):\n            torch._dynamo.graph_break()  # Cause graph break so inline fails\n            return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\n\n        def fn(x):\n            # nested context manager failed previously\n            with torch.no_grad():\n                with torch.enable_grad():\n                    a = torch.sin(x)\n                    b = reversible(a)\n                    c = torch.sigmoid(b)\n                    c.sum().backward()\n                    return x.grad\n\n        x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n        x.grad = None\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            ref = fn(x)\n\n        x.grad = None\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            res = opt_fn(x)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n\nclass ContextlibContextManagerTests(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\n    def setUp(self):\n        self._prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib\n        self._u_prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = True\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = True\n\n    def tearDown(self):\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = self._prev\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = self._u_prev\n\n    def test_ctx_basic0(self):\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def set_default_dtype(dtype):\n            old_dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()\n            try:\n                torch.set_default_dtype(dtype)",

}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "        if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n            # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\n            # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\n            # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\n            # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\n            if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\n                block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\n                    new_offset\n                ] = {}\n\n                def remap_block_offsets(\n                    instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n                ) -> None:\n                    # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\n                    # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\n                    # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\n                    # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\n                    # replaced.\n                    prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\n                    for inst in instructions:\n                        if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\n                            meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n                        ):\n                            break\n                        if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\":\n                            prefix_blocks.append(inst)\n\n                    # offsets into prefix\n                    for inst, o in zip(\n                        prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n                    ):\n                        block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\n\n                    # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\n                    old_start_offset = (\n                        cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\n                    )\n                    # offsets into old bytecode\n                    old_inst_offsets = sorted(\n                        n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\n                    )\n                    targets = _filter_iter(\n                        instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\n                    )\n                    new_targets = _filter_iter(\n                        zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\n                        targets,\n                        lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\n                    )\n                    for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\n                        block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\n\n                transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\n\n            # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\n            setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\n                meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\n                for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\n            )\n        return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\n            meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\n        )\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py",
    "numLines": 63,
    "startLine": 614,
    "totalLines": 676
  },
  "type": "text"
}

    "content": "            cleanup: list[Instruction] = []\n            hooks = {fn.stack_index: fn for fn in setup_fns}\n            hook_target_offsets = {\n                fn.stack_index: setup_fn_target_offsets[i]\n                for i, fn in enumerate(setup_fns)\n            }\n            offset_to_inst = {inst.offset: inst for inst in instructions}\n            # map old hook targets to new targets generated by the hook\n            old_hook_target_remap = {}\n            null_idxes_i = 0\n            stack_ctx_vars_d = dict(stack_ctx_vars)  # type: ignore[var-annotated,arg-type]\n            for i in range(nstack):\n                while (\n                    null_idxes_i < len(null_idxes)\n                    and null_idxes[null_idxes_i] == i + null_idxes_i\n                ):\n                    prefix.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n                    null_idxes_i += 1\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=f\"___stack{i}\"))\n                if i in hooks:\n                    hook = hooks.pop(i)\n                    hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\n                    prefix.extend(hook_insts)\n                    if is_py311_plus:\n                        hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\n                        if hook_target_offset in offset_to_inst:\n                            old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n                            meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\n                            old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\n                if i in stack_ctx_vars_d:\n                    # NOTE: we assume that current stack var is a context manager CLASS!\n                    # Load args for context variable and construct it\n                    prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(stack_ctx_vars_d[i]))\n\n            if is_py311_plus:\n                # reverse the mapping since targets of later/nested contexts are inserted\n                # into the mapping later, but show up earlier in the prefix.\n                meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = list(\n                    reversed(meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap)\n                )\n\n            assert not hooks\n\n            # NOTE: we assume that local var is a context manager CLASS!\n            # initialize inactive context vars in argnames\n            for name, vals in argnames_ctx_vars:\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=name))\n                prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(vals))\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=name))\n\n            # 3.12+: store NULL into variables that were NULL\n            if argnames_null:\n                assert sys.version_info >= (3, 12)\n                for v in argnames_null:\n                    assert v not in args\n                    prefix.extend(\n                        [\n                            create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"),\n                            create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=v),\n                        ]\n                    )\n\n            # Call nested resume function\n            if nested_code_objs:\n                prefix.extend(\n                    [\n                        # set up __nested_resume_fns[-1] call\n                        *add_push_null(\n                            [\n                                create_instruction(\n                                    \"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"\n                                ),\n                                create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                                create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n                            ]",

102. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py",

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py",
  "newString": "            # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\n            setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\n                meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset].get(n, n)\n                for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\n            )",
  "oldString": "            # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\n            setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\n                meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\n                for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\n            )",
  "originalFile": "\"\"\"\nThis module provides functionality for resuming Python execution at specific points in code,\nprimarily used by PyTorch Dynamo for control flow handling and optimization. It implements\nbytecode transformation and execution state management to enable:\n\n- Resuming execution at arbitrary points in Python bytecode\n- Managing context managers and their state across execution boundaries\n- Transforming and generating new code objects with preserved execution state\n- Supporting Python 3.11+ exception handling and block management\n- Restoring torch function mode stacks and other execution context\n\nThe module is critical for PyTorch Dynamo's ability to optimize code while preserving\nPython semantics and execution state.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport copy\nimport dataclasses\nimport sys\nimport types\nfrom collections.abc import Iterable\nfrom contextlib import AbstractContextManager\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional\n\nfrom .bytecode_transformation import (\n    add_push_null,\n    bytecode_from_template,\n    create_call_function,\n    create_instruction,\n    create_jump_absolute,\n    create_load_const,\n    Instruction,\n    overwrite_instruction,\n    transform_code_object,\n    unique_id,\n)\nfrom .utils import ExactWeakKeyDictionary\n\n\n# taken from code.h in cpython\nCO_OPTIMIZED = 0x0001\nCO_NEWLOCALS = 0x0002\nCO_VARARGS = 0x0004\nCO_VARKEYWORDS = 0x0008\nCO_NESTED = 0x0010\nCO_GENERATOR = 0x0020\nCO_NOFREE = 0x0040\nCO_COROUTINE = 0x0080\nCO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE = 0x0100\nCO_ASYNC_GENERATOR = 0x0200\n\n# trace_rules.py import this constant for consistency\nTORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX = \"torch_dynamo_resume_in\"\nIS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME = \"__is_tracing_resume_prologue\"\n\n\ndef _initial_push_null(insts: list[Instruction]) -> None:\n    if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n        insts.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n        if sys.version_info < (3, 13):\n            insts.append(create_instruction(\"SWAP\", arg=2))\n\n\n# Generates bytecode from template and splits the code where LOAD_FAST dummy is present.\ndef _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n    template: Callable[..., Any],\n    stack_index: int,\n    varname_map: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\n) -> tuple[list[Instruction], list[Instruction]]:\n    template_code = bytecode_from_template(template, varname_map=varname_map)\n    template_code.append(create_instruction(\"POP_TOP\"))\n\n    # adjust exception table entry depth\n    for inst in template_code:\n        if inst.exn_tab_entry:\n            inst.exn_tab_entry.depth += stack_index\n\n    # search for LOAD_FAST dummy and replace it with 2 NOPs (we can break up the bytecode between them)\n    dummy_idx, dummy_inst = next(\n        (\n            (i, inst)\n            for i, inst in enumerate(template_code)\n            if inst.opname == \"LOAD_FAST\" and inst.argval == \"dummy\"\n        ),\n        (None, None),\n    )\n    assert dummy_idx is not None and dummy_inst is not None\n\n    # replace LOAD_FAST dummy with first NOP marking exception area\n    overwrite_instruction(dummy_inst, [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n\n    # POP_TOP follows LOAD_FAST dummy - replace with NOP marking end of exception area\n    assert template_code[dummy_idx + 1].opname == \"POP_TOP\"\n    overwrite_instruction(template_code[dummy_idx + 1], [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n\n    return template_code[: dummy_idx + 1], template_code[dummy_idx + 1 :]\n\n\ndef _try_except_tf_mode_template(dummy: Any, stack_var_name: Any) -> None:\n    # NOTE: Make sure this name matches what is generated by symbolic_convert:import_source\n    # on torch._dynamo.utils.\n    global __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils\n    try:\n        dummy\n    except:  # noqa: E722, B001\n        __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils.set_torch_function_mode_stack(  # type: ignore[name-defined]\n            stack_var_name\n        )\n        raise\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass ReenterWith:\n    stack_index: int\n    target_values: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]] = None\n\n    def try_except_torch_function_mode(\n        self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n    ) -> list[Instruction]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Codegen based off of:\n        try:\n            (rest)\n        except:\n            (restore previous tf mode stack)\n            raise\n        \"\"\"\n        from .variables.torch_function import get_prev_stack_var_name\n\n        setup_try_except, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n            _try_except_tf_mode_template,\n            self.stack_index,\n            varname_map={\"stack_var_name\": get_prev_stack_var_name()},\n        )\n        cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n\n        return setup_try_except\n\n    # If we do not want to destroy the stack, we can do the same thing as a\n    # `SETUP_WITH` block, only that we store the context manager in a local_symbol\n    def try_finally(\n        self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n    ) -> list[Instruction]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Codegen based off of:\n        load args\n        enter context\n        try:\n            (rest)\n        finally:\n            exit context\n        \"\"\"\n        # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\n        load_args = []\n        if self.target_values:\n            load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\n        ctx_name = unique_id(f\"___context_manager_{self.stack_index}\")\n        if ctx_name not in code_options[\"co_varnames\"]:\n            code_options[\"co_varnames\"] += (ctx_name,)\n        for name in [\"__enter__\", \"__exit__\"]:\n            if name not in code_options[\"co_names\"]:\n                code_options[\"co_names\"] += (name,)\n\n        create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\n        _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\n        create_ctx.extend(\n            [\n                *load_args,\n                *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\n                create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=ctx_name),\n            ]\n        )\n\n        def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\n            ctx.__enter__()\n            try:\n                dummy\n            finally:\n                ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n\n        setup_try_finally, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n            _template, self.stack_index, varname_map={\"ctx\": ctx_name}\n        )\n        cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n        return create_ctx + setup_try_finally\n\n    def __call__(\n        self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n    ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], Optional[Instruction]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Codegen based off of:\n        with ctx(args):\n            (rest)\n        \"\"\"\n        # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\n        load_args = []\n        if self.target_values:\n            load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\n\n        create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\n        _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\n        create_ctx.extend(\n            [\n                *load_args,\n                *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\n            ]\n        )\n\n        def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\n            with ctx:\n                dummy\n\n        setup_with, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n            _template, self.stack_index\n        )\n        cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n\n        load_fast_ctx_inst = next(\n            (\n                inst\n                for inst in setup_with\n                if inst.opname == \"LOAD_FAST\" and inst.argval == \"ctx\"\n            ),\n            None,\n        )\n        assert load_fast_ctx_inst is not None\n        # ctx already loaded on stack before the template - no need to LOAD_FAST\n        overwrite_instruction(load_fast_ctx_inst, [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n\n        # 3.11+ only\n        push_exc_info_gen = (\n            inst for inst in epilogue if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\"\n        )\n        push_exc_info_inst = next(push_exc_info_gen, None)\n        # expect only 1 PUSH_EXC_INFO in epilogue\n        assert next(push_exc_info_gen, None) is None\n\n        return create_ctx + setup_with, push_exc_info_inst\n\n\n@dataclasses.dataclass\nclass ResumeFunctionMetadata:\n    code: types.CodeType\n    instructions: list[Instruction] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\n    # Python 3.11+ fields\n    # NOTE: Python 3.11 removed blocks, but for our purposes, a \"block\" consists\n    # of instructions of all exception table entries that have the same target.\n\n    # map from PUSH_EXC_INFO's in the prefix to original block target offset\n    prefix_block_target_offset_remap: list[int] = dataclasses.field(\n        default_factory=list\n    )\n    # per-offset map from new block target offsets to original block target offsets\n    block_target_offset_remap: dict[int, dict[int, int]] = dataclasses.field(\n        default_factory=dict\n    )\n\n\ndef _filter_iter(\n    l1: Iterable[Any],\n    l2: Iterable[Any],\n    cond: Callable[[Any, Any], bool],\n) -> list[Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Two-pointer conditional filter.\n    e.g. _filter_iter(insts, sorted_offsets, lambda i, o: i.offset == o)\n    returns the instructions with offsets in sorted_offsets\n    \"\"\"\n    it = iter(l2)\n    res: list[Instruction] = []\n    try:\n        cur = next(it)\n        for val in l1:\n            if cond(val, cur):\n                res.append(val)\n                cur = next(it)\n    except StopIteration:\n        pass\n    return res\n\n\ndef _load_tuple_and_call(tup: tuple[Any, ...]) -> list[Instruction]:\n    insts: list[Instruction] = []\n    _initial_push_null(insts)\n    insts.extend(create_load_const(val) for val in tup)\n    insts.extend(create_call_function(len(tup), False))\n    return insts\n\n\nclass ContinueExecutionCache:\n    cache = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\n    generated_code_metadata = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\n\n    @classmethod\n    def lookup(cls, code: types.CodeType, lineno: int, *key: Any) -> types.CodeType:\n        if code not in cls.cache:\n            cls.cache[code] = {}\n        key = tuple(key)\n        if key not in cls.cache[code]:\n            cls.cache[code][key] = cls.generate(code, lineno, *key)\n        return cls.cache[code][key]\n\n    @classmethod\n    def generate(\n        cls,\n        code: types.CodeType,\n        lineno: int,\n        offset: int,\n        setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],  # only used in Python 3.11+\n        nstack: int,\n        argnames: tuple[str, ...],\n        argnames_null: tuple[str, ...],\n        setup_fns: tuple[ReenterWith, ...],\n        stack_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\n        argnames_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\n        null_idxes: tuple[int, ...],\n        # mainly used to ensure distinct code objects per stack trace,\n        # which prevents excessive recompilation of inner frames\n        nested_code_objs: tuple[types.CodeType],\n    ) -> types.CodeType:\n        assert offset is not None\n        assert not (\n            code.co_flags\n            & (CO_GENERATOR | CO_COROUTINE | CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE | CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR)\n        )\n        assert code.co_flags & CO_OPTIMIZED\n        if code in ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata:\n            return cls.generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n                code,\n                lineno,\n                offset,\n                setup_fn_target_offsets,\n                nstack,\n                argnames,\n                argnames_null,\n                setup_fns,\n                stack_ctx_vars,\n                argnames_ctx_vars,\n                null_idxes,\n                nested_code_objs,\n            )\n\n        is_py311_plus = sys.version_info >= (3, 11)\n        meta = ResumeFunctionMetadata(code)\n\n        def update(\n            instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n        ) -> None:\n            meta.instructions = copy.deepcopy(instructions)\n\n            args = [\"__nested_resume_fns\", \"__nested_frame_values\"]\n            args += [f\"___stack{i}\" for i in range(nstack)]\n            args.extend(v for v in argnames if v not in args)\n            freevars = tuple(code_options[\"co_cellvars\"] or []) + tuple(\n                code_options[\"co_freevars\"] or []\n            )\n            freevars = tuple(sorted(freevars))\n            code_options[\"co_name\"] = (\n                f\"{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{code_options['co_name']}_at_{lineno}\"\n            )\n            if is_py311_plus:\n                qualified_path = code_options[\"co_qualname\"].rsplit(\".\", maxsplit=1)\n                if len(qualified_path) == 1:\n                    code_options[\"co_qualname\"] = code_options[\"co_name\"]\n                else:\n                    assert len(qualified_path) == 2\n                    module_name, co_name = qualified_path\n                    code_options[\"co_qualname\"] = (\n                        f\"{module_name}.{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{co_name}_at_{lineno}\"\n                    )\n            code_options[\"co_firstlineno\"] = lineno\n            code_options[\"co_cellvars\"] = ()\n            code_options[\"co_freevars\"] = freevars\n            code_options[\"co_argcount\"] = len(args)\n            code_options[\"co_posonlyargcount\"] = 0\n            code_options[\"co_kwonlyargcount\"] = 0\n            code_options[\"co_varnames\"] = tuple(\n                args\n                + [v for v in argnames_null if v not in args]\n                + [v for v in code_options[\"co_varnames\"] if v not in args]\n                + [IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME]\n            )\n            code_options[\"co_flags\"] = code_options[\"co_flags\"] & ~(\n                CO_VARARGS | CO_VARKEYWORDS\n            )\n            target = next(i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n\n            prefix = []\n            if is_py311_plus:\n                if freevars:\n                    prefix.append(\n                        create_instruction(\"COPY_FREE_VARS\", arg=len(freevars))\n                    )\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"RESUME\", arg=0))\n\n            # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue to prevent graph breaks.\n            # This doesn't really do anything at runtime, but dynamo will trace this\n            # and will know that we're in a resume function prologue.\n            prefix.extend(\n                [\n                    create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=True),\n                    create_instruction(\n                        \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n                    ),\n                ]\n            )\n\n            cleanup: list[Instruction] = []\n            hooks = {fn.stack_index: fn for fn in setup_fns}\n            hook_target_offsets = {\n                fn.stack_index: setup_fn_target_offsets[i]\n                for i, fn in enumerate(setup_fns)\n            }\n            offset_to_inst = {inst.offset: inst for inst in instructions}\n            # map old hook targets to new targets generated by the hook\n            old_hook_target_remap = {}\n            null_idxes_i = 0\n            stack_ctx_vars_d = dict(stack_ctx_vars)  # type: ignore[var-annotated,arg-type]\n            for i in range(nstack):\n                while (\n                    null_idxes_i < len(null_idxes)\n                    and null_idxes[null_idxes_i] == i + null_idxes_i\n                ):\n                    prefix.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n                    null_idxes_i += 1\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=f\"___stack{i}\"))\n                if i in hooks:\n                    hook = hooks.pop(i)\n                    hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\n                    prefix.extend(hook_insts)\n                    if is_py311_plus:\n                        hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\n                        if hook_target_offset in offset_to_inst:\n                            old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n                            meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\n                            old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\n                if i in stack_ctx_vars_d:\n                    # NOTE: we assume that current stack var is a context manager CLASS!\n                    # Load args for context variable and construct it\n                    prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(stack_ctx_vars_d[i]))\n\n            if is_py311_plus:\n                # reverse the mapping since targets of later/nested contexts are inserted\n                # into the mapping later, but show up earlier in the prefix.\n                meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = list(\n                    reversed(meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap)\n                )\n\n            assert not hooks\n\n            # NOTE: we assume that local var is a context manager CLASS!\n            # initialize inactive context vars in argnames\n            for name, vals in argnames_ctx_vars:\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=name))\n                prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(vals))\n                prefix.append(create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=name))\n\n            # 3.12+: store NULL into variables that were NULL\n            if argnames_null:\n                assert sys.version_info >= (3, 12)\n                for v in argnames_null:\n                    assert v not in args\n                    prefix.extend(\n                        [\n                            create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"),\n                            create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=v),\n                        ]\n                    )\n\n            # Call nested resume function\n            if nested_code_objs:\n                prefix.extend(\n                    [\n                        # set up __nested_resume_fns[-1] call\n                        *add_push_null(\n                            [\n                                create_instruction(\n                                    \"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"\n                                ),\n                                create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                                create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n                            ]\n                        ),\n                        # del __nested_resume_fns[-1]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                        create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n                        # load [__nested_resume_fns, __nested_frame_values]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"BUILD_LIST\", arg=2),\n                        # load __nested_frame_values[-1]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                        create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n                        # create [\n                        #     __nested_resume_fns,\n                        #     __nested_frame_values,\n                        #     *__nested_frame_values[-1],\n                        # ]\n                        create_instruction(\"LIST_EXTEND\", arg=1),\n                        # del __nested_frame_values[-1]\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n                        create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n                        # delete __nested values\n                        create_instruction(\"DELETE_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n                        create_instruction(\n                            \"DELETE_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"\n                        ),\n                        # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks\n                        # in the nested resume\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=False),\n                        create_instruction(\n                            \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n                        ),\n                        # finish the call\n                        create_instruction(\"CALL_FUNCTION_EX\", arg=0),\n                    ]\n                )\n            else:\n                # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks after the jump\n                prefix.extend(\n                    [\n                        create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=False),\n                        create_instruction(\n                            \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n                        ),\n                    ]\n                )\n\n            prefix.append(create_jump_absolute(target))\n\n            # because the line number table monotonically increases from co_firstlineno\n            # remove starts_line for any instructions before the graph break instruction\n            # this will ensure the instructions after the break have the correct line numbers\n            for inst in instructions:\n                if inst.offset == target.offset:\n                    break\n                inst.starts_line = None\n                if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n                    inst.positions = None\n\n            if cleanup:\n                prefix.extend(cleanup)\n                prefix.extend(cls.unreachable_codes(code_options))\n\n            # remap original instructions' exception table entries\n            if old_hook_target_remap:\n                assert is_py311_plus\n                for inst in instructions:\n                    if (\n                        inst.exn_tab_entry\n                        and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\n                    ):\n                        inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                            inst.exn_tab_entry.target\n                        ]\n\n            # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\n            instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\n\n        new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\n        ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\n        return new_code\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\n        \"\"\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\"\"\"\n        return [\n            create_load_const(None),\n            create_instruction(\"RAISE_VARARGS\", arg=1),\n        ]\n\n    @classmethod\n    def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n        cls,\n        code: types.CodeType,\n        lineno: int,\n        offset: int,\n        setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\n        *args: Any,\n    ) -> types.CodeType:\n        \"\"\"\n        This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\n        to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\n        from the original code object so that if control flow paths\n        converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\n        resume functions).\n        \"\"\"\n\n        meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\n            code\n        ]\n        new_offset = -1\n\n        def find_new_offset(\n            instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n        ) -> None:\n            nonlocal new_offset\n            (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n            # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\n            (new_target,) = (\n                i2\n                for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\n                if i1 is target\n            )\n            assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\n            assert new_target.offset is not None\n            new_offset = new_target.offset\n\n        transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\n        assert new_offset >= 0\n\n        if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n            # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\n            # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\n            # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\n            # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\n            if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\n                block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\n                    new_offset\n                ] = {}\n\n                def remap_block_offsets(\n                    instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n                ) -> None:\n                    # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\n                    # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\n                    # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\n                    # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\n                    # replaced.\n                    prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\n                    for inst in instructions:\n                        if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\n                            meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n                        ):\n                            break\n                        if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\":\n                            prefix_blocks.append(inst)\n\n                    # offsets into prefix\n                    for inst, o in zip(\n                        prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n                    ):\n                        block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\n\n                    # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\n                    old_start_offset = (\n                        cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\n                    )\n                    # offsets into old bytecode\n                    old_inst_offsets = sorted(\n                        n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\n                    )\n                    targets = _filter_iter(\n                        instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\n                    )\n                    new_targets = _filter_iter(\n                        zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\n                        targets,\n                        lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\n                    )\n                    for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\n                        block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\n\n                transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\n\n            # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\n            setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\n                meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\n                for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\n            )\n        return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\n            meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\n        )\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [

106. Bash @ -
Input
{

Before: old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n   - After: if hook_target_offset in offset_to_inst:\n               old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n   - Purpose: Prevent KeyError when hook_target_offset doesn't exist in nested context managers with graph breaks on Python 3.11+\n   \n   Change 2 (Line 670): Add fallback for missing offsets\n   - 1. EDIT /workspace/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py
Before
                if i in hooks:
                    hook = hooks.pop(i)
                    hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)
                    prefix.extend(hook_insts)
                    if is_py311_plus:
                        hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)
